Pickup version: "You haven't thrown my photo in the river yet — guess we're still karmically tied."

items.sourceNote: 「划龍舟先學打鼓」英文 dragon boat training jokes 格式；改寫為原創新手第一次划龍舟情境

zh-tw.content:
新手第一次划龍舟。

船長：「划槳的口令是聽鼓聲。」

新手：「好。」

船長：「咚——划。咚——划。」

新手照做。

船長：「咚咚咚——加速。」

新手加速。

船長：「咚咚咚咚咚咚——」

新手：「⋯⋯這是要我們飛起來嗎？」

船長：「不是。我手滑了。」

新手：「⋯⋯」

船長：「但你已經划了 30 公尺，恭喜你打破練習紀錄。」

新手：「所以下次你想讓我們快一點⋯⋯」

船長：「我就繼續手滑。」

新手：「⋯⋯船長，這就是傳說中的『心機鼓』嗎？」

船長：「叫做愛的鼓勵。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 新手龍舟梗加上「心機鼓 vs. 愛的鼓勵」雙關——適合傳給公司端午划龍舟團隊賽的同事，或是健身教練群組。船長最後那句「愛的鼓勵」一語雙關，既是鼓聲也是鼓勵，意外溫暖
variations: 短版：「船長：咚咚咚咚咚。新手：要飛了？船長：我手滑。」
辦公室版：「老闆：手滑加薪。員工：⋯⋯請繼續手滑。」

en.content:
First-time paddler on a dragon boat.

Captain: "You paddle to the drum."

Rookie: "Got it."

Captain: "Boom — paddle. Boom — paddle."

Rookie follows.

Captain: "Boom-boom-boom — speed up."

Rookie speeds up.

Captain: "Boom-boom-boom-boom-boom-boom-boom—"

Rookie: "...Are we supposed to take off?"

Captain: "No. My hand slipped."

Rookie: "..."

Captain: "But you just paddled thirty meters. Congrats, new practice record."

Rookie: "So next time you want us to go faster..."

Captain: "I'll just slip again."

Rookie: "...Captain, is this what they call strategic drumming?"

Captain: "I call it cheerleading."
en.editorNote: Rookie dragon boat scene built on the captain's 'slipped hand' becoming the team's secret weapon. Works for office team-building chats during Dragon Boat season — the 'cheerleading' callback is surprisingly warm
variations: Short version: "Captain: boom-boom-boom-boom. Rookie: are we flying? Captain: my hand slipped."
Office version: "Boss: oops, accidental raise. Employee: ...please keep slipping."

items.sourceNote: 龍舟划錯方向經典梗 + 現代 Google Maps 導航延伸；改寫為原創龍舟賽船長乘客對話

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽進行到一半。

隊長突然喊：「停！我們是不是划反了？」

隊員：「不會啊，鼓聲很清楚。」

隊長：「那為什麼別的隊都從我們旁邊往反方向過去？」

隊員：「⋯⋯也許他們才是錯的。」

隊長拿出手機打開 Google Maps。

螢幕顯示：「請於 30 公尺後迴轉。」

隊員：「你是不是把目的地設成永和豆漿？」

隊長：「⋯⋯比賽完不是要慶功嗎？」

全船沉默五秒。

然後一致大喊：「先吃豆漿再說！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代科技碰上傳統龍舟的反差梗——「先吃豆漿再說」這個轉折適合傳到公司端午活動群組，重點是團隊明明搞砸了卻一致決定先去吃宵夜，那種台灣人的隨性最討喜
variations: 短版：「為什麼別人都往反方向？我們是不是划錯了？⋯⋯也許他們才是錯的。」
辦公室版：「整個團隊方向錯，但同時決定先吃飯——我們果然是 team。」

en.content:
Halfway through the dragon boat race.

Captain: "Wait. Are we going the wrong way?"

Paddler: "No way, the drum is loud and clear."

Captain: "Then why is every other team passing us in the opposite direction?"

Paddler: "...Maybe they're the ones who are wrong."

Captain pulls out his phone and opens Google Maps.

Screen: "Make a U-turn in 30 meters."

Paddler: "Did you set the destination to the noodle shop?"

Captain: "...Aren't we celebrating after the race?"

Five seconds of silence.

Then, in unison: "Noodles first!"
en.editorNote: Modern-tech-meets-tradition gag — the team realizing they're losing the race but unanimously deciding food matters more is the warmest punchline. Send to office team-building chats during festival season
variations: Short version: "Why is every other team going the other way? ...Maybe they're wrong."
Office version: "Whole team picked the wrong direction, then voted to eat first. That's real teamwork."

items.sourceNote: 端午午時立蛋傳統習俗 + AI 物理模擬延伸；改寫為原創工程師父子對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午十二點，爸爸開始認真立蛋。

兒子：「爸，這個用 AI 算重心會比較準。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯」

兒子打開手機 App，掃描蛋的形狀，計算重心位置，標記出最佳支點。

爸爸照著標記點立。

蛋倒了。

兒子：「再試一次。」

蛋又倒了。

兒子：「⋯⋯這個 App 可能有 bug。」

爸爸什麼都沒說，把蛋往桌上一放，蛋立起來了。

兒子：「⋯⋯爸，你怎麼辦到的？」

爸爸：「我選了一顆桌面有凹槽的位置。」

兒子：「這算作弊吧？」

爸爸：「這叫經驗。AI 學不會的東西。」

兒子默默把 App 解除安裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午午時立蛋的傳統 vs. 現代科技反差——爸爸用「桌面凹槽」這種長輩智慧打敗 AI，最後一句「AI 學不會的東西」適合傳到家族群組，特別是家裡有理工科小孩跟長輩鬥嘴的場景
variations: 短版：「兒子用 AI 算重心，蛋倒了三次。爸爸把蛋放在桌面凹槽上，立起來了。」
職場版：「年輕人用工具，長輩用直覺。最後立起來的那個才算贏。」

en.content:
Noon, Dragon Boat Festival. Dad starts seriously trying to stand an egg upright.

Son: "Dad, AI can calculate the center of gravity for that."

Dad: "..."

Son opens an app, scans the egg, computes the optimal pivot point, marks it.

Dad places the egg on the marked spot.

Egg falls.

Son: "Try again."

Egg falls again.

Son: "...the app might have a bug."

Dad silently puts the egg down on a different spot. It stands.

Son: "...Dad, how?"

Dad: "I picked a spot where the table has a tiny dent."

Son: "Isn't that cheating?"

Dad: "It's called experience. AI can't learn that."

Son quietly uninstalls the app.
en.editorNote: Standing an egg upright at noon on Dragon Boat Festival is a real Taiwanese tradition. Plays the AI-vs-elder-wisdom angle for laughs — works great in family chats with engineering kids who lose to dad's lifehacks
variations: Short version: "Son used AI to find the egg's center of gravity. Dad found a dent in the table. Guess who won."
Office version: "Young people use tools. Elders use intuition. Whichever stands up first wins."

items.sourceNote: 端午香包傳統習俗 + 現代香水/約會延伸雙關；改寫為原創姐妹對話

zh-tw.content:
妹妹端午節要去約會。

姐姐：「妳今天身上是什麼味道？」

妹妹：「香水啊。」

姐姐：「不是，是另一個味道。」

妹妹：「⋯⋯喔，阿嬤硬塞給我的香包，說端午節要戴，可以驅蟲避邪。」

姐姐：「妳要去約會還戴香包？」

妹妹：「阿嬤說戴了會交到好對象。」

姐姐：「也對啦，能接受妳身上有艾草味的男生，應該真的不錯。」

妹妹：「⋯⋯」

姐姐：「這就叫篩選機制。」

妹妹默默把香包塞進包包最深處。

結果一打開包包還是有味道。

妹妹：「⋯⋯算了，命中註定。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午香包碰上現代約會的反差梗——「能接受妳身上有艾草味的男生應該真的不錯」這句姐姐金句，適合傳給單身姐妹淘群組。最後「命中註定」收尾既無奈又溫暖
variations: 短版：「戴香包約會＝最強篩選機制，能接受艾草味的對象一定是真愛。」
閨蜜版：「阿嬤的香包就是戀愛照妖鏡，留下來的才是真心。」

en.content:
Little sister's heading out on a date — Dragon Boat Festival night.

Big sis: "What's that smell on you?"

Little sis: "Perfume."

Big sis: "No, the other smell."

Little sis: "...Oh. Grandma forced this scented sachet on me. Said I have to wear it during the festival to ward off evil."

Big sis: "You're wearing an herbal pouch on a date?"

Little sis: "Grandma said it'll help me find a good partner."

Big sis: "Honestly, fair. Any guy who can handle you smelling like mugwort is probably a keeper."

Little sis: "..."

Big sis: "It's called a filter."

Little sis stuffs the sachet into the deepest corner of her bag.

Opens the bag. Still smells.

Little sis: "...Fine. Destiny it is."
en.editorNote: Festival sachet meets modern dating — the 'any guy who can handle the mugwort smell is a keeper' line is the punchline. Send to single-friends group chats; the 'destiny it is' ending lands warm
variations: Short version: "Wearing a herbal sachet on a date = the ultimate filter. Survivors are real ones."
Bestie version: "Grandma's sachet is a love detector. Whoever stays is the real deal."

items.sourceNote: 端午雄黃酒 + 白蛇傳許仙白娘子典故 + 現代婚姻黑色幽默延伸；改寫為原創夫妻對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節晚餐，老公倒了一杯雄黃酒給老婆。

老婆：「⋯⋯這什麼意思？」

老公：「傳統啊，端午喝雄黃酒辟邪。」

老婆瞇起眼睛。

老婆：「你不會是想學許仙吧？」

老公：「⋯⋯什麼意思？」

老婆：「許仙就是端午節給白娘子喝雄黃酒，結果發現她是蛇。」

老公：「我哪有這個意思。」

老婆：「那你倒這杯做什麼？」

老公沉默了三秒。

老公：「⋯⋯只是想確認一下。」

老婆把酒推回去。

老婆：「我建議你不要確認。」

老公：「為什麼？」

老婆：「因為確認了之後，你可能會比許仙更慘。」

老公默默把酒倒掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午雄黃酒源自白蛇傳典故，這版是老婆神反擊——「比許仙更慘」這句留白超有力，適合傳給已婚朋友群組，懂的都懂。已婚男性看完都會默默點頭
variations: 短版：「你倒雄黃酒給我喝？想學許仙？我建議你不要確認。」
甜蜜版：「我不需要喝雄黃酒，因為我已經被你迷住了。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival dinner. Husband pours his wife a glass of realgar wine.

Wife: "...What's this for?"

Husband: "Tradition. Realgar wine wards off evil during the festival."

Wife narrows her eyes.

Wife: "You're not trying to pull a Xu Xian on me, are you?"

Husband: "...What do you mean?"

Wife: "Xu Xian gave his wife realgar wine on Dragon Boat Festival. Turns out she was a snake."

Husband: "That's not what I meant."

Wife: "Then why pour it?"

Three seconds of silence.

Husband: "...Just wanted to confirm something."

Wife pushes the glass back.

Wife: "I'd recommend not confirming."

Husband: "Why?"

Wife: "Because if you confirm, things will end worse for you than they did for Xu Xian."

Husband quietly pours the wine down the sink.
en.editorNote: Realgar wine on Dragon Boat Festival comes from the Legend of the White Snake — Xu Xian served it to his wife and discovered she was a snake. The wife's counter-threat lands hard for married-friends group chats. Husbands will silently nod
variations: Short version: "Pouring me realgar wine? Trying to pull a Xu Xian? I'd recommend not confirming."
Sweet version: "I don't need realgar wine — I'm already enchanted by you."

items.sourceNote: 端午粽子熱量梗 + 鹼粽/南部粽/北部粽口味比較 + 健身減肥延伸；改寫為原創健身房對話

zh-tw.content:
健身教練：「端午節到了，提醒大家粽子熱量很高。」

學員 A：「一顆多少？」

教練：「南部粽大約 600 大卡，北部粽 500 大卡。」

學員 B：「那鹼粽呢？」

教練：「鹼粽 200 大卡。」

學員 B：「太好了，我吃鹼粽。」

教練：「⋯⋯但你要沾糖粉。」

學員 B：「⋯⋯加多少？」

教練：「看你想沾多少。」

學員 B：「⋯⋯沾很多。」

教練：「那大概 700 大卡。」

學員 B：「⋯⋯」

學員 A：「教練，那我們有什麼選擇？」

教練：「不要吃。」

全班沉默。

學員 C 緩緩舉手。

學員 C：「教練，我有更好的方案。」

教練：「請說。」

學員 C：「明天再運動。」

全班鼓掌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節限定的健身房黑色幽默——「明天再運動」這個全民共識梗適合傳到健身群組或減肥日記群。重點是教練被學員 C 用「拖延戰術」反殺，掌聲響起的畫面感很有戲
variations: 短版：「教練：粽子熱量超高。學員：那我們怎麼辦？教練：不要吃。學員：⋯⋯明天再運動。」
辦公室版：「主管：這個 deadline 很急。員工：那我們怎麼辦？員工 B：明天再做。」

en.content:
Trainer: "Dragon Boat Festival reminder — zongzi calories are no joke."

Student A: "How much per piece?"

Trainer: "Southern-style around 600. Northern-style around 500."

Student B: "What about alkaline zongzi?"

Trainer: "200 calories."

Student B: "Great, I'll eat that."

Trainer: "...But you dip it in sugar."

Student B: "...How much sugar?"

Trainer: "As much as you want."

Student B: "...A lot."

Trainer: "Then about 700 calories."

Student B: "..."

Student A: "Coach, what are our options?"

Trainer: "Don't eat them."

The whole class goes silent.

Student C slowly raises a hand.

Student C: "Coach, I have a better plan."

Trainer: "Go ahead."

Student C: "I'll work out tomorrow."

The whole class applauds.
en.editorNote: Festival-specific gym humor — the universal 'I'll work out tomorrow' consensus gets the round of applause. Send to fitness chats or diet-journal groups. Student C's procrastination move is the real MVP
variations: Short version: "Coach: zongzi has 700 calories. Class: ...we'll work out tomorrow."
Office version: "Boss: this deadline is urgent. Team: ...we'll start tomorrow."

items.sourceNote: 田馥甄「玉琴顧粽」（欲擒故縱）諧音梗格式；改寫為原創職場場景對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節連假前夕，全公司只剩玉琴還在加班。

同事好奇問：「為什麼端午節大家都放假，只有妳要加班？」

玉琴頭也不抬：「因為我叫玉琴顧粽。」

同事：「⋯⋯？」

玉琴：「欲擒故縱啊。」

同事：「所以妳是故意留下來讓老闆覺得妳很重要？」

玉琴終於抬起頭，笑得很神秘：「你終於懂了。」

隔週玉琴升職了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「玉琴顧粽」=「欲擒故縱」——天后級諧音梗改寫成職場黑色幽默，適合傳到上班族群組或加班同事 LINE，重點在最後一句「升職了」的反差神展開
variations: 短版：「同事：為什麼妳要加班？玉琴：因為我叫玉琴顧粽，欲擒故縱。」
戀愛版：「他遲遲不回我訊息，原來他叫玉琴顧粽，在跟我欲擒故縱。」

en.content:
The night before the Dragon Boat Festival long weekend, only Yuqin is still at her desk.

A coworker asks: "Why is everyone off but you?"

Yuqin, without looking up: "Because my name means 'play hard to get.'"

Coworker: "...What?"

Yuqin: "It's a strategy."

Coworker: "So you're staying late on purpose to make the boss think you're indispensable?"

Yuqin finally looks up with a knowing smile: "Now you get it."

She got promoted the following week.
en.editorNote: Workplace dark humor inspired by a popular Mandarin pun (Yuqin's name sounds like 'play hard to get'). Send to office group chats — the promotion punchline lands the workplace-strategy joke perfectly
variations: Short version: "Coworker: 'Why are you working late?' Yuqin: 'It's called playing hard to get. The boss is the target.'"
Dating version: "He won't text me back — turns out he's just playing hard to get. Or he's busy. Probably the second one."

items.sourceNote: drag-gone boat / leaky boat 系列英文諧音梗；改寫為原創龍舟比賽對話

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽開始前，新手隊員問教練：「為什麼這艘船一直在漏水？」

教練：「因為它是龍舟啊。」

新手：「⋯⋯這跟漏水有什麼關係？」

教練：「『龍』『漏』不分。」

新手沉默了三秒。

新手：「教練，我可以下船嗎？」

教練：「不行，你已經上了賊船。」

新手：「⋯⋯不是龍舟嗎？」

教練：「現在是賊船了。」

全隊大笑，比賽開始，他們最後一名。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「龍漏不分」是台語諧音梗（龍/漏發音相近），適合配上龍舟比賽照片或運動社團，重點在「賊船」的神來一筆——划龍舟的人看了會懂
variations: 短版：「為什麼龍舟一直漏水？因為龍漏不分。」
辦公室版：「為什麼這個專案一直出包？因為它是『漏』隊。」

en.content:
Before the race, a rookie asks the coach: "Why does this boat keep leaking?"

Coach: "Because it's a dragon boat."

Rookie: "...What does that have to do with leaking?"

Coach: "It's a drag-gone boat. The water's gone too."

Rookie: "..."

Rookie: "Coach, can I get off?"

Coach: "No. You're already in too deep."

Rookie: "...Literally or metaphorically?"

Coach: "Yes."

The whole team cracks up. The race starts. They finish last.
en.editorNote: Plays on the classic 'drag-gone boat' pun plus 'in too deep.' Works as a caption for dragon boat race photos or amateur sports team chats. The 'they finish last' kicker seals it
variations: Short version: "Why's the dragon boat leaking? Because it's a drag-gone boat."
Office version: "Why is this project sinking? Because we're all in too deep. Literally and metaphorically."

items.sourceNote: 「屈原給三天假」+「南北粽差異/划船救援」網路梗改寫；原創歷史對話

zh-tw.content:
歷史課上，老師問：「同學們，端午節是為了紀念誰？」

全班齊聲：「屈原！」

老師：「很好，那我們該如何感謝他？」

學生 A：「吃粽子。」

學生 B：「划龍舟。」

學生 C：「放三天假。」

老師：「⋯⋯最後一個是重點對吧。」

學生 C：「老師，您想想看，馬克思留給我們一堆讀不完的書，屈原留給我們三天假——您比較感謝誰？」

老師沉默了。

下課鐘響，全班鼓掌歡送屈原。
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫自網路經典梗「屈原給三天假」對比馬克思的對照——適合傳到學生群組或上班族放假前的群組，重點在老師的沉默和「鼓掌歡送屈原」的畫面感
variations: 短版：「為什麼我們感謝屈原？因為他留給我們三天假。」
上班族版：「同事們，這個禮拜要感謝誰？感謝屈原，他給了我們連假。」

en.content:
History class. Teacher asks: "Who do we commemorate on Dragon Boat Festival?"

The class, in unison: "Qu Yuan!"

Teacher: "Great. So how do we thank him?"

Student A: "Eat zongzi."

Student B: "Race dragon boats."

Student C: "Take three days off."

Teacher: "...That last one is the real answer, isn't it."

Student C: "Teacher, think about it. Karl Marx gave us textbooks we'll never finish reading. Qu Yuan gave us a three-day weekend. Who would YOU thank more?"

The teacher goes silent.

The bell rings. The whole class applauds in Qu Yuan's honor.
en.editorNote: Built on the viral Chinese-internet comparison between Marx (endless reading) and Qu Yuan (three days off). Works in student chats or office groups the Friday before the long weekend — the applause kicker makes it feel like a sitcom scene
variations: Short version: "Why do we thank Qu Yuan? Because he gave us a three-day weekend."
Office version: "Who do we owe this long weekend to? Qu Yuan. Truly the GOAT of holiday architects."

items.sourceNote: 南部粽 vs 北部粽 經典爭論梗；改寫為原創家庭群組對話

zh-tw.content:
家族群組裡，姑姑傳了一張粽子照片。

姑姑：「我自己包的北部粽，糯米先炒過，超香！」

三秒後，阿姨回應。

阿姨：「那個叫油飯包粽葉。真正的粽子是水煮的南部粽。」

姑姑：「南部粽根本是粽葉版的稀飯。」

阿姨：「北部粽根本是裹著葉子的便當。」

群組陷入南北戰爭。

這時，住國外的表哥傳了一句話。

表哥：「我這邊只買得到鹼粽。」

全家瞬間停戰，集體傳訊：「快回來，給你包真的粽子。」

南北恩怨在親情面前不堪一擊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節每年都會上演的南北粽戰爭——適合傳到家族群組炒熱氣氛，重點在「住國外的表哥」一句話讓全家停戰的反轉，溫馨中帶笑點
variations: 短版：「南部粽是粽葉版稀飯，北部粽是裹著葉子的便當——但只要有人在國外吃不到，大家就會停戰。」
辦公室版：「咖啡黨 vs 茶黨吵了一整天，直到實習生說『我只喝白開水』，全辦公室瞬間和好。」

en.content:
In the family group chat, Auntie A posts a zongzi photo.

Auntie A: "Northern-style, made it myself. Stir-fried the rice first — so fragrant!"

Three seconds later, Auntie B replies.

Auntie B: "That's not zongzi. That's oil rice wearing a leaf. Real zongzi is southern-style — boiled."

Auntie A: "Southern zongzi is basically congee in a leaf."

Auntie B: "Northern zongzi is a lunchbox with a costume."

The chat descends into civil war.

Then the cousin who moved abroad chimes in.

Cousin: "Over here I can only buy the alkaline kind."

The entire family stops fighting instantly: "Come home. We'll make you real ones."

North-vs-south rivalry dies the second family is involved.
en.editorNote: The annual northern-vs-southern zongzi war is a Taiwanese family group chat tradition. The 'cousin abroad' twist resets everyone to wholesome mode — perfect for sending into your own family chat to either spark or defuse the debate
variations: Short version: "Northern zongzi vs southern zongzi: nobody wins until someone says they can't get either, and suddenly everyone's a peacemaker."
Office version: "Coffee vs tea debate raged all day until the intern said 'I only drink water,' and the whole office reconciled out of pity."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 2026 行事曆「請 4 休 9」攻略討論；原創上班族對話

zh-tw.content:
同事 A 興奮地拿著行事曆衝進辦公室。

A：「我研究出來了，端午節請 4 天可以休 9 天！」

同事 B 抬起頭。

B：「請 4 休 9，聽起來像數學題。」

A：「不對，這是人生智慧。」

B：「那為什麼你看起來這麼累？」

A：「因為我要把這 9 天的工作量塞進請假前的 4 天。」

B 沉默了。

A：「⋯⋯所以這 9 天我會在家躺平，假裝那 4 天從來沒發生過。」

B：「結論：請 4 休 9 等於爆肝 4 天加躺平 9 天。」

A：「沒錯，但連假是真的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 端午連假請 4 休 9 的真實寫照——適合傳到辦公室群組或請假申請被卡的時候，重點在「爆肝 4 天加躺平 9 天」的精準總結，每個上班族都懂
variations: 短版：「請 4 休 9 的真相：爆肝 4 天，躺屍 9 天，回到公司繼續爆肝。」
主管視角：「員工請 4 休 9，主管心 4 休 0——因為要幫忙 cover 那 4 天的工作。」

en.content:
Coworker A bursts into the office waving a calendar.

A: "I figured it out — take 4 days off and you get a 9-day weekend!"

Coworker B looks up.

B: "Take 4 get 9. Sounds like a math problem."

A: "No, it's life wisdom."

B: "Then why do you look exhausted?"

A: "Because I have to cram 9 days of work into the 4 days before I leave."

B goes silent.

A: "...And then I'll spend the 9 days lying on the couch pretending those 4 days never happened."

B: "So 'take 4 get 9' equals burnout for 4 days plus corpse mode for 9 days."

A: "Correct. But the long weekend is real."
en.editorNote: Captures the universal pre-vacation hustle — works for any culture where you have to do double the work just to disappear for a week. Drop this in the office Slack the Monday before a long weekend
variations: Short version: "PTO math: 4 days of overtime + 9 days of comatose lying-down = vacation."
Manager version: "Your team takes 4 days off, you take 0 — because you're covering their 4 days."

items.sourceNote: 屈原跳江主題的諧音梗；原創職場版本對話

zh-tw.content:
下午五點五十九分，辦公室的氣氛很微妙。

小李盯著時鐘，雙手已經放在電腦關機鍵上。

五點五十九分五十九秒。

他按下關機，背起包包，一個健步衝向電梯。

主管從會議室出來：「小李，你跑這麼快幹嘛？」

小李回頭：「主管，今天端午節前一天，我要去汨羅江。」

主管：「⋯⋯你要去跳江？」

小李：「不是，我要去等屈原，提醒他下次跳早一點，我們才能早點放假。」

主管沉默了三秒。

主管：「我跟你一起去。」

兩人攜手衝出公司，留下整層樓的同事面面相覷。

屈原這個梗，跨越兩千年仍在拯救上班族。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原投江包裝成上班族的精神信仰——適合連假前一天傳到辦公室群組，重點在主管最後加入逃跑行列的反轉，所有打工人都會心一笑
variations: 短版：「謝謝屈原跳得早，讓我們今天可以早點下班。」
純對話版：「你要去跳江嗎？」「不，我要去拜託屈原下次跳早一點。」

en.content:
5:59 PM. The office air is tense.

Li stares at the clock, hands hovering over the shutdown button.

5:59:59.

He clicks. Grabs his bag. Sprints toward the elevator.

His manager emerges from a meeting room: "Li, where's the fire?"

Li turns: "Boss, it's the day before Dragon Boat Festival. I'm going to the Miluo River."

Manager: "...You're going to jump in?"

Li: "No. I'm going to wait for Qu Yuan and tell him to jump earlier next time, so we get off work sooner."

Three seconds of silence.

Manager: "I'm coming with you."

The two of them bolt out of the office together, leaving the entire floor staring at each other in disbelief.

Qu Yuan's legacy: still saving office workers two thousand years later.
en.editorNote: Reframes the Qu Yuan legend as an office-worker patron saint — the boss joining in is the kicker. Send this in your team chat the afternoon before a long weekend, regardless of country
variations: Short version: "Thank you Qu Yuan for jumping early. We get off work because of you."
Pure dialogue: "You jumping in the river?" "No. I'm asking Qu Yuan to jump earlier next time."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 out-of-office auto-reply 梗；原創職場逃避主題對話

zh-tw.content:
客戶寄信過來：「請問訂單什麼時候可以出貨？」

收到自動回覆。

「您好，本人正在休假，預計 6 月 22 日回到辦公室。
緊急事項請聯絡同事張先生。」

客戶轉寄給張先生。

張先生的自動回覆。

「您好，本人正在休假，預計 6 月 22 日回到辦公室。
緊急事項請聯絡同事王小姐。」

客戶轉寄給王小姐。

王小姐的自動回覆。

「您好，本人正在休假，預計 6 月 22 日回到辦公室。
緊急事項請寄信給原本聯絡的同事。」

客戶望著螢幕，意識到自己困在連假的無限迴圈裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假最寫實的客服體驗——自動回覆指向另一個自動回覆，最後回到原點。適合傳給做業務或客服的朋友，看了會心一笑也會嘆氣
variations: 短版：「連假版自動回覆：請聯絡 A → A 的回覆：請聯絡 B → B 的回覆：請聯絡 A。」
客戶吶喊版：「我只想問訂單什麼時候到，不是要參加密室逃脫。」

en.content:
A client emails: "When will my order ship?"

Auto-reply arrives.

"Hello, I'm on vacation and will return June 22.
For urgent matters, please contact my colleague David."

The client forwards to David.

David's auto-reply.

"Hello, I'm on vacation and will return June 22.
For urgent matters, please contact my colleague Sarah."

The client forwards to Sarah.

Sarah's auto-reply.

"Hello, I'm on vacation and will return June 22.
For urgent matters, please contact the person who originally emailed you."

The client stares at the screen, realizing they're trapped in a long-weekend infinite loop.
en.editorNote: The most accurate depiction of customer support during a holiday week — every auto-reply points to another auto-reply until you loop back to the start. Send this to anyone in sales, support, or account management
variations: Short version: "Holiday OOO chain: Contact A → A says contact B → B says contact A. You are now in escape-room mode."
Client meltdown: "I just wanted a shipping update. I didn't sign up for a mystery campaign."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「連假變假休息真加班」討論；原創家庭對話

zh-tw.content:
連假第一天早上九點，我躺在床上。

媽媽走進房間：「起床了，今天連假要好好利用。」

我：「我在利用啊，我在利用床。」

媽媽：「⋯⋯我列了一份清單。」

她拿出一張 A4 紙。

「上午：陪我去市場買粽子的料。
下午：陪我包粽子。
傍晚：陪我送粽子給三個阿姨。
晚上：陪我看龍舟轉播。
隔天：重複一次，因為要包給三舅。」

我看著清單。

我：「媽，這是連假還是行軍？」

媽媽：「這是孝順。」

我：「我可以選擇上班嗎？」

媽媽：「不行，公司放假。」

我：「那我可以選擇逃跑嗎？」

媽媽：「⋯⋯試試看，後果自負。」

我默默把行李箱推回衣櫃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午連假最殘酷的真相：上班是工作，回家是工作的升級版——適合傳給已婚或還住家裡的朋友，重點在「連假還是行軍」的精準形容和媽媽不可挑戰的權威
variations: 短版：「連假在公司叫休息，連假在家叫包粽軍訓營。」
爸爸版：「我以為連假可以休息，結果媽媽派我去搬十袋糯米。」

en.content:
First day of the long weekend. 9 AM. I'm in bed.

Mom walks in: "Get up. We need to make good use of this holiday."

Me: "I am making good use of it. I'm using the bed."

Mom: "...I made a list."

She produces a full sheet of paper.

"Morning: come with me to the market for zongzi ingredients.
Afternoon: help me wrap zongzi.
Evening: deliver zongzi to three aunties.
Night: watch the dragon boat broadcast with me.
Tomorrow: repeat, because we still need a batch for Uncle Three."

I stare at the list.

Me: "Mom, is this a long weekend or a military deployment?"

Mom: "This is being a good child."

Me: "Can I choose to go to work instead?"

Mom: "No. The office is closed."

Me: "Can I choose to escape?"

Mom: "...Try it. See what happens."

I quietly push my suitcase back into the closet.
en.editorNote: The brutal truth of Asian-family long weekends: the office is rest, home is an upgrade of the office. Send this to friends still living with parents or married into family-heavy holiday traditions — the closing 'suitcase back in the closet' line lands every time
variations: Short version: "At the office, a long weekend is rest. At Mom's house, a long weekend is dumpling boot camp."
Dad version: "I thought I'd relax this holiday. Then Mom assigned me to carry ten bags of glutinous rice."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自南北龍舟比賽差異的網路梗；原創觀眾對話

zh-tw.content:
電視轉播北部某地的龍舟比賽。

畫面中，三艘龍舟同時翻船，選手們在水裡掙扎。

爸爸：「他們在幹嘛？」

我：「比賽。」

爸爸：「比什麼？比誰先沉下去嗎？」

我：「⋯⋯應該是。」

切換到南部的轉播。

畫面整齊劃一，鼓聲節奏一致，龍舟像箭一樣往前衝。

爸爸：「這才像比賽。」

我：「網友說，北部龍舟是去陪屈原的，南部龍舟才是去救屈原的。」

爸爸沉默了三秒。

爸爸：「那屈原到底要不要被救？」

我：「看是誰先到吧。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯這個答案太現實了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北龍舟風格的經典對比梗，把翻船率包裝成「陪屈原」vs「救屈原」——適合在端午節龍舟轉播時傳到家族群組，重點在最後「看誰先到」的職場式回答
variations: 短版：「北部龍舟去陪屈原，南部龍舟去救屈原，結論：屈原很忙。」
辦公室版：「我們團隊像北部龍舟——出發很整齊，中間集體翻船，最後留在水裡互相安慰。」

en.content:
The TV is showing a dragon boat race from up north.

Three boats capsize simultaneously. Crews are flailing in the water.

Dad: "What are they doing?"

Me: "Racing."

Dad: "Racing what? Who sinks first?"

Me: "...Seems like it."

The broadcast cuts to a southern race.

Perfect synchronization. Drums in lockstep. The boats fly forward like arrows.

Dad: "Now THAT'S a race."

Me: "People online say northern dragon boats are there to keep Qu Yuan company. Southern ones are actually trying to rescue him."

Dad pauses for three seconds.

Dad: "So... does Qu Yuan want to be rescued or not?"

Me: "Depends who gets there first."

Dad: "...That's a depressingly corporate answer."
en.editorNote: The classic north-vs-south dragon boat meme, reframing the capsize rate as 'keeping Qu Yuan company' vs 'rescuing Qu Yuan.' Great to drop in a family chat during the festival broadcast — the closing 'whoever arrives first' line is pure office-worker fatalism
variations: Short version: "Northern dragon boats keep Qu Yuan company. Southern ones rescue him. Either way, Qu Yuan has a busy weekend."
Office version: "Our team is a northern dragon boat — perfect launch, mid-race capsize, then we comfort each other in the water."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路流傳的「馬克思 vs 屈原」對比梗；原創學生對話版本

zh-tw.content:
歷史課上，老師問：「對中華文化最有貢獻的人是誰？」

班上沉默。

小明舉手：「屈原。」

老師：「為什麼？」

小明：「因為他幫我們爭取到三天連假。」

全班笑翻。

老師：「孔子呢？孟子呢？這些聖人不算嗎？」

小明：「老師，他們有放假嗎？」

老師沉默。

小明：「沒放假的聖人，學生記不住。」

老師：「⋯⋯下課，回家好好讀書。」

小明：「老師，明天端午節，要回家慶祝屈原。」

老師關上講義：「⋯⋯你贏了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原包裝成「放假之神」的經典梗，比直接背歷史好記一百倍——適合傳給還在準備期末考的學生朋友或當老師的人，重點在「沒放假的聖人記不住」這句太真實
variations: 短版：「對中華文化貢獻最大的是屈原，因為他讓我們有三天連假。」
上班族版：「公司最有貢獻的不是 CEO，是設立國定假日的人。」

en.content:
In history class, the teacher asks: "Who contributed most to Chinese culture?"

Silence.

Ming raises his hand: "Qu Yuan."

Teacher: "Why?"

Ming: "Because he got us a three-day weekend."

The class loses it.

Teacher: "What about Confucius? Mencius? Don't the sages count?"

Ming: "Teacher, did they give us a day off?"

The teacher goes silent.

Ming: "Sages without holidays don't stick in a student's memory."

Teacher: "...Class dismissed. Go home and study."

Ming: "Teacher, tomorrow is Dragon Boat Festival. I have to go home and celebrate Qu Yuan."

The teacher closes the textbook: "...You win."
en.editorNote: Reframes Qu Yuan as the patron saint of paid time off — way easier to remember than actual history dates. Send this to teachers, students cramming for finals, or anyone who has ever loved a long weekend more than philosophy
variations: Short version: "Greatest contributor to Chinese culture: Qu Yuan. He got us three days off."
Office version: "The most important person at this company isn't the CEO. It's whoever scheduled the holidays."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自南北粽甜鹹之爭網路梗圖；原創 LINE 群組對話

zh-tw.content:
公司 LINE 群組，端午節前一週。

小王：「請問大家粽子要訂甜的還是鹹的？」

三秒鐘後，500 則訊息。

北部派：「當然鹹的，糯米炒過才香。」

南部派：「鹹的不算粽子，蒸熟的才是真粽。」

客家派：「你們吵什麼，鹼粽沾糖才是王道。」

外省派：「湖州粽才是粽子的祖宗。」

養生派：「請問有低 GI 版本嗎？」

小王：「⋯⋯」

小王：「我只是想問訂幾顆。」

群組：「先解決甜鹹再說。」

小王關掉群組通知，默默訂了五種口味各十顆。

端午節到了，公司茶水間出現一場聯合國會議。
zh-tw.editorNote: 粽子界的甜鹹之爭比豆腐腦還激烈——適合傳到辦公室群組或家族群組，重點在最後「聯合國會議」的精準形容，每個負責訂便當的人都懂這種絕望
variations: 短版：「問粽子訂甜還是鹹，是辦公室版本的點燃世界大戰。」
家族群組版：「阿嬤說只有她包的才是真粽，其他都是異教徒。」

en.content:
Company LINE group. One week before Dragon Boat Festival.

Wang: "Hey everyone, should I order sweet or savory zongzi?"

Three seconds later: 500 messages.

Northern faction: "Savory obviously. Stir-fried glutinous rice is the only way."

Southern faction: "Stir-fried doesn't count. Steamed is the real zongzi."

Hakka faction: "You're all wrong. Alkaline zongzi dipped in sugar is the answer."

Mainlander faction: "Huzhou zongzi is the ancestor of all zongzi."

Wellness faction: "Is there a low-glycemic version?"

Wang: "..."

Wang: "I just wanted to know how many to order."

Group: "Settle the sweet-vs-savory war first."

Wang mutes the group and quietly orders ten of every flavor.

Festival day arrives. The office kitchen looks like a UN summit.
en.editorNote: The zongzi sweet-vs-savory war is more intense than any pizza-pineapple debate — relatable to anyone who's ever tried to take a group lunch order. The closing 'UN summit' image is the kill shot
variations: Short version: "Asking the office whether to order sweet or savory zongzi is how you start World War III."
Family group version: "Grandma says only her recipe is real zongzi. All others are heretics."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節立蛋傳說的網路討論；原創家庭場景對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午十二點整，全家圍在餐桌邊。

媽媽：「快快快，這個時間立蛋會成功，會帶來一整年好運。」

爸爸蹲在桌邊，手抖到不行。

弟弟手機計時器倒數：「十、九、八⋯⋯」

爸爸：「不要催！蛋會緊張！」

弟弟：「⋯⋯蛋會緊張？」

爸爸：「五、四、三⋯⋯」

蛋立起來了，整整一秒鐘。

然後倒下。

媽媽：「不算，沒撐到中午十二點零一分。」

爸爸：「那就是說我的好運只有一秒？」

媽媽：「沒錯。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯這跟我買的樂透中獎時間差不多。」

全家陷入沉思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節立蛋的真實寫照——人人都要試，立起來的瞬間比中樂透還激動——適合傳給有在玩這個民俗的家族長輩，重點在爸爸「我的好運只有一秒」的精準自嘲
variations: 短版：「端午立蛋立起來的那一秒，是我一整年最接近神蹟的時刻。」
辦公室版：「我午休立蛋成功了，下午就被主管叫去開會——這就是端午好運的真相。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival. Noon, sharp. The whole family is gathered around the dining table.

Mom: "Quick quick quick, if you stand an egg upright at noon today, you get good luck for a whole year."

Dad crouches at the table edge, hands shaking.

My brother starts a phone countdown: "Ten, nine, eight..."

Dad: "Stop counting! You're stressing the egg!"

Brother: "...The egg is stressed?"

Dad: "Five, four, three..."

The egg stands. For exactly one second.

Then falls.

Mom: "Doesn't count. Didn't make it past 12:01."

Dad: "So my good luck this year is one second long?"

Mom: "Correct."

Dad: "...That's about the same length as every lottery ticket I've ever bought."

The family falls into deep contemplation.
en.editorNote: Captures the absurdity of the noon egg-balancing tradition — everyone has tried it, the one-second success feels like winning the lottery, and the egg always falls. The dad's lottery self-burn is the closer
variations: Short version: "The one second my egg stood upright at noon was the closest I'll get to a miracle this year."
Office version: "I balanced an egg at lunchtime and immediately got pulled into a meeting. That's the truth about festival luck."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自父親節卡片祝福語中「老爸冷笑話」與「髮際線」題材；原創家庭場景對話

zh-tw.content:
父親節晚餐，全家圍著蛋糕。

我：「爸，你知道你最大的優點是什麼嗎？」

爸爸眼睛一亮：「孝順？英俊？幽默？」

我：「你的笑話冷到能延緩全球暖化。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯」

媽媽：「還有他的髮際線在勇敢地對抗地心引力。」

爸爸：「我以為今天是父親節，不是吐槽大會。」

弟弟：「爸，吐槽就是我們愛你的方式。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯那你們可以少愛我一點。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親節最真實的家庭互動——用吐槽包裝愛意，爸爸嘴上嫌棄心裡偷笑。適合配上全家福照片發限動，或傳到家族群組讓爸爸假裝生氣三秒鐘
variations: 短版：「爸，你的冷笑話是我們家最有效的空調。」
卡片版：「老爸，謝謝你的冷笑話讓我們家四季如冬，省下不少冷氣費。父親節快樂。」

en.content:
Father's Day dinner. Whole family around the cake.

Me: "Dad, you know what your greatest strength is?"

Dad's eyes light up: "Loyalty? Good looks? Sense of humor?"

Me: "Your jokes are cold enough to reverse global warming."

Dad: "..."

Mom: "Also his hairline is putting up a brave fight against gravity."

Dad: "I thought today was Father's Day, not a roast."

Little brother: "Dad, the roast is how we show love."

Dad: "...Could you love me a little less?"
en.editorNote: The most relatable Father's Day moment — affection delivered as gentle roasting, dad pretends to be annoyed but is secretly enjoying it. Pair with a family photo for an Instagram story, or send to the family group chat to wind dad up for exactly three seconds
variations: Short version: "Dad's cold jokes are the most efficient air conditioning in our house."
Card version: "Dad, thanks to your cold jokes our house stays in winter all year. Saved a fortune on AC. Happy Father's Day."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自英文 Father's Day puns 中 'nacho average dad' 與 'in queso' 食物諧音梗；原創對話版本

zh-tw.content:
父親節，女兒拿著一張手作卡片。

女兒：「爸，這張卡片給你。」

爸爸打開卡片，上面畫了一塊起司。

爸爸：「⋯⋯這是什麼？」

女兒：「以防你不知道——你是最棒的爸爸（cheese 起司 / jeez 厲害的諧音）。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯我有讀英文。」

女兒：「我知道，但你笑點很低，這種爛梗你會喜歡。」

爸爸把卡片貼到冰箱上：「對，我超喜歡。」

女兒：「我就說吧。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親節的甜蜜真相——爸爸明明知道梗很爛，但因為是孩子做的就變成全世界最珍貴的禮物。適合送給做手作卡片的小朋友家長，或當作親子互動的可愛文案
variations: 短版：「爸爸的冰箱貼著我五歲畫的卡片，二十年了沒換過。」
送禮版：「老爸，這個爛笑話是我能想到最配你的禮物。父親節快樂。」

en.content:
Father's Day. Daughter brings out a handmade card.

Daughter: "Dad, this is for you."

Dad opens it. There's a drawing of a piece of cheese.

Dad: "...What is this?"

Daughter: "In queso you didn't know — you're nacho average dad."

Dad: "...That's two food puns."

Daughter: "I know. Your humor bar is so low, I knew you'd love it."

Dad sticks the card on the fridge: "You're right. I love it."

Daughter: "Told you."
en.editorNote: The sweet truth of Father's Day — dad knows the joke is terrible, but because his kid made it, it becomes the most precious thing on the fridge. Great for parents who are about to receive a handmade card, or for cute parent-kid social posts
variations: Short version: "My dad's fridge still has the card I made when I was five. Twenty years and counting."
Gift version: "Dad, this terrible pun is the most on-brand gift I could give you. Happy Father's Day."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自香港屯門龍舟撞船「薩莉亞之役」網路經典梗；原創觀眾席對話版本

zh-tw.content:
端午節龍舟賽，岸邊全家圍觀。

阿志：「我們公司隊在第三道，看好他們！」

哨聲一響，第三道龍舟猛烈衝出。

三秒後，第三道撞上第四道。

第四道翻船。

第三道也翻船。

第二道為了閃避，撞到第一道。

四條船全部在水裡。

媽媽：「⋯⋯這是什麼比賽？」

阿志：「⋯⋯團體賽。」

爸爸：「⋯⋯團體下水賽？」

阿志：「公司今年強調團隊精神。」

媽媽：「⋯⋯確實很有精神。」

岸邊觀眾鼓掌。屈原在江底笑了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟賽從競技活動升級成「集體下水大會」的荒謬幽默——適合傳給有去看龍舟賽的朋友，或當作公司端午活動翻車後的安慰文案，重點在「屈原在江底笑了」的神來一筆
variations: 短版：「我們公司龍舟隊三秒翻船，主管說這叫凝聚向心力。」
感性版：「龍舟賽真正的意義不是贏，是讓全隊一起下水陪屈原聊聊天。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival race. Family watching from the shore.

Zhi: "Our company team is in lane three. Cheer for them!"

Whistle blows. Lane three launches hard.

Three seconds later, lane three slams into lane four.

Lane four capsizes.

Lane three capsizes too.

Lane two swerves to dodge and crashes into lane one.

All four boats are in the water.

Mom: "...What kind of race is this?"

Zhi: "...A team event."

Dad: "...A team-falls-in-the-water event?"

Zhi: "Our company emphasized teamwork this year."

Mom: "...They really committed to it."

The crowd applauds. Qu Yuan, at the bottom of the river, smiles.
en.editorNote: Turns a dragon boat race into a synchronized swimming event by accident — perfect for friends who've actually witnessed a race pileup, or as consolation for any company team that capsized at their festival event. The 'Qu Yuan smiles at the bottom of the river' closer is the kicker
variations: Short version: "Our company dragon boat capsized in three seconds. Manager called it 'team bonding.'"
Sentimental version: "The real meaning of a dragon boat race isn't winning. It's the whole team joining Qu Yuan for a chat underwater."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節連假與工作群組相關的社畜題材；原創 LINE 群組劇場

zh-tw.content:
端午連假第一天，早上八點。

主管在公司群組發了訊息：「大家連假愉快！順便提醒一下下週的進度。」

群組瞬間安靜。

阿明：「⋯⋯」

阿華：「⋯⋯」

小芳已讀，沒回。

主管：「怎麼都不說話？」

阿明：「報告主管，我們在包粽子。」

阿華：「報告主管，我在划龍舟。」

小芳：「報告主管，我在江邊，跟屈原聊聊人生。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

主管：「⋯⋯好好過節，下週再說。」

群組爆出愛心和拇指。

屈原表示：終於有人來找我了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 社畜的端午連假心聲——主管一句「順便」就毀掉整個連假心情，所有人集體用「我在陪屈原」當免戰金牌——適合傳給端午連假還被主管 cue 的朋友，重點在屈原從被紀念變成社畜避風港
variations: 短版：「主管連假發訊息提進度，我說我在陪屈原，他就閉嘴了。」
回覆版：「不好意思主管，我現在訊號不好——因為我在江底。」

en.content:
First day of the Dragon Boat long weekend. 8 a.m.

Boss messages the work group chat: "Happy holidays, everyone! Quick reminder about next week's deadlines."

The group goes dead silent.

Ming: "..."

Hua: "..."

Fang reads it. Doesn't reply.

Boss: "Why is no one talking?"

Ming: "Sorry boss, I'm wrapping zongzi."

Hua: "Sorry boss, I'm rowing a dragon boat."

Fang: "Sorry boss, I'm by the river having a heart-to-heart with Qu Yuan."

Boss: "..."

Boss: "...Enjoy the holiday. We'll talk next week."

The group explodes with heart and thumbs-up emojis.

Qu Yuan, somewhere down below: Finally, someone visits me.
en.editorNote: The wage-slave Dragon Boat anthem — boss says 'just a quick reminder' and ruins everyone's long weekend, until the whole team uses Qu Yuan as a collective shield. Send this to anyone whose boss has DMed them on a holiday. The twist is Qu Yuan becoming the patron saint of avoiding work
variations: Short version: "Boss messaged about deadlines during the holiday. I said I was with Qu Yuan. He stopped typing."
Reply version: "Sorry boss, bad signal — I'm currently at the bottom of the river."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節梗圖區的「立蛋」中午十二點傳說；原創家庭對話版本

zh-tw.content:
中午十一點五十八分。

媽媽在廚房大喊：「快快快！等下要立蛋！」

我衝過去：「為什麼要立？」

媽媽：「端午節中午十二點立蛋會帶來好運。」

我：「現在不是各種角度都可以立嗎？」

媽媽：「那不一樣，那是物理。今天立的是命理。」

蛋立起來了。

媽媽：「快拍照，發給你阿嬤。」

我：「然後呢？」

媽媽：「然後你今年就會考上、加薪、找到對象。」

我：「⋯⋯」

我：「媽，那如果蛋倒了？」

媽媽看著我，眼神認真。

「那是你站姿不對。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節立蛋是台灣家庭年度大事，媽媽永遠相信中午十二點的蛋有玄學能量——重點在最後媽媽把責任推給「你的站姿」，完美呈現亞洲媽媽邏輯：蛋立起來是好運，倒了是你的錯，適合傳給每年都要立蛋的家庭群組
variations: 短版：「媽，蛋倒了。」「那是你的問題，不是蛋的問題。」
升級版：「我立了五次都沒立起來。」「那你今年就是準備了，不是好運。」

en.content:
11:58 a.m. on Dragon Boat Festival.

Mom yells from the kitchen: "Quick, quick! We have to stand the egg up!"

I run over. "Why?"

Mom: "If you balance an egg at noon today, you get good luck for the whole year."

Me: "But you can balance an egg at any time. That's just physics."

Mom: "That's different. That's physics. This is destiny."

The egg stands.

Mom: "Take a photo. Send it to Grandma."

Me: "And then what?"

Mom: "Then this year you'll get into college, get a raise, and find a partner."

Me: "..."

Me: "Mom, what if the egg falls over?"

Mom looks at me, dead serious.

"Then you were standing wrong."
en.editorNote: Egg-balancing at noon is a sacred Dragon Boat Festival tradition in Taiwan. Mom is convinced the noon egg has mystical powers, and the punchline is the classic Asian-mom logic: egg stands = good luck, egg falls = your fault. Great for the family group chat that does this every year
variations: Short version: "Mom, the egg fell." "That's a you problem, not an egg problem."
Upgrade: "I tried five times." "Then this year you get effort, not luck."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節北部粽 vs 南部粽戰爭的網路經典題材；原創 LINE 群組劇場

zh-tw.content:
端午節前三天，公司群組。

阿明：「明天吃粽子，誰要團購？」

小芳：「南部粽，水煮的那種。」

阿華：「不行，要北部粽，蒸的才香。」

小芳：「水煮才入味。」

阿華：「蒸的才有米香。」

群組安靜了三十秒。

小琪：「我都吃廣式粽。」

阿明：「⋯⋯」

阿華：「⋯⋯」

小芳：「⋯⋯」

阿明：「請小琪離開這個群組。」

小琪：「為什麼？」

阿明：「你連戰爭都不參加，你不配。」

（小琪已被移出群組。）

屈原表示：兩千年了你們還在吵這個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節南北粽之爭是台灣的年度宗教戰爭，比政治還激烈——廣式粽是中立國，反而被兩派一起排擠，重點在屈原冷眼旁觀兩千年——適合傳給每年都要爆發南北戰的家族群組或同事，戰起來
variations: 短版：「我吃廣式粽。」「請離開群組。」
升級版：「我都買超商粽。」「⋯⋯滾。」

en.content:
Three days before Dragon Boat Festival. Office group chat.

Ming: "Anyone want to do a zongzi group order?"

Fang: "Southern-style. The boiled kind."

Hua: "No. Northern-style. Steamed is better."

Fang: "Boiled has more flavor."

Hua: "Steamed has more rice aroma."

The chat goes silent for thirty seconds.

Qi: "I just eat Cantonese-style."

Ming: "..."

Hua: "..."

Fang: "..."

Ming: "Please remove Qi from this chat."

Qi: "Why?"

Ming: "You refused to pick a side. You don't belong here."

(Qi has been removed from the group.)

Qu Yuan, somewhere down below: Two thousand years and you're still arguing about this.
en.editorNote: The northern-vs-southern zongzi war is Taiwan's annual religious conflict — more heated than politics. Cantonese-style is the neutral country that gets exiled by both sides, and Qu Yuan watches the same fight repeat every year. Send this to any family chat that erupts every Dragon Boat Festival
variations: Short version: "I eat Cantonese zongzi." "Please leave the group."
Upgrade: "I buy mine at 7-Eleven." "...Get out."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午祝福語中「粽橫職場」諧音梗；原創職場剧本

zh-tw.content:
端午節當天，主管在群組發祝福：

「祝大家端午節愉快，事業粽橫，財運粽是來。」

阿明：「謝謝主管！」

阿華：「主管端午節安康！」

小芳：「祝主管粽攬大權。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

主管：「小芳你這個諧音梗有點問題。」

小芳：「沒有啊，我祝您粽攬大權，總攬大權的意思。」

主管：「下次祝福語請改成『身體健康』就好。」

小芳：「好的主管，祝您粽身健康。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

（小芳已被加入考核名單。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節諧音梗放在職場最危險，「粽」字什麼都能套但搞不好就 GG——小芳一路用諧音梗到主管心累，最後被加入考核名單，重點在語言的力量超越節日——適合傳給愛玩諧音梗的同事，警告他們收斂一點
variations: 短版：「祝主管粽攬大權。」「你被開除了。」
升級版：「祝您粽身健康。」「滾。」

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival morning. Boss posts a greeting in the work chat:

"Happy Dragon Boat Festival, team! May your careers always rise and prosperity always come your way."

Ming: "Thank you, boss!"

Hua: "Happy holidays, boss!"

Fang: "Wishing you total control of the company, boss."

Boss: "..."

Boss: "Fang, that's a slightly weird thing to say."

Fang: "What? I'm wishing you executive power. That's a compliment."

Boss: "Next time, just write 'good health.'"

Fang: "Got it, boss. Wishing you total health."

Boss: "..."

(Fang has been added to the performance review list.)
en.editorNote: Holiday puns at work are landmines — Fang keeps pushing wordplay until the boss gives up and adds her to the review list. The joke is that language wins out over festive cheer. Send to that coworker who can't stop with the puns
variations: Short version: "Wishing you complete corporate control, boss." "You're fired."
Upgrade: "Wishing you total health." "Get out."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方端午節英文笑話 dragon boat 雙關；原創對話結構

zh-tw.content:
公司辦端午節龍舟團建。

主管：「大家準備好了嗎？這次比賽我們一定要贏！」

全隊：「好！」

主管：「記住，划船最重要的是『團隊默契』。」

比賽開始。

第一秒，槳全部打在一起。

第三秒，船開始原地打轉。

第七秒，阿明的槳飛出去。

第十秒，阿華掉下水。

第十二秒，船翻了。

所有人在水裡。

主管邊游邊喊：「我說的『默契』不是這個意思！」

小芳：「主管，我們確實很有默契。」

主管：「哪裡？」

小芳：「我們同時掉進水裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 公司龍舟團建災難現場，「默契」變成集體翻船的代名詞——主管原本想要團隊精神，結果得到的是大家同步落水的反諷，重點在小芳最後的補刀完美呈現職場黑色幽默——適合傳給被迫參加團建的同事
variations: 短版：「划船最重要的是默契。」三秒後全隊落水。
升級版：「我們很有默契——同時放棄。」

en.content:
Company dragon boat team-building event.

Boss: "Everyone ready? We're winning this thing!"

Team: "Yeah!"

Boss: "Remember, the secret to rowing is teamwork."

The race starts.

Second 1: All the paddles collide.

Second 3: The boat starts spinning in place.

Second 7: Ming's paddle flies out of his hands.

Second 10: Hua falls in.

Second 12: The boat capsizes.

Everyone is in the water.

Boss, treading water: "That's not what I meant by teamwork!"

Fang: "Boss, we are very coordinated, actually."

Boss: "How?"

Fang: "We all fell in at the same time."
en.editorNote: A corporate dragon boat team-building disaster. The boss wants team spirit and instead gets perfectly synchronized failure. Fang's deadpan callback at the end is peak workplace dark humor. Send to anyone trapped in a mandatory company event
variations: Short version: "Teamwork is everything!" Three seconds later, everyone is in the water.
Upgrade: "We're very coordinated — we all gave up at the same time."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節送禮場景與長輩反應的網路題材；原創親戚對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，回阿嬤家。

我：「阿嬤，這盒粽子送您。」

阿嬤：「哎喲幹嘛買這個，浪費錢。」

我：「沒有啦，孝敬您的。」

阿嬤：「下次不要買了。」

我：「好。」

（五分鐘後。）

阿嬤打電話給隔壁阿姨：「秀珍啊，我孫子送我一盒很貴的粽子耶！」

阿嬤：「對啊，台北買的，一顆要一百多塊！」

阿嬤：「我孫子最孝順了，每年都記得回來看我。」

阿嬤：「對對對，下次叫他多買一些。」

我在客廳，假裝沒聽到。

阿嬤掛電話走過來：「下次真的不用買了，浪費錢。」

我：「好，阿嬤。」

（我已經在想明年要買哪一家了。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 阿嬤的端午節雙面行為——當面說浪費錢，背後跟鄰居炫耀一整天，這是台灣阿嬤的標準操作系統，重點在最後孫子已經默默規劃明年要買哪一家，因為大家都懂這套劇本——適合傳給每年都被阿嬤「念」但其實阿嬤很開心的人
variations: 短版：「下次不要買了。」「好。」（隔天阿嬤跟全鄰居炫耀。）
升級版：「浪費錢。」（轉身打電話：「我孫子超孝順！」）

en.content:
Day before Dragon Boat Festival. Visiting Grandma.

Me: "Grandma, I brought you a box of zongzi."

Grandma: "Aiyo, why did you spend money on this. Waste of money."

Me: "It's nothing. Just for you."

Grandma: "Don't buy this again."

Me: "Okay."

(Five minutes later.)

Grandma calls the neighbor: "Hsiu-Chen! My grandson brought me a box of fancy zongzi!"

Grandma: "Yeah, from Taipei. Over a hundred bucks per dumpling!"

Grandma: "My grandson is the most filial one, comes home every year."

Grandma: "Right right right, I'll tell him to buy more next time."

Me, in the living room, pretending not to hear.

Grandma hangs up and walks back. "Really, don't buy this again. Waste of money."

Me: "Okay, Grandma."

(I'm already planning which shop to order from next year.)
en.editorNote: Grandma's classic two-faced holiday move — tells you it's a waste of money, then spends an hour bragging to the entire neighborhood. The grandson already knows the script and is planning next year's order. Send to anyone whose grandma 'scolds' them every holiday while secretly loving it
variations: Short version: "Don't buy this next time." "Okay." (Next day she tells the entire street.)
Upgrade: "Waste of money." (Turns around, dials phone: "My grandson is SO filial!")

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路流傳「屈原給我們留下假期」段子；原創課堂對話結構

zh-tw.content:
國文老師：「同學們，你們最喜歡哪一位古代詩人？」

小明舉手：「屈原。」

老師感動：「為什麼？是因為他的愛國情操，還是〈離騷〉的文學成就？」

小明：「都不是。」

老師：「那是什麼？」

小明：「因為李白、杜甫、蘇軾都留下一堆詩給我們背。」

老師：「⋯⋯」

小明：「只有屈原，給我們留下了三天連假。」

全班鼓掌。

老師默默把小明的名字寫在黑板上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 國文老師的浪漫遇上學生的現實主義，屈原在學生心中的地位永遠是「放假之神」而不是「愛國詩人」——重點在全班鼓掌的瞬間老師徹底放棄治療——適合傳給每年端午連假在 LINE 群組發「謝謝屈原」的朋友
variations: 短版：「我最喜歡屈原。」「為什麼？」「他沒留詩，只留假。」
升級版：「屈原是我的偶像。」「他寫了什麼？」「他寫了三天連假。」

en.content:
Literature teacher: "Class, who's your favorite ancient poet?"

Ming raises his hand. "Qu Yuan."

Teacher, moved: "Is it his patriotism? Or the literary brilliance of his poems?"

Ming: "Neither."

Teacher: "Then what?"

Ming: "Li Bai, Du Fu, Su Shi — they all left us piles of poems to memorize."

Teacher: "..."

Ming: "Only Qu Yuan left us a three-day holiday."

The entire class applauds.

The teacher silently writes Ming's name on the board.
en.editorNote: Literature class romance crashes into student pragmatism. To students, Qu Yuan will forever be the patron saint of long weekends, not patriotism. The applause moment is when the teacher gives up entirely. Send to anyone who texts 'thanks Qu Yuan' every Dragon Boat Festival
variations: Short version: "My favorite poet is Qu Yuan." "Why?" "No poems, just vacation days."
Upgrade: "Qu Yuan is my hero." "What did he write?" "He wrote us a three-day weekend."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 Dcard 端午節粽子綁繩相關笑話素材；原創便利店場景

zh-tw.content:
凌晨兩點，便利商店。

夜班店員：「先生，這個粽子要幫您加熱嗎？」

阿明：「好啊，謝謝。」

店員把粽子放進微波爐，按了三分鐘。

（叮。）

店員打開微波爐——

裡面是一團綠色的爆炸現場，米粒貼在門上，粽葉融化，繩子打結燒焦。

店員：「⋯⋯先生。」

阿明：「嗯？」

店員：「您沒拆繩子。」

阿明：「啊？粽子不是連繩子一起微波嗎？」

店員：「⋯⋯」

阿明：「我以為繩子是調味料。」

店員深呼吸：「您是第一個吃過粽子的人類嗎？」

阿明：「⋯⋯端午節快樂。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 便利商店凌晨微波粽子大爆炸現場，重點在「我以為繩子是調味料」這句話完美呈現現代人對傳統食物的零知識——夜班店員的靈魂拷問「您是第一個吃過粽子的人類嗎」是全劇高潮——適合傳給那種會問「鳳梨酥裡面有鳳梨嗎」的朋友
variations: 短版：「粽子要加熱嗎？」「好。」（爆炸）「您沒拆繩子。」
升級版：「我以為繩子是調味料。」「先生請離開。」

en.content:
Two A.M. at the convenience store.

Night clerk: "Sir, would you like me to heat this zongzi?"

Ming: "Yeah, thanks."

Clerk puts the zongzi in the microwave. Three minutes.

(Ding.)

Clerk opens the door —

It's a green explosion. Rice grains glued to the glass. Bamboo leaves melted. The string knotted, charred, and smoking.

Clerk: "...Sir."

Ming: "Yeah?"

Clerk: "You didn't untie the string."

Ming: "Huh? Don't you microwave the whole thing with the string?"

Clerk: "..."

Ming: "I thought the string was a seasoning."

Clerk takes a deep breath. "Sir, are you the first human to ever eat a zongzi?"

Ming: "...Happy Dragon Boat Festival."
en.editorNote: Late-night convenience store microwave catastrophe. The line 'I thought the string was a seasoning' perfectly captures modern food illiteracy. The clerk's soul-piercing 'are you the first human to ever eat one of these' is the peak of the bit. Send to that friend who asks if pineapple cake has pineapple in it
variations: Short version: "Want it heated?" "Sure." (Explosion.) "You didn't take the string off."
Upgrade: "I thought the string was a seasoning." "Sir, please leave."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自西方 dragon boat 雙關英文笑話；原創獨白結構

zh-tw.content:
我：「今年端午節我要少吃一點。」

我媽：「為什麼？」

我：「為了健康，控制熱量。」

我媽：「好啊，那這顆粽子你不吃囉？」

我：「⋯⋯一顆還好。」

（吃完。）

我媽：「那這顆呢？阿嬤特別做的。」

我：「⋯⋯阿嬤的不能拒絕。」

（吃完。）

我媽：「冰箱還有鹼粽，配蜂蜜。」

我：「⋯⋯蜂蜜有助消化。」

（吃完。）

我媽：「樓下王太太送了五顆。」

我：「⋯⋯人情很重要。」

（吃完。）

隔天我站上體重機，多了兩公斤。

我：「沒關係，端午節後再開始減肥。」

我媽：「中秋節快到了。」

我：「⋯⋯」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節減肥宣言的完整失敗流程，從「一顆還好」一路退到「中秋節快到了」，重點在每一個藉口都是台灣家庭真實會說的話：阿嬤、人情、消化——適合傳給每年都說要減肥但每年都失敗的朋友（也就是所有人）
variations: 短版：「今年端午我要少吃。」三小時後，吃了七顆。
升級版：「端午節後再減肥。」「中秋快到了。」「中秋後再減。」「過年快到了。」

en.content:
Me: "This Dragon Boat Festival, I'm eating less."

Mom: "Why?"

Me: "Health. Calorie control."

Mom: "Okay. So you don't want this zongzi?"

Me: "...One is fine."

(Eaten.)

Mom: "What about this one? Grandma made it special."

Me: "...Can't say no to Grandma."

(Eaten.)

Mom: "There's alkaline zongzi in the fridge. Pairs with honey."

Me: "...Honey helps digestion."

(Eaten.)

Mom: "Mrs. Wang downstairs sent over five more."

Me: "...Neighborly relations matter."

(Eaten.)

Next day, I step on the scale. Up four pounds.

Me: "It's fine. I'll start the diet after the festival."

Mom: "Mid-Autumn is coming."

Me: "..."
en.editorNote: The complete collapse of a Dragon Boat Festival diet pledge — from 'just one' down to 'Mid-Autumn is coming.' Every excuse is one a real family actually uses: grandma, neighbors, digestion. Send to anyone who promises to diet every year and fails every year (so, everyone)
variations: Short version: "Eating less this year." Three hours later: seven zongzi in.
Upgrade: "I'll diet after the festival." "Mid-Autumn's coming." "After that." "Lunar New Year's coming."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路南北粽口味之爭梗圖；原創家族 LINE 群組對話

zh-tw.content:
家族 LINE 群組，端午節前夕。

阿姨：「今年要訂粽子，大家要南部還北部？」

大舅（高雄）：「當然南部粽，水煮才正統。」

二舅（台北）：「北部粽油飯口感才香，南部粽根本是糯米湯。」

大舅：「北部粽就是油飯包葉子，沒靈魂。」

二舅：「南部粽軟爛得像嬰兒副食品。」

大舅：「⋯⋯」

二舅：「⋯⋯」

阿姨：「那⋯⋯各訂十顆？」

大舅：「不行，這是原則問題。」

二舅：「對，這是價值觀的衝突。」

表妹：「我吃湖州粽。」

群組沉默。

大舅：「⋯⋯她不是我們家的小孩。」

二舅：「⋯⋯改名換姓吧。」

阿嬤跳出來：「都吃我包的客家粄粽就好。」

全群組：「好。」

（阿嬤永遠是終結者。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣家族 LINE 群組端午節必上演的南北粽宗教戰爭，大舅和二舅口味之爭最後被表妹的湖州粽和阿嬤的客家粄粽一鍋端——重點在阿嬤一出聲全員秒投降，這就是阿嬤在家族中的政治地位——適合傳到自家群組引發新一輪戰爭
variations: 短版：「南部粽。」「北部粽。」「我吃湖州粽。」「⋯⋯你被開除家族。」
升級版：阿嬤一句「吃我包的」，全群組秒安靜。

en.content:
Family LINE group. Day before Dragon Boat Festival.

Auntie: "Time to order zongzi. Northern or southern style?"

Uncle One (Kaohsiung): "Southern, obviously. Boiled is the only authentic way."

Uncle Two (Taipei): "Northern zongzi has that proper oily-rice texture. Southern is basically rice soup."

Uncle One: "Northern zongzi is just sticky rice wrapped in a leaf. No soul."

Uncle Two: "Southern zongzi is mushy like baby food."

Uncle One: "..."

Uncle Two: "..."

Auntie: "How about... ten of each?"

Uncle One: "No. This is a matter of principle."

Uncle Two: "Yes. This is a clash of values."

Cousin: "I eat Huzhou-style."

Group chat: silence.

Uncle One: "...She's not one of us."

Uncle Two: "...Change her name."

Grandma enters: "Everyone's eating the Hakka ones I'm making."

Whole group: "Yes ma'am."

(Grandma is always the final boss.)
en.editorNote: The annual Taiwanese family group chat war between Northern and Southern zongzi factions, with the cousin's Huzhou-style answer triggering immediate excommunication. The grandma-finishing-move is the real punchline — she speaks, everyone surrenders. Send to your own family chat to start fresh hostilities
variations: Short version: "Southern." "Northern." "I eat Huzhou." "...You're disowned."
Upgrade: Grandma: "You're eating mine." The chat goes silent forever.

items.sourceNote: 2026 端午連假請4休9 攻略文中的辦公室現象；原創職場對話

zh-tw.content:
六月初的辦公室。

我：「主管，我想請 6/15 到 6/18 這四天假。」

主管：「為什麼？」

我：「家裡有事。」

主管：「⋯⋯你家是不是剛好放九天？」

我：「⋯⋯」

主管：「全公司都跟我說家裡有事，加起來這個月台灣會發生兩百件家庭緊急狀況。」

我：「主管，這叫請4休9，是國家級的數學優化。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

主管：「准了。」

我：「謝謝！」

主管：「但 6/22 上班你最好給我準時。」

我：「⋯⋯」

（那天我遲到了。理由是時差。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 年端午節 6/19-6/21 連假，只要請 6/15-18 就能連休 9 天，全台灣上班族都在排隊請假——重點在「國家級的數學優化」和「請完 9 天回來居然有時差」這種沒人信但大家都用的爛理由，適合傳給也在規劃請4休9的同事
variations: 短版：「請四天，休九天。」「⋯⋯這不是請假，是套利。」
升級版：請假理由欄寫「響應政府連假政策」。

en.content:
Early June, the office.

Me: "Boss, I'd like to take June 15 through 18 off."

Boss: "Why?"

Me: "Family stuff."

Boss: "...Does your family happen to be on vacation for exactly nine days?"

Me: "..."

Boss: "Everyone in this office is telling me 'family stuff.' That's two hundred family emergencies in Taiwan this month."

Me: "Boss, this is called Take-Four-Get-Nine. It's national-level math optimization."

Boss: "..."

Boss: "Approved."

Me: "Thanks!"

Boss: "But you better be on time on June 22."

Me: "..."

(I was late. I blamed jet lag.)
en.editorNote: In 2026 the Dragon Boat Festival falls June 19-21, and taking June 15-18 off chains you a 9-day break — every office worker in Taiwan is queuing for the same days. 'National-level math optimization' and the jet-lag-after-a-domestic-staycation excuse make this one to forward to the coworker who's also gaming the calendar
variations: Short version: "Take four, get nine." "...That's not vacation, that's arbitrage."
Upgrade: Reason on the leave form: "Supporting government holiday policy."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路流傳「屈原最佛心，留給我們三天連假」梗；原創上班族吐槽版

zh-tw.content:
國文老師：「同學們，端午節要感謝誰？」

小明：「感謝屈原。」

老師：「為什麼？」

小明：「因為他是史上最佛心的詩人。」

老師：「⋯⋯你的意思是？」

小明：「其他詩人留下一堆要背的詩，只有屈原留給我們三天連假。」

全班鼓掌。

老師：「⋯⋯」

老師：「下週要背〈離騷〉。」

小明：「⋯⋯」

小明：「老師，我收回剛剛說的話。屈原也留了詩。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原從愛國詩人翻譯成「放假之神」是現代學生的浪漫——重點在小明前後反差，剛剛還在感謝屈原，一聽到要背〈離騷〉立刻翻臉，這是每個被國文課摧殘過的台灣人共同記憶，適合傳到同學群組引發回憶殺
variations: 短版：「屈原是史上最佛心的詩人，因為他留給我們三天連假。」
升級版：「老師，李白能不能也跳一個江？」

en.content:
Chinese teacher: "Class, who do we thank on Dragon Boat Festival?"

Ming: "Qu Yuan."

Teacher: "Why?"

Ming: "Because he's the most generous poet in history."

Teacher: "...How so?"

Ming: "Other poets left behind piles of poems for us to memorize. Qu Yuan left us a three-day weekend."

The class applauds.

Teacher: "..."

Teacher: "You'll be memorizing 'Li Sao' next week."

Ming: "..."

Ming: "Teacher, I take it back. Qu Yuan left poems too."
en.editorNote: Translating Qu Yuan from patriotic poet into 'god of paid holidays' is the modern student's love language. The whiplash from grateful to backpedaling the second the teacher mentions memorization is universal — every Taiwanese person who survived Mandarin class will recognize it. Good for the school group chat
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan is history's kindest poet — he left us a three-day weekend."
Upgrade: "Teacher, can Li Bai also throw himself in a river?"

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路龍舟比賽梗圖；原創職場龍舟賽對照版

zh-tw.content:
公司年度團建：龍舟比賽。

主管：「這次比賽是訓練團隊默契！大家加油！」

比賽開始。

業務部：節奏整齊，鼓聲響亮，五分鐘衝過終點。

工程部：每個人划的速度不一樣，有人滑手機，有人在 debug，有人問為什麼船會晃。

行銷部：忙著拍照發 IG，船還沒動。

人資部：在岸上發問卷：「請問您對本次活動的滿意度⋯⋯」

會計部：在算每槳的成本效益。

總經理：在另一條船上。獨自一人。划得很快。

結果業務部第一名。

主管：「果然團隊合作是關鍵。」

工程師：「不，關鍵是其他部門完全沒在划。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把端午龍舟賽改成台灣公司部門對抗賽，重點在每個部門的刻板印象都被狙擊到位——工程師滑手機、行銷只拍照、人資發問卷、會計算成本、老闆獨划，這是每個上過班的人都會點頭的真實寫照，適合傳到公司群組（如果你不怕被認出來）
variations: 短版：「龍舟賽，業務第一，工程師還在 debug 船為什麼會晃。」
升級版：人資部沒進終點，他們在岸上分析數據。

en.content:
Annual company team-building: dragon boat race.

Manager: "This race is about building team chemistry! Let's go!"

Race begins.

Sales team: perfect rhythm, loud drums, across the finish in five minutes.

Engineering: everyone paddling at different speeds. Someone's on their phone. Someone's debugging. Someone's asking why the boat keeps wobbling.

Marketing: too busy filming Reels. Boat hasn't moved.

HR: still on the dock, handing out surveys. "On a scale of 1 to 5, how would you rate this event..."

Finance: calculating the ROI of each paddle stroke.

The CEO: in a separate boat. Alone. Paddling very fast.

Sales wins.

Manager: "See? Teamwork is the key."

Engineer: "No. The key is that no other department was actually rowing."
en.editorNote: Turning the Dragon Boat race into a department-by-department company satire — every stereotype lands: engineers debugging, marketing filming, HR collecting surveys, finance running cost analysis, CEO going solo. Every office worker will nod. Share to the company chat at your own risk
variations: Short version: "Sales won the dragon boat race. Engineering is still debugging why the boat wobbles."
Upgrade: HR never crossed the finish line. They're still analyzing the data.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路粽子外送/收禮梗；原創外送平台訂單情境

zh-tw.content:
端午節下午三點，我打開外送 app。

搜尋：「粽子」。

出現：四百二十三家店。

我：「⋯⋯怎麼選？」

滑到第一家：南部肉粽，限時 8 折。
滑到第二家：北部油飯粽，免運。
滑到第三家：素粽養生組，買十送二。
滑到第四家：冰粽，端午涼感新體驗。
滑到第五家：泡麵店推出粽子口味泡麵。

我：「⋯⋯」

媽媽傳訊息：「中午阿嬤剛寄了二十顆給你。」

下午四點，門鈴響。

是樓下王太太：「我自己包的，給你嚐嚐。」（十顆）

下午五點，公司同事 LINE：「主管發的端午福利到了喔，記得領。」（八顆）

下午六點，男友送來：「我媽特別交代要給你。」（十二顆）

冰箱滿了。

我打開外送 app，搜尋：「胃藥」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 現代台灣人端午節最大的問題不是吃不到粽子，是粽子從四面八方湧入——阿嬤、鄰居、公司、男友媽，加上外送平台上四百多家選擇，最後不是吃粽是吃胃藥，這是每年端午都要上演一次的台灣現象，適合傳給每年都被粽子淹沒的朋友
variations: 短版：「端午節最該下載的不是外送 app，是消化酵素。」
升級版：冰箱：「我塞不下了。」我：「再撐一下，還有公司福利沒到。」

en.content:
Three p.m. on Dragon Boat Festival. I open the food delivery app.

Search: "zongzi."

Results: 423 restaurants.

Me: "...How am I supposed to choose?"

First result: Southern pork zongzi, 20% off.
Second: Northern oily-rice zongzi, free delivery.
Third: Vegetarian zongzi wellness pack, buy 10 get 2.
Fourth: Iced zongzi, a cool new festival experience.
Fifth: A ramen shop launching zongzi-flavored instant noodles.

Me: "..."

Mom texts: "Grandma shipped you twenty zongzi at noon."

4 p.m. Doorbell.

Mrs. Wang from downstairs: "Homemade. Try them." (Ten.)

5 p.m. Office LINE: "Holiday bonus zongzi from the boss is here — come pick yours up." (Eight.)

6 p.m. Boyfriend arrives: "My mom specifically said these are for you." (Twelve.)

The fridge is full.

I reopen the delivery app. Search: "antacids."
en.editorNote: The real Dragon Boat Festival crisis in modern Taiwan isn't finding zongzi — it's surviving the flood. Grandma, neighbor, office, boyfriend's mom, plus 400+ delivery options. By sundown you're not eating dumplings, you're shopping for antacids. Send to anyone who gets buried under rice dumplings every June
variations: Short version: "The most necessary app for Dragon Boat Festival isn't food delivery — it's digestive enzymes."
Upgrade: Fridge: "I'm full." Me: "Hang on, the office bonus pack isn't here yet."

items.sourceNote: 改寫自 2026 超商創意粽推薦報導（榴槤粽、香菜粽）；原創家族群組情境

zh-tw.content:
家族群組。

表妹：「今年超商出了榴槤粽欸！」

大姑：「⋯⋯榴槤包進粽子裡？」

表妹：「還有香菜粽。」

二舅：「香菜⋯⋯包進粽子裡？」

表妹：「還有鹹蛋黃流心粽、麻辣鴨血粽、起司熱狗粽。」

阿嬤傳了一張照片進來。

是她剛包好的傳統南部粽。

阿嬤：「我這個是肉粽。」

阿嬤：「肉。粽。」

表妹：「⋯⋯」

二舅：「阿嬤這個我吃。」

大姑：「我也吃這個。」

表妹：「⋯⋯我也吃這個好了。」

阿嬤：「乖。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 年超商粽子越出越獵奇——榴槤、香菜、起司熱狗，但只要阿嬤端出傳統肉粽，整個家族瞬間集體投降。這是台灣端午節最真實的代際對話：再怎麼追潮流，最後還是回歸阿嬤的肉粽，適合傳到家族群組讓阿嬤偷笑
variations: 短版：「超商再怎麼創新，端午節最強的還是阿嬤的肉粽。」
升級版：表妹後來偷偷買了榴槤粽，沒人敢承認自己買了。

en.content:
Family group chat.

Cousin: "7-Eleven launched a durian zongzi this year!"

Aunt: "...Durian. Inside a zongzi."

Cousin: "There's also a cilantro one."

Uncle: "Cilantro. Inside a zongzi."

Cousin: "And salted-egg-yolk lava zongzi, mala duck blood zongzi, cheese hot dog zongzi."

Grandma sends a photo.

It's the traditional Southern pork zongzi she just wrapped.

Grandma: "Mine is pork."

Grandma: "Pork. Zongzi."

Cousin: "..."

Uncle: "I'll eat Grandma's."

Aunt: "Same."

Cousin: "...I'll have Grandma's too."

Grandma: "Good kids."
en.editorNote: Convenience stores in 2026 are getting wilder — durian zongzi, cilantro zongzi, cheese hot dog zongzi. But the second Grandma drops a photo of her traditional pork zongzi, the entire family folds. Peak Taiwanese generational dynamics. Send it to the family chat and watch Grandma smile
variations: Short version: "No matter how wild 7-Eleven gets, Grandma's pork zongzi always wins."
Upgrade: Cousin secretly bought the durian one. Nobody will admit they did.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自英文龍舟雙關笑話彙整（oar/sore/canoe-dle）；原創隊伍命名情境

zh-tw.content:
公司報名龍舟賽，要取隊名。

主管：「大家集思廣益，要響亮、要有氣勢。」

業務：「無敵戰艦號。」

會計：「成本控管號。」

人資：「永續發展號。」

工程師：「Segmentation Fault 號。」

主管：「⋯⋯」

主管：「不能取英文。」

工程師：「分段錯誤號。」

主管：「⋯⋯也不行。」

行銷：「乘風破浪追夢人號。」

主管：「太長。」

實習生小聲說：「⋯⋯划划號？」

主管：「⋯⋯為什麼？」

實習生：「因為我們只會划划而已。」

全場沉默。

主管：「就決定是這個了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 公司報名龍舟賽取隊名是端午節最容易翻車的職場情境——業務想耍帥、會計想省錢、工程師講技術哏、行銷寫文案、最後實習生一句自嘲反而成為共識。「划划號」一語雙關：划船+敷衍划水，台灣上班族秒懂，適合傳到公司群組
variations: 短版：「公司龍舟隊名最後決定叫『划划號』，因為我們只會划划而已。」
升級版：報名表交出去，對手隊看了笑場：『他們是來搞笑的吧？』結果我們第一名。

en.content:
The office is registering a dragon boat team. We need a name.

Manager: "Brainstorm. Make it bold. Make it powerful."

Sales: "Invincible Warship."

Finance: "Cost-Optimized Vessel."

HR: "Sustainable Future Boat."

Engineer: "Segmentation Fault."

Manager: "..."

Manager: "Make it less technical."

Engineer: "Null Pointer."

Manager: "...Still no."

Marketing: "Riders of the Wind, Chasers of the Dream."

Manager: "Too long."

Intern, quietly: "...Paddling Around?"

Manager: "...Why?"

Intern: "Because that's all we're going to do."

Silence.

Manager: "Sold."
en.editorNote: Naming the office dragon boat team is peak workplace chaos — sales going macho, finance penny-pinching, engineering throwing in tech jokes, marketing writing ad copy. Then the intern lands the kill shot with brutal self-awareness. 'Paddling Around' doubles as both rowing and slacking off — every office worker reading this gets it instantly
variations: Short version: "We named our dragon boat team 'Paddling Around' because that's all we're going to do."
Upgrade: The rival team laughed when they saw our name. We won the race.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路經典「米飯看包子表演」段子；原創便利商店熟食區場景

zh-tw.content:
便利商店熟食區。

白飯走進去，看見一群朋友。

包子：「嗨白飯，最近好嗎？」

白飯：「還行。」

白飯環顧四周，菜包、肉包、豆沙包、芝麻包都在。

白飯心想：「你們這群人怎麼穿得都一樣⋯⋯」

這時粽子走了進來。

白飯眼睛一亮：「欸！這位看起來不太一樣！」

粽子把粽葉一脫。

白飯：「⋯⋯」

白飯：「靠，原來是自己人。」

粽子：「沒辦法啊，端午節限定造型而已。」

白飯：「那你平常是？」

粽子：「滷肉飯。」

白飯激動：「我就說你看起來很眼熟！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 改寫經典「米飯遇到粽子是老鄉」段子，加入便利商店熟食區情境跟滷肉飯彩蛋——粽子本質就是糯米飯穿了粽葉，端午節後又回去當滷肉飯，台味十足，適合冷笑話愛好者
variations: 短版：「粽子脫掉粽葉後，白飯說：『靠，原來是自己人。』」
升級版：白飯：「那油飯呢？」粽子：「他是我哥。」

en.content:
Convenience store hot food section.

Rice walks in and sees a group of friends.

Bun: "Hey Rice, what's up?"

Rice: "Same old."

Rice looks around. Veggie buns, pork buns, red bean buns, sesame buns — all here.

Rice thinks: "Why are you guys all wearing the same outfit..."

Then Zongzi walks in.

Rice perks up: "Oh! This one looks different!"

Zongzi peels off the bamboo leaf.

Rice: "..."

Rice: "Damn. You're one of us."

Zongzi: "Can't help it. Holiday-limited costume."

Rice: "What do you do normally?"

Zongzi: "Braised pork rice."

Rice, excited: "I KNEW you looked familiar!"
en.editorNote: A modern take on the classic 'rice and zongzi are countrymen' joke, set in a 7-Eleven hot food aisle with a braised pork rice (lurou fan) easter egg. Zongzi is basically glutinous rice in a leaf disguise. Perfect for fans of corny food puns
variations: Short version: "Zongzi peeled off the leaf. Rice said: 'Damn. You're one of us.'"
Upgrade: Rice: 'What about Oily Rice?' Zongzi: 'That's my brother.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自端午節祝福語/粽子諧音成語梗；原創 LINE 群組情境

zh-tw.content:
端午節早上九點，LINE 群組大亂鬥。

阿明：「端午節快樂，祝大家粽是順利。」

小華：「粽橫四海。」

大雄：「粽情享受。」

美玲：「粽望所歸。」

阿傑：「粽生緣起。」

群組沉默五秒。

阿傑：「⋯⋯這個我自己想的，可以嗎？」

小華：「不行。太硬。」

美玲：「重來。」

阿傑：「⋯⋯粽⋯⋯粽⋯⋯」

阿明：「想不到就講『阿粽快樂』就好。」

阿傑：「阿粽快樂。」

群組已讀。

沒人回。

阿傑：「⋯⋯」

阿傑：「我下次先準備。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節 LINE 群組諧音梗大戰是現代台灣人最熟悉的儀式，重點在阿傑「粽生緣起」太硬被同儕打槍、最後勉強講「阿粽快樂」被已讀不回。「阿粽」=「阿中」（自我代稱），既諧音又有點冷，適合分享給每年都被諧音梗難倒的朋友
variations: 短版：「LINE 群組端午節諧音大戰，輸的人就是想不出新梗的那個。」
升級版：阿傑當天下午又傳了一句『粽以為傲』，群組終於回他：『晚了。』

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival, 9 a.m. The LINE group chat goes nuclear.

Ming: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival, may everything zong smoothly."

Hua: "Zong-quering the seas."

Xiong: "Zong-uine joy."

Meiling: "Zong-imously voted."

Jie: "Zong-nected by fate."

Five seconds of silence.

Jie: "...I made that one up. Does it count?"

Hua: "No. Too forced."

Meiling: "Try again."

Jie: "...Zong... Zong..."

Ming: "If you can't think of one, just say 'Happy Zong-day.'"

Jie: "Happy Zong-day."

Group chat: read.

No replies.

Jie: "..."

Jie: "Next year I'm prepping in advance."
en.editorNote: The LINE group pun war on Dragon Boat Festival morning is a sacred Taiwanese ritual. The dynamic of one person trying too hard, getting roasted, then defaulting to a lazy 'Happy Zong-day' that gets read but ignored is universally relatable. Share with the friend who always loses the pun chain
variations: Short version: "Dragon Boat LINE group pun war: whoever runs out of zong-puns first loses."
Upgrade: Jie tried again in the afternoon with 'Zong-fident in you.' Group finally replied: 'Too late.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路屈原高學歷貴族冷知識梗；原創歷史人物面試情境

zh-tw.content:
屈原穿越到 2026 年，去面試。

HR：「請簡單自我介紹。」

屈原：「我是楚國貴族，左徒兼三閭大夫，精通文學、外交、政治改革。」

HR：「⋯⋯學歷？」

屈原：「楚國最高學府，貴族出身，受過完整六藝教育。」

HR：「上一份工作離職原因？」

屈原：「⋯⋯被同事陷害，被老闆放逐。」

HR：「為什麼選擇我們公司？」

屈原：「因為你們的端午節福利有粽子。」

HR：「⋯⋯」

HR：「最後一個問題，你抗壓性如何？」

屈原沉默了很久。

屈原：「⋯⋯這題可以跳過嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 屈原其實是楚國高學歷貴族——左徒、三閭大夫，主管外交跟教育，被現代 HR 問起來瞬間翻車。重點在最後抗壓性那題，端午節由來就是他跳汨羅江，這個梗冷中帶酸、有點黑色幽默，適合分享給每年端午都在公司加班的朋友
variations: 短版：「HR 問屈原抗壓性如何，屈原說：『這題可以跳過嗎？』」
升級版：HR：『歡迎你下週一報到。』屈原：『⋯⋯我可以在家工作嗎？』

en.content:
Qu Yuan time-travels to 2026 and lands a job interview.

HR: "Please introduce yourself briefly."

Qu Yuan: "Aristocrat of the Kingdom of Chu, served as Zuotu and Sanlu Dafu, specialties include literature, diplomacy, and political reform."

HR: "...Education?"

Qu Yuan: "Top academy of Chu, classical six-arts curriculum, noble background."

HR: "Reason for leaving your last job?"

Qu Yuan: "...Framed by colleagues. Exiled by the king."

HR: "Why are you interested in our company?"

Qu Yuan: "Because your Dragon Boat Festival bonus includes zongzi."

HR: "..."

HR: "Last question — how do you handle pressure?"

Long silence.

Qu Yuan: "...Can I skip this one?"
en.editorNote: Most people don't know Qu Yuan was actually a high-ranking aristocrat with elite education — Zuotu (foreign minister) and Sanlu Dafu (head of royal education). Drop him into a modern HR interview and the whole 'origin of Dragon Boat Festival is he drowned himself' detail becomes very awkward. Dark-comedy energy. Share with the friend stuck working overtime through the holiday
variations: Short version: "HR asked Qu Yuan how he handles pressure. Qu Yuan: 'Can I skip this one?'"
Upgrade: HR: 'You start Monday.' Qu Yuan: '...Can I work from home?'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路經典「外籍員工誤吃粽葉」段子；原創跨國公司辦公室情境

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節發粽子，外籍同事 Mike 拿了一顆回座位。

隔天早上，Mike 走過來。

Mike：「謝謝公司的粽子，肉跟飯都很好吃。」

我：「不客氣。」

Mike：「不過外面那層生菜有點硬。」

我：「⋯⋯生菜？」

Mike：「綠色的那層，我嚼了很久才嚥下去。」

我：「⋯⋯Mike。」

我：「那是粽葉。」

Mike：「⋯⋯粽葉？」

我：「是用來包的，不是用來吃的。」

Mike 沉默了三秒。

Mike：「⋯⋯所以我昨天到底吃了什麼？」

我：「⋯⋯纖維質。」

Mike：「⋯⋯難怪今天早上⋯⋯」

我：「我不需要聽下一句。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 公司發粽子給外籍同事最容易出現的災難——粽葉是包裝不是食物，但沒人事先說，最後外籍同事連葉子一起嗑掉，當事人滿臉問號加上腸胃慘案，這是跨國公司端午節真實會發生的場景，適合傳給有外籍同事的辦公室群組
variations: 短版：「外籍同事吃粽子連粽葉一起吞，隔天說：『那層生菜有點硬。』」
升級版：從此公司每年發粽子都附一張說明書，標題：『先脫葉子，再吃。』

en.content:
The office hands out zongzi for Dragon Boat Festival. Mike, our American coworker, takes one back to his desk.

Next morning, Mike walks over.

Mike: "Thanks for the zongzi. The meat and rice were great."

Me: "Glad you liked it."

Mike: "Though the lettuce on the outside was a little tough."

Me: "...Lettuce?"

Mike: "The green layer. I had to chew it for a while."

Me: "...Mike."

Me: "That's a bamboo leaf."

Mike: "...A bamboo leaf?"

Me: "It's the wrapper. You don't eat it."

Three seconds of silence.

Mike: "...So what exactly did I eat yesterday?"

Me: "...Fiber."

Mike: "...That explains this morning."

Me: "I do not need to hear the next sentence."
en.editorNote: Classic Dragon Boat Festival office disaster — nobody warns the foreign coworker that the bamboo leaf is a wrapper, not food. Mike chews through the whole thing, regrets it, and then nearly overshares about the bathroom consequences. Universal cross-cultural workplace comedy. Send to any team with international hires
variations: Short version: "Mike ate the bamboo leaf too. Next day: 'The lettuce was kind of tough.'"
Upgrade: Now every year the office attaches an instruction card: 'Step 1: Remove the leaf.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路南北粽戰爭迷因；原創伴侶家庭聚餐情境

zh-tw.content:
第一次去男友家過端午節。

我：南部人。
男友：北部人。

男友媽端出油飯粽。

男友：「我們家是北部粽，先把米炒過再蒸。」

我笑著點頭。

心裡 OS：「這不就是飯糰用粽葉包嗎。」

男友媽：「妳家是哪一種？」

我：「⋯⋯南部粽。生米加配料下去煮。」

男友：「水煮的吧？」

我：「⋯⋯水煮的。」

男友：「沒味道吧？」

我抬頭，看著他。

男友媽輕輕咳了一聲。

男友：「⋯⋯我是說，清爽。」

男友：「清爽。」

男友：「我超愛清爽的。」

我：「⋯⋯」

男友媽：「兒子，這顆粽子是我幫她留的。」

男友：「⋯⋯好。」

我（內心）：「媽我可以叫妳媽嗎。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北粽戰爭是台灣端午節永恆主題，第一次去伴侶家過節更是地雷區。男友差點翻車，男友媽神救援——這種婆媳合作打兒子的場景在台灣家庭超常見，適合分享給有跨南北戀情經驗的朋友
variations: 短版：「男友說南部粽沒味道。男友媽說：『這顆是我幫她留的。』」
升級版：那天之後，男友每次回我家都自動說：『南部粽真的比較香。』

en.content:
First Dragon Boat Festival at my boyfriend's family.

Me: from the south.
Him: from the north.

His mom serves Northern-style zongzi.

Boyfriend: "Ours is Northern style. The rice is stir-fried first, then steamed."

I smile and nod.

Internal monologue: "So it's basically an oily rice ball in a leaf."

His mom: "What does your family make?"

Me: "...Southern style. Raw rice and fillings, boiled together."

Boyfriend: "Boiled, right?"

Me: "...Yes."

Boyfriend: "Kind of bland?"

I look up at him.

His mom clears her throat.

Boyfriend: "...I mean, light. Refreshing."

Boyfriend: "Refreshing."

Boyfriend: "I love refreshing."

Me: "..."

His mom: "Son. This one is the one I set aside for her."

Boyfriend: "...Okay."

Me, internally: "Mom, can I call you Mom."
en.editorNote: The North vs. South zongzi war is Taiwan's longest-running food feud, and meeting your partner's family during Dragon Boat Festival is a minefield. Boyfriend almost crashes the dinner — boyfriend's mom executes a flawless rescue. This mother-girlfriend alliance against the boyfriend is peak Taiwanese family dynamics. Share with anyone in a cross-regional relationship
variations: Short version: "Boyfriend called Southern zongzi bland. His mom said: 'This one is the one I set aside for her.'"
Upgrade: Ever since, every time he visits my family he announces: 'Southern zongzi is actually better.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自網路立蛋冷笑話彙整；原創端午正午直播情境

zh-tw.content:
端午節正午十二點，我開直播立蛋。

直播標題：「正午陽氣最強，立蛋必成功！」

觀眾：12 人。

第一顆：滾走。
第二顆：倒了。
第三顆：滾走。
第四顆：碎了。

觀眾：35 人。

留言：「主播你是不是在唬人？」

我：「再給我一次機會！」

第五顆：站起來了！

我：「我成功啦——！」

下一秒。

我家的貓跳上桌子。

蛋：滾走、倒下、碎掉。

貓：（盯著我）。

我：「⋯⋯」

觀眾：187 人。

留言洗版：「貓貓才是主角」、「貓貓贏了」、「給貓貓加雞腿」。

我看著一桌蛋液跟貓。

我：「⋯⋯下播。」

貓：「喵。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋是台灣世代相傳的儀式，現代年輕人改成開直播挑戰，重點在好不容易成功了，貓貓一秒收場。直播觀眾從 12 人衝到 187 人，主角換成貓——這就是網路時代的端午節，適合所有養貓的人
variations: 短版：「端午立蛋直播終於成功，下一秒貓跳上桌。下播。」
升級版：那場直播後我漲粉三千，全部都是來看貓的。

en.content:
Dragon Boat Festival, noon. I'm livestreaming the egg-balancing tradition.

Stream title: "Peak yang energy at noon — guaranteed success!"

Viewers: 12.

Egg one: rolls off.
Egg two: tips over.
Egg three: rolls off.
Egg four: cracks.

Viewers: 35.

Chat: "Are you scamming us?"

Me: "One more try!"

Egg five: STANDS UP.

Me: "I DID IT —!"

Next second.

My cat jumps on the table.

Egg: rolls, tips, cracks.

Cat: (staring at me).

Me: "..."

Viewers: 187.

Chat floods: "The cat is the real main character." "Cat wins." "Give the cat a treat."

I look at the puddle of egg and the cat.

Me: "...Ending stream."

Cat: "Meow."
en.editorNote: Balancing eggs at noon is a generations-old Taiwanese Dragon Boat ritual. The modern upgrade: do it on livestream. The punchline of finally succeeding only to have your cat instantly end the dream is universally relatable for anyone who's tried to film anything with a pet in the room. Viewers jump from 12 to 187 — the cat is the star now
variations: Short version: "Egg finally stood up on livestream. Next second the cat jumped on the table. End of stream."
Upgrade: I gained 3,000 followers from that stream. All of them came for the cat."

items.sourceNote: 粽子熱量 health-guilt joke format; original dialogue and structure

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，我跟朋友信誓旦旦：「今年我只吃一顆粽子。」

朋友：「真的假的？你做得到？」

我：「我這個人說一就是一。」

隔天傍晚，朋友傳訊息來：「你今天吃幾顆？」

我：「一⋯⋯天吃六顆，一共兩天。」

朋友：「所以是十二顆。」

我：「數學是體育老師教的，別算了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 一顆粽子熱量大概等於一碗半的飯，這個梗精準打到每個端午節立志少吃卻越吃越多的人——適合傳到減肥同好群組互相取暖，或自嘲用
variations: 短版：「今年端午我只吃一顆粽子。一天一顆，連吃一個禮拜。」
健身版：「教練說一顆粽子要跑十公里才消得掉。我看著手上第四顆，決定明年再說。」

en.content:
The day before Dragon Boat Festival, I swore to my friend: "This year I'm only eating one zongzi."

Friend: "For real? You can do that?"

Me: "When I say one, I mean one."

The next evening my friend texts: "So how many did you eat today?"

Me: "One... a meal. Six a day. For two days."

Friend: "That's twelve."

Me: "My gym teacher taught me math. Let's not do this."
en.editorNote: One sticky-rice dumpling is roughly a meal and a half of calories. This one lands for anyone who vows to cut back every Dragon Boat Festival and somehow eats more. Great for sharing in a diet-buddy group chat
variations: Short version: "This year I'm only eating one zongzi. One per meal, all week long."
Fitness version: "My trainer said one zongzi takes a 10K run to burn off. I looked at my fourth one and decided to deal with it next year."

items.sourceNote: 龍舟划船 teamwork-at-work crossover concept; rewritten with original office scene

zh-tw.content:
公司辦端午龍舟團建，主管在岸邊喊：「同心協力！一起划！」

結果船上十個人，划的方向全都不一樣。

船在原地轉圈。

主管：「你們平常開會不是配合得很好嗎？」

後排同事小聲說：「開會我們都在裝睡，誰知道彼此要往哪划。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舟最講究節奏一致，這梗把職場「表面團結、實際各划各的」演得淋漓盡致——傳到部門群組，主管會心一笑（或心一沉）
variations: 短版：「龍舟團建最大的收穫，就是發現我們部門連划船都無法同步。」
升級版：「教練說要找一個鼓手定節奏，我們全船一致推派最會在會議上講幹話的那位。」

en.content:
The company held a Dragon Boat team-building race. The boss shouts from the dock: "Teamwork! Paddle together!"

Ten people in the boat. Ten different directions.

The boat just spins in place.

Boss: "Don't you all coordinate great in meetings?"

A guy in the back, quietly: "In meetings we're all pretending to be asleep. Nobody knows which way anyone's actually rowing."
en.editorNote: Dragon boat racing is all about rhythm and sync, which makes it the perfect mirror for a team that looks united on the surface but is secretly all paddling its own way. Drop it in the department chat
variations: Short version: "Biggest takeaway from the dragon boat outing: our team can't even row in sync."
Upgrade: "Coach said we needed a drummer to set the pace, so the whole boat nominated the guy who's best at talking nonsense in meetings."

items.sourceNote: 粽橫四海 (縱橫四海) homophone blessing pun; original framing

zh-tw.content:
端午節，老闆在群組發祝福：「祝大家粽橫四海，事業順利！」

小編立刻接：「老闆，那我們也祝您粽是賺錢！」

老闆：「會做人喔，這個月獎金有了。」

隔壁同事傳訊息問：「他真的會發嗎？」

小編：「會啦，粽會的，只是不知道哪一年。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽橫四海」=「縱橫四海」、「粽是」=「總是」、「粽會」=「總會」——一次塞三個諧音梗，適合端午節在公司群組求獎金（不保證有效）
variations: 短版：「端午節快樂，祝你粽橫四海、好運連連！」——安全版，傳給長官客戶都不出錯
情侶版：「祝我們粽是黏在一起，像粽葉裡的米一樣分不開。」

en.content:
On Dragon Boat Festival, the boss posts in the group chat: "Wishing everyone great success, may you conquer the seas!"

The intern fires back: "Boss, and may you zong-stantly make money!"

Boss: "Smooth talker. There's your bonus this month."

A coworker DMs: "Is he actually going to pay out?"

Intern: "He will. Zong-day. Just not sure which year."
en.editorNote: Stacks three zongzi puns where 'zong' (粽) plays on 'always/eventually' (總). Perfect for angling for a bonus in the work chat on the holiday — results not guaranteed
variations: Short version: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may you conquer the seas and have luck on your side!" Safe to send to a boss or client.
Couple version: "May we stay zong-stuck together, inseparable like rice in a bamboo leaf."

items.sourceNote: 屈原 modern reinterpretation joke; original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師問：「同學，屈原為什麼投江？」

小明舉手：「因為他沒有手機。」

老師：「⋯⋯這跟手機有什麼關係？」

小明：「如果他有手機，心情不好可以滑短影片、傳訊息給朋友、或是發一篇長文討拍，根本不用跳江。」

老師沉默三秒：「⋯⋯這題我給你滿分，但你放學留下來。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用現代年輕人的視角重新解讀屈原，笑點在於把投江換成「沒有手機可以紓壓」——適合在端午節傳到班級群組或同學會，懂的人都會笑
variations: 短版：「屈原最大的悲劇，是活在一個沒有 Wi-Fi 的時代。」
上班族版：「屈原如果是現代人，應該會選擇遞辭呈而不是投江——雖然結果可能差不多慘。」

en.content:
History teacher: "Class, why did the poet Qu Yuan throw himself into the river?"

Ming raises his hand: "Because he didn't have a phone."

Teacher: "...What does a phone have to do with it?"

Ming: "If he'd had one, he could've scrolled short videos, texted a friend, or posted a long sad caption fishing for sympathy. No need to jump."

Teacher, after three seconds: "...Full marks. But you're staying after class."
en.editorNote: Reframes the ancient Qu Yuan legend through a Gen-Z lens — the gag being that the tragedy was really a lack of modern coping tools. Lands in a class or alumni chat for anyone who knows the festival's origin
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan's real tragedy was living in an age with no Wi-Fi."
Office version: "If Qu Yuan were alive today, he'd hand in his resignation instead of jumping — though the outcome might be just as grim."

items.sourceNote: 家族群組 zongzi-delivery family humor; original scene

zh-tw.content:
媽媽傳訊息：「我包了粽子，你要幾顆？」

我：「五顆就好，謝謝媽。」

三天後，宅配送來一箱。

我打開：四十顆。

我打電話回去：「媽，我說五顆。」

媽：「五顆怎麼夠吃，我多包一點，分同事啦、冰起來啦、過節要有過節的樣子。」

我看著冰箱，已經塞不下了。

我：「⋯⋯那我同事這個月都吃粽子了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 媽媽的「我多包一點」永遠是天文數字，這個梗精準命中每個收過家裡寄來大量食物的遊子——適合傳到家族群組，媽媽看了會說「哪有那麼誇張」（但其實有）
variations: 短版：「跟媽說要五顆粽子，收到四十顆。這就是母愛的單位換算。」
升級版：「同事問我為什麼天天帶粽子，我說這是我媽的愛，吃不完的那種。」

en.content:
Mom texts: "I made zongzi. How many do you want?"

Me: "Five is plenty, thanks Mom."

Three days later, a box arrives by courier.

I open it: forty.

I call home: "Mom, I said five."

Mom: "Five isn't enough! I made extra — share with coworkers, freeze some, you have to celebrate properly."

I stare at my fridge. It won't close anymore.

Me: "...Guess my coworkers are eating zongzi all month."
en.editorNote: A mom's 'I made a little extra' is always an astronomical number. This nails every kid living away from home who's received a giant care package of food. Send it to the family chat — Mom will say 'it's not THAT much' (it is)
variations: Short version: "Told Mom I wanted five zongzi. Got forty. That's the conversion rate of a mother's love."
Upgrade: "Coworker asked why I bring zongzi every day. I said it's my mom's love — the kind you can't finish."

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone blessing pool (粽如泰山 etc.); rewritten as original group-chat exchange

zh-tw.content:
群組裡有人發了一串端午祝福：

「祝大家端午節粽如泰山、粽橫四海、粽身是膽！」

小美回：「最後一句怪怪的。」

發訊息的人：「打太快了，我是想說『一身是膽』。」

小美：「不，我是說你前面兩句也很怪。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 拿端午諧音祝福語自我吐槽的梗，「粽如泰山」=「重如泰山」、「粽橫四海」=「縱橫四海」——適合在家族或朋友群組裡，當有人認真發長串祝福時補一刀
variations: 短版：「端午快樂，祝你粽如泰山、心想事粽。（沒錯，每個字都硬要塞粽。）」

en.content:
Someone drops a string of festival blessings in the group chat:

"Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may you be zong-fident, zong-quering, and zong-stoppable!"

Mei: "That last one feels off."

The sender: "Typo. I meant unstoppable."

Mei: "No, I mean the first two were also off."
en.editorNote: Self-roasting take on the over-the-top zongzi-pun blessings people mass-send (zong stands in for every syllable). Drop it right after someone earnestly posts a long blessing chain in the group
variations: Short version: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may you be zong-fident and zong-tent. (Yes, I forced 'zong' into every word.)"

items.sourceNote: dragon boat 'oar-some / row-mantic' water-pun cluster; rewritten as original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節辦龍舟比賽，主管在岸上喊：「加油！划快一點！」

划到一半，阿凱突然停下來。

隊友：「你幹嘛停？」

阿凱：「我發現一件事——我們這艘船叫『業績號』，划得越快，公司賺越多，我加班還是沒加班費。」

全船沉默。然後一起把槳放下了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟比賽連結到上班族的職場無力感，笑點在於「划得越快越虧」的覺悟——適合在端午連假前傳到同事群組，引發共鳴式苦笑
variations: 短版：「龍舟比賽輸了，主管問為什麼。我說：因為我們划的是『加班號』，沒人想贏。」

en.content:
The company holds a dragon boat race for the festival. The manager yells from the bank: "Faster! Paddle harder!"

Halfway through, Kai stops rowing.

Teammate: "Why'd you stop?"

Kai: "Just realized something — our boat's named 'Quarterly Targets.' The faster we paddle, the more the company makes, and I still don't get paid overtime."

Dead silence. Then the whole crew set their oars down.
en.editorNote: Connects the dragon boat race to that universal employee burnout feeling — the gag is the dawning 'the faster I row, the more I lose.' Send it to the work chat right before the long festival weekend
variations: Short version: "We lost the dragon boat race. Manager asked why. I said: our boat was called 'Unpaid Overtime' — nobody wanted to win."

items.sourceNote: 甜粽 vs 鹹粽 north-south flame-war meme; original sketch

zh-tw.content:
第一次帶女友回家過端午。

我媽端出鹹粽，女友默默拿出自己帶的甜粽。

氣氛瞬間凝結。

我媽：「妹妹，粽子怎麼可以是甜的？」

女友：「阿姨，粽子怎麼可以是鹹的？」

兩人對看三秒。

我夾起一顆鹼粽，沾糖吃：「你們看，我兩邊都不得罪。」

結果兩個人一起瞪我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典甜鹹粽之爭加上「想兩邊討好結果兩邊都得罪」的下場——適合傳給那種愛在群組裡挑起甜鹹話題的朋友，或當你家剛好南北聯姻
variations: 短版：「端午節最危險的不是龍舟，是飯桌上同時出現甜粽和鹹粽。」

en.content:
First Dragon Boat Festival bringing my girlfriend home.

My mom serves savory zongzi. My girlfriend quietly pulls out the sweet ones she brought.

The air freezes instantly.

Mom: "Sweetie, how can a zongzi be sweet?"

Girlfriend: "Auntie, how can a zongzi be savory?"

They stare each other down for three seconds.

I pick up a plain alkaline zongzi, dip it in sugar: "See? I'm not taking sides."

They both glared at me.
en.editorNote: The classic sweet-vs-savory zongzi war, plus the fate of the guy who tries to please both sides and offends both. Perfect for that friend who loves starting the sweet-vs-savory debate in the group chat
variations: Short version: "The most dangerous thing at Dragon Boat Festival isn't the boats — it's sweet and savory zongzi appearing on the same table."

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone romance line (粽情/鍾情) pool; rewritten as original confession scene

zh-tw.content:
他在端午節鼓起勇氣告白：「我對你是粽情難捨、無法自拔。」

她：「你是想說鍾情吧？」

他：「對，但端午節我想加點儀式感。」

她：「那我也回你一句——」

他緊張地等著。

她：「我對你的感覺，就像鹼粽——沒沾糖之前，很難說有沒有味道。」

他：「……所以我要去買糖嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 節日限定告白橋段，「粽情難捨」=「鍾情難捨」，女生用鹼粽比喻曖昧的模糊狀態——適合傳給曖昧對象當開場，結局走向看對方接不接得住
variations: 短版：「端午節限定告白：我對你一見粽情，連口味都懶得問。」

en.content:
On Dragon Boat Festival he works up the nerve: "I'm hopelessly zong-smitten with you."

Her: "You mean smitten?"

Him: "Yeah, but it's the festival, I wanted a little flair."

Her: "Then let me answer you—"

He waits, nervous.

Her: "My feelings for you are like a plain alkaline zongzi — before you add sugar, it's hard to say if there's any flavor at all."

Him: "...So should I go buy sugar?"
en.editorNote: A festival-only confession bit — 'zong-smitten' puns on the Chinese for 'smitten,' and she answers with a deadpan plain-zongzi metaphor for being unsure. Send it to your crush as an opener; the ending depends on whether they can volley it back
variations: Short version: "Festival confession: I'm love-at-first-bite for you — didn't even ask the flavor."

items.sourceNote: 立蛋 noon-egg-balancing custom + diet humor; original scene

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，全家擠在桌邊試著立蛋。

爸：「聽說午時立得起來，整年好運。」

試了二十分鐘，全倒。

這時我把一顆粽子立在桌上，穩穩地。

我：「蛋立不起來，粽子可以，今年我改拜粽子。」

媽：「你那不是好運，是因為你吃太多粽子，連桌子都認得它了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 結合端午午時立蛋習俗和吃太多粽子的自嘲，笑點在媽媽的神補刀——適合在端午當天傳到家族群組，配一張立蛋失敗的照片更有效
variations: 短版：「端午立蛋失敗十次，立粽子一次就成功。這說明我跟粽子比較有緣。」

en.content:
Noon on Dragon Boat Festival, the whole family crowds the table trying to balance an egg upright.

Dad: "They say if it stands at noon, you get good luck all year."

Twenty minutes. Every egg topples.

So I stand a zongzi on the table instead. Rock solid.

Me: "Eggs won't stand, but zongzi will. This year I'm worshipping zongzi."
