Mom: "That's not luck — you've eaten so many zongzi even the table recognizes them now."
en.editorNote: Mixes the noon egg-balancing custom with self-deprecating 'I've eaten too many zongzi' humor; the punchline is Mom's counter-jab. Send it on the day with a photo of your failed egg attempts
variations: Short version: "Failed to balance an egg ten times, balanced a zongzi on the first try. Guess the zongzi and I just have a connection."

items.sourceNote: oar-some / row-mantic 划龍舟英文諧音梗；改寫為原創對話，加入中文龍舟版本

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節辦龍舟比賽，主管想振奮士氣。

主管：「大家加油，我們這隊一定划得最好！」

我：「為什麼這麼有信心？」

主管：「因為我們是『一條心』，划龍舟最怕的就是有人『另有所槳』。」

全隊安靜了三秒。

後排同事：「主管，我們昨天就已經有三個人請假了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「另有所槳」=「另有所謀」，把划槳和心思不齊的職場現實結合——適合公司端午活動前傳到群組暖場，主管本人在線時效果加倍
variations: 短版：「划龍舟團隊精神最重要，最怕的不是輸，是有人『另有所槳』。」

en.content:
The company holds a dragon boat race for the festival, and the manager wants to fire everyone up.

Manager: "Come on team, we're going to row the best out there!"

Me: "Why so confident?"

Manager: "Because we're all in the same boat — the worst thing in a dragon boat is someone with a different oar-genda."

Three seconds of silence.

Guy in the back: "Boss, three people already called in sick yesterday."
en.editorNote: 'Oar-genda' puns on 'agenda' — the gap between team-spirit cheerleading and people quietly bailing. Great to drop in the group chat before a company dragon boat outing, especially if the boss reads it
variations: Short version: "Team spirit matters most in a dragon boat. The real danger isn't losing — it's a teammate with their own oar-genda."

items.sourceNote: 屈原投江 / 楚辭 文學哏；原創歷史穿越情境改寫

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師問：「同學，端午節是為了紀念誰？」

小明：「屈原。」

老師：「很好，那你知道為什麼要包粽子嗎？」

小明：「為了不讓魚吃屈原。」

老師：「答對了。」

小明：「老師，那現在魚都不愛吃粽子了，是不是代表魚也開始注意身材？」

老師：「……是不是代表你又沒交作業？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把投江餵魚的典故，硬轉到現代減肥話題，再被老師神回——適合傳到家長群組或當作端午歷史小科普的開場，笑點在最後的突襲補刀
variations: 短版：「端午包粽子本來是給魚吃的，現在魚都嫌熱量太高，可見魚比我有自制力。」

en.content:
History teacher: "Class, who does Dragon Boat Festival commemorate?"

Kid: "Qu Yuan."

Teacher: "Good. And why do we wrap zongzi?"

Kid: "So the fish would eat those instead of Qu Yuan."

Teacher: "Correct."

Kid: "Teacher, so if the fish won't touch zongzi anymore, does that mean the fish are watching their figures too?"

Teacher: "...Does that mean you skipped your homework again?"
en.editorNote: Takes the classic 'wrap zongzi so the fish leave Qu Yuan alone' legend and swerves it into modern diet humor, then gets ambushed by the teacher's counter. Works as an opener for a light festival history post
variations: Short version: "Zongzi were originally fish food. Now even the fish say they're too high-calorie — turns out the fish have more willpower than I do."

items.sourceNote: 南部粽 vs 北部粽 戰爭迷因；改寫為原創家庭對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節飯桌上，北部來的女婿問：「媽，這是南部粽吧？怎麼是水煮的？」

岳母：「對啊，南部粽要用煮的才香。」

女婿：「可是這樣不就跟肉粽湯差不多？」

岳母放下筷子。

岳母：「你是來吃飯的，還是來吃官司的？」

女婿默默把整顆粽子塞進嘴裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北粽之爭是台灣每年端午的固定戰場，笑點在岳母「吃官司」的威嚇——適合傳給跨南北的另一半，提醒對方回鄉過節時嘴巴放乾淨點
variations: 短版：「端午節最危險的不是龍舟翻船，是你在岳母面前說南部粽像粽子湯。」

en.content:
At the festival dinner, the son-in-law from the north asks: "Mom, this is a southern-style zongzi, right? Why is it boiled?"

Mother-in-law: "Yes, southern zongzi has to be boiled to bring out the flavor."

Son-in-law: "But then isn't it basically just zongzi soup?"

She sets down her chopsticks.

Mother-in-law: "Are you here to eat dinner, or to start a lawsuit?"

The son-in-law silently stuffs the entire zongzi into his mouth.
en.editorNote: Taiwan's perennial north-vs-south zongzi war, with the mother-in-law's 'start a lawsuit' threat as the punchline. Send it to a partner from the other region as a friendly warning to watch their mouth at the in-laws'
variations: Short version: "The real danger on Dragon Boat Festival isn't capsizing — it's calling southern zongzi 'just soup' in front of your mother-in-law."

items.sourceNote: 粽 / 重 體重諧音 + 連假發胖自嘲；原創情境

zh-tw.content:
端午連假結束，我站上體重計。

數字往上跳了兩公斤。

我：「不可能，我才吃三顆粽子。」

我媽：「三顆？我看你是把整鍋的『粽』量都扛在身上了。」

我：「媽，這叫節日紀念，是文化傳承。」

我媽：「那你這肚子，傳承得也太完整了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽」量=「重」量，連假發胖的萬年自嘲——適合端午連假收假日傳到群組，配一張體重計照片，引發大家共鳴的哀號
variations: 短版：「端午節後我終於懂什麼叫『粽量級選手』，因為我整個人都升級了。」

en.content:
After the Dragon Boat Festival long weekend, I step on the scale.

The number jumps two kilos.

Me: "No way, I only ate three zongzi."

Mom: "Three? Looks to me like you're carrying the whole pot's worth of weight on you."

Me: "Mom, this is honoring the holiday. It's cultural heritage."

Mom: "Well, that belly of yours sure inherited it completely."
en.editorNote: The eternal post-long-weekend weight-gain self-roast — zongzi being heavy in every sense. Send it on the last day of the festival break with a photo of your scale to summon everyone's collective groan
variations: Short version: "After the festival I finally understand 'heavyweight division' — the whole me got an upgrade."

items.sourceNote: 立蛋 / 龍舟 習俗 + 加班族時事自嘲；原創上班族情境

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「端午連假你要去看龍舟比賽嗎？」

我：「不用看，我每天上班就是在划龍舟。」

同事：「怎麼說？」

我：「老闆在前面打鼓，我在後面拚命划，划到終點還是回到同一條河。」

同事沉默。

我：「而且我們這條船，連粽子都沒得發。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把划龍舟的鼓手、划手結構，對應成老闆和員工的職場日常，苦中作樂——適合端午連假前傳到同事群組，引發社畜共鳴
variations: 短版：「端午節不用特地去看龍舟，我每天上班就是龍舟選手，只是這條船永遠到不了岸。」

en.content:
Coworker: "Are you going to watch the dragon boat races this long weekend?"

Me: "No need. I row a dragon boat every day at work."

Coworker: "How so?"

Me: "The boss bangs the drum up front, I paddle like crazy in the back, and when I reach the finish line I'm right back on the same river."

Silence.

Me: "And on our boat, they don't even hand out zongzi."
en.editorNote: Maps the dragon boat's drummer-and-paddlers setup onto the boss-and-employee grind — gallows humor for the overworked. Send it to coworkers before the festival break for that wage-slave solidarity
variations: Short version: "No need to go watch dragon boats — I'm a paddler every workday. This boat just never reaches shore."

items.sourceNote: 屈原放假時事哏（馬克思留下難懂文本、屈原留下三天假）改寫；原創放假吐槽情境

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師問：「屈原對後世最大的貢獻是什麼？」

小明舉手：「放了我們三天假。」

全班拍手叫好。

老師：「我是想聽《離騷》。」

小明：「老師，《離騷》害我背了一個禮拜，連假只有三天，這明明是負的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原的文學貢獻和現代人最在意的「連假」拿來對比，學生視角吐槽——適合端午連假前傳到同學群組，懂的人都會心一笑
variations: 短版：「屈原最偉大的地方，就是替我們爭取到三天連假，至於《離騷》我們改天再聊。」

en.content:
History teacher: "What was Qu Yuan's greatest gift to later generations?"

Kid raises hand: "Three days off work."

The whole class cheers.

Teacher: "I was hoping you'd say his poetry."

Kid: "Teacher, his poetry took me a week to memorize. The holiday is only three days. That's a net loss."
en.editorNote: Plays Qu Yuan's literary legacy against the thing modern people actually care about — the long weekend. Send it to classmates or coworkers before the festival break for an easy laugh
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan's true legacy is our three-day weekend. We'll discuss the poetry some other time."

items.sourceNote: 粽子諧音「中子」→ 考試「中」榜祝福哏改寫；原創考生情境

zh-tw.content:
考試前一天，阿嬤塞了一顆粽子給我。

阿嬤：「端午吃粽，包你高『粽』。」

我：「阿嬤，是高『中』啦。」

阿嬤：「我知道啊，所以才叫你多吃幾顆，中越多越好。」

我：「……那我等下要去廁所幾趟。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「高粽」諧音「高中」（考試上榜），阿嬤的吉祥話配上孫子的腸胃現實——適合考季前傳給要考試的親友，祝福兼搞笑
variations: 短版：「端午吃粽，包你高粽——考生請收下這份諧音祝福。」

en.content:
The day before my exam, Grandma handed me a zongzi.

Grandma: "Eat a rice dumpling on the festival and you're guaranteed to wrap up first place."

Me: "Grandma, the word is 'pass,' not 'wrap.'"

Grandma: "I know. That's why I'm telling you to eat several — the more you wrap, the better you pass."

Me: "...Then I'm going to be making a lot of bathroom trips."
en.editorNote: In Chinese 'zong' (zongzi) sounds like 'zhong' (to pass / hit the mark on an exam), so Grandma's blessing is a pun — paired with the grandkid's very real digestive math. Send it to friends sitting exams during exam season
variations: Short version: "Eat a zongzi on the festival and you'll wrap up that exam — every test-taker deserves this blessing."

items.sourceNote: 南北龍舟「划船救人 vs 陪屈原」差異哏改寫；原創旁觀對話情境

zh-tw.content:
弟弟看龍舟比賽，問：「他們划這麼快是要去哪？」

我：「以前是要去救屈原啊。」

弟弟：「那現在屈原找到了嗎？」

我：「兩千多年了，每年都重划一次，到現在還沒找到。」

弟弟：「哥，會不會是大家划錯方向了。」

我看著終點線，陷入沉思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟「救屈原」的傳說和兩千年來年年重演的荒謬感拉在一起，弟弟一句童言神補刀——適合一家人看龍舟轉播時的應景對話
variations: 短版：「龍舟年年都在划，救了兩千多年還沒救到，我懷疑大家是划錯方向了。」

en.content:
My little brother watches the dragon boat race and asks: "Why are they paddling so fast? Where are they going?"

Me: "They used to be racing to save Qu Yuan."

Brother: "So did they find him yet?"

Me: "It's been over two thousand years. They redo the race every year and still haven't."

Brother: "Bro... maybe everyone's been paddling the wrong way."

I stare at the finish line, deep in thought.
en.editorNote: Pairs the dragon boat 'rescue Qu Yuan' legend with the absurdity of re-running it for two millennia — then a kid lands the killer line. Perfect for family banter while the race is on TV
variations: Short version: "They've been paddling to rescue Qu Yuan for two thousand years and still haven't found him. I'm starting to think they're going the wrong way."

items.sourceNote: 端午中午立蛋習俗改寫；原創上班族立蛋情境哏

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，同事興奮地說：「快！現在立蛋會成功，立起來一整年都好運。」

我試了五分鐘，蛋立起來了。

同事：「哇你運氣超好！」

結果下午老闆就把我叫進辦公室，多派了三個專案。

我：「原來立起來的是我的工作量。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把端午立蛋的好運習俗，反轉成上班族工作量暴增的黑色幽默——適合在端午當天傳到公司群組，社畜看了會苦笑
variations: 短版：「端午立蛋成功，結果立起來的不是好運，是我下半年的工作量。」

en.content:
At noon on the Dragon Boat Festival, a coworker says excitedly: "Quick! Eggs balance upright right now — stand one up and you'll have good luck all year."

I try for five minutes. The egg stands.

Coworker: "Wow, you're so lucky!"

That afternoon the boss calls me in and dumps three more projects on me.

Me: "So that's what stood up — my workload."
en.editorNote: Flips the festival egg-balancing good-luck tradition into dark office humor about a ballooning workload. Send it to the company group chat on festival day for a weary chuckle from your fellow wage-slaves
variations: Short version: "Balanced my festival egg perfectly. Turns out the thing that stood up wasn't my luck — it was my workload for the rest of the year."

items.sourceUrl: https://tw.news.yahoo.com/天后也愛諧音梗-田馥甄問端午節為何只有玉琴加班
items.sourceNote: '欲擒故縱' homophone riddle (玉琴顧粽) circulating from celebrity quip; rewritten as an original office riddle with a new punchline

zh-tw.content:
「考你一題：端午節大家都放假，為什麼公司只有玉琴一個人要加班？」

「為什麼？」

「因為『玉琴顧粽』啊。」

「……欲擒故縱？這跟加班有什麼關係？」

「沒關係，老闆只是想找個聽起來很有道理的理由叫她來顧粽子。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典諧音謎題「玉琴顧粽=欲擒故縱」，再補一刀老闆硬湊理由的職場真實感——適合端午連假前傳到公司群組，被點名加班的同事看了會會心一笑
variations: 純謎題版：「端午節為什麼只有玉琴要加班？因為玉琴顧粽（欲擒故縱）。」——適合當猜謎開場，講完記得自己先笑

en.content:
"Riddle: everyone's off for the Dragon Boat Festival, so why is Grace the only one stuck working?"

"Why?"

"Because she's the keeper of the dumplings."

"...how is that a reason to make her come in?"

"It isn't. The boss just wanted something that sounded official enough to put her on dumpling duty."
en.editorNote: An office riddle built on a Chinese pun (玉琴顧粽 'minding the dumplings' sounds like 欲擒故縱 'playing hard to get'), capped with a jab at bosses inventing fake reasons. Drop it in the group chat before the long weekend
variations: Riddle-only version: "Why's the keeper of the dumplings the only one working the holiday? Because someone had to mind the zongzi."

items.sourceNote: 'oar-some' / rowing pun family from English dragon boat joke roundups; rewritten as an original paddler dialogue

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽前，隊長對大家精神喊話：「今天我們一定要划出最好的成績！」

隊員阿凱舉手：「隊長，我有個問題。」

「說。」

「如果我們輸了，是不是就『槳』勵獎而已？」

隊長盯著他三秒：「你再講一個諧音梗，我就把你『槳』下水。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟的「槳」拿來玩諧音（獎勵獎、獎下水），笑點在隊長被冷到反擊——適合分組競賽、團隊活動暖場，講完氣氛會瞬間又熱又尷尬
variations: 短版：「龍舟隊輸了會得什麼？槳勵獎。」——適合當一句話冷笑話，丟出去看誰先翻白眼

en.content:
Before the race, the captain rallies the crew: "Today we're going to row the best run of our lives!"

Kevin raises his hand. "Captain, quick question."

"Go ahead."

"If we lose, do we at least get an oar-some participation trophy?"

The captain stares for three seconds. "One more pun and I'm tossing you oar-board."
en.editorNote: Leans on the 'oar/awe' pun (oar-some, oar-board) with the captain firing back at the groaner. Great as an icebreaker for any team race or paddling event — expect immediate eye-rolls
variations: Short version: "Why don't dragon boats make good comedians? Their jokes are too oar-dinary."

items.sourceNote: novelty cold-dessert zongzi trend (楊枝甘露/香菜冰粽) from festival news; rewritten as an original family reaction joke

zh-tw.content:
今年端午，我買了網路爆紅的「楊枝甘露冰粽」回家給阿嬤嚐鮮。

阿嬤咬了一口，皺眉：「這是粽子？怎麼是冰的、還甜的？」

我：「這是創新口味啦，現在年輕人都吃這個。」

阿嬤默默走進廚房，端出一盤她包的傳統鹹粽：「來，吃個正常的，壓壓驚。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用網紅冰粽對比阿嬤傳統鹹粽，笑點在阿嬤那句「壓壓驚」——適合端午回家過節時傳到家族群組，長輩看了絕對認同
variations: 短版：「買了楊枝甘露冰粽給阿嬤，她吃一口問我：『這到底是粽子還是甜點？』我也答不出來。」

en.content:
This year I brought home the viral mango-pomelo iced zongzi for Grandma to try.

She takes one bite and frowns. "This is a zongzi? Why is it cold? And sweet?"

"It's a new flavor, Grandma. All the young people eat these now."

She quietly walks to the kitchen and returns with a plate of her classic savory dumplings. "Here. Eat a real one to recover from the shock."
en.editorNote: Pits trendy iced dessert zongzi against Grandma's traditional savory ones, landing on her deadpan 'recover from the shock.' Perfect for the family group chat when you head home for the festival
variations: Short version: "Gave Grandma a fancy iced dessert zongzi. She took one bite and asked, 'Is this a dumpling or a dessert?' Honestly, I had no answer."

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone blessing format (粽/眾, 粽/中) from 2026 端午賀詞 compilation; rewritten as an original lottery-themed exchange

zh-tw.content:
「端午節我幫你買了一張刮刮樂。」

「謝謝！有中嗎？」

「沒中。但我想說的是——刮刮樂沒中沒關係，反正我早就『粽』了你的獨。」

「……你是想說『中了你的毒』？」

「對，端午節限定告白，只送你一個。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把刮刮樂「中獎」和「粽」諧音串成告白，土到掉渣但很可愛——適合端午節傳給曖昧對象，沒中刮刮樂剛好當話題，告白失敗也能裝沒事
variations: 純祝福版：「祝你端午節粽（眾）星拱月、好運連連，粽（中）獎機會都歸你。」——適合傳給朋友，諧音吉祥又不尷尬

en.content:
"I bought you a scratch ticket for the festival."

"Aw, thanks! Did it win?"

"Nope. But here's the thing — the ticket didn't win, yet somehow I'm already wrapped up in you."

"...wrapped up like a zongzi?"

"Exactly. Limited-edition festival confession, one per customer."
en.editorNote: Ties a losing scratch ticket to a 'wrapped up in you' confession (a nod to zongzi being wrapped). Cheesy but sweet — send it to your crush on festival day, and the dud ticket doubles as a graceful exit if it flops
variations: Blessing-only version: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may all the luck (and every winning ticket) be wrapped up just for you."

items.sourceNote: 「欲擒故縱／玉琴顧粽」諧音腦筋急轉彎流行於端午梗圖；改寫為原創的辦公室對話版本

zh-tw.content:
「考你一題端午節腦筋急轉彎：為什麼全公司都放連假，只有一個同事還在加班？」

「裁員？專案趕不完？」

「都不是。因為她叫玉琴，要『玉琴顧粽』啊。」

「……欲擒故縱？這跟加班有什麼關係？」

「沒關係，但你笑了，端午連假就值得了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「欲擒故縱」拆成「玉琴顧粽」的經典諧音腦筋急轉彎，重點在最後那句自嘲——適合端午連假前在辦公室群組丟出來，緩和大家收假前的厭世感
variations: 極短版：「端午節為什麼只有玉琴要加班？因為她要『玉琴顧粽』（欲擒故縱）。」
接龍版：再加一句「那阿宗呢？阿宗在『阿宗顧粽』，意思一樣，只是比較累。」

en.content:
"Festival brain-teaser: the whole office is off for the long weekend, so why is one coworker still at her desk?"

"Layoffs? A deadline she can't escape?"

"Nope. Her job today is literally to guard the dumplings. She's on zongzi duty."

"...that's not a real job."

"It's not. But you smiled, so the long weekend is already worth it."
en.editorNote: An English-friendly version of the classic festival riddle, leaning on the absurd 'zongzi duty' punchline and the self-aware closer. Drop it in the work chat right before the long weekend to take the edge off everyone's pre-holiday mood
variations: Short version: "Why is one coworker stuck at the office during the festival? She got assigned to zongzi guard duty. Someone has to."

items.sourceNote: dragon-boat 'oar-some / paddle' pun cluster; rewritten as an original coach-and-rower exchange

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽前，隊長精神喊話：「大家記住，我們是一條船上的人，要同心協力！」

新隊員舉手：「隊長，那如果我划錯邊怎麼辦？」

隊長：「沒關係，那代表你『槳』來會是個不一樣的人才——只是不是划龍舟的那種。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用「槳」（ㄐㄧㄤˇ）諧音「將」來收尾，吐槽划錯邊的隊員——適合分享給有參加龍舟隊、或公司端午團隊活動的朋友，自嘲團隊默契用
variations: 短版：「教練說我『槳』來大有可為，後來才發現他是說我太常划錯邊。」

en.content:
Before the dragon boat race, the captain rallies the crew. "Remember — we're all in the same boat. Total teamwork out there!"

A rookie raises a hand. "Captain, what if I paddle on the wrong side?"

Captain: "Then you'll be truly oar-some... at slowing us all down. Just stick to copying the person in front of you."
en.editorNote: Built around the 'oar-some' pun with a deadpan jab at the rookie. Great for friends on a dragon boat team or doing a company festival paddling event — perfect for ribbing whoever keeps catching crabs
variations: Short version: "Coach told me I had oar-some potential. Turned out he meant I had an oar-some talent for paddling the wrong way."

items.sourceNote: 「粽（中）／高粽（中）」榜諧音祝福格式；改寫為原創的學生考試對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節前，媽媽塞了一顆肉粽給準備考試的兒子。

兒子：「媽，吃粽子真的會考上嗎？」

媽媽：「當然，吃了『包粽』（包中）。」

兒子：「那我這次模擬考爆掉，是因為吃太少還是吃太多？」

媽媽：「是因為你光吃粽子不念書。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用「包粽／包中」諧音串起考生吃粽子求好運的習俗，笑點在媽媽最後神補刀——適合端午前傳給準備考試的同學或自己的小孩，吉祥又不失幽默
variations: 純祝福版：「祝你端午節包粽（包中）、高粽（高中）、粽（中）榜題名，一口氣全包了。」
短版：「吃粽子會包中，所以我考前吃了三顆——結果包『腫』了。」

en.content:
The day before the festival, Mom hands her exam-bound son a sticky rice dumpling.

"Mom, does eating a zongzi actually help me pass?"

"Of course. In Chinese, 'wrapped' sounds just like 'guaranteed to pass.' It's basically a study aid."

"Then why did I bomb my last practice test — too few or too many?"

"Because you only ate the dumplings. You skipped the studying part."
en.editorNote: Explains the Taiwanese custom of eating zongzi for exam luck (the pun on 'wrapped' = 'pass'), capped by Mom's perfect reality check. Send it to a student in your life before the festival — lucky and funny at once
variations: Short version: "They say eating a zongzi guarantees you pass, so I ate three before the test. Now I'm guaranteed a stomachache."

items.sourceNote: 屈原投江 / 立蛋習俗的端午梗圖題材；改寫為原創的屈原冷哲學對話

zh-tw.content:
「聽說端午節中午十二點可以把蛋立起來，因為地心引力最平衡。」

「真的假的？」

「假的，其實你練習夠久，哪天都立得起來。」

「那為什麼大家只在端午立蛋？」

「因為只有端午，連立一顆蛋都會被全家拍手叫好。其他日子立蛋，只會被問你是不是又沒事做。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 拆穿端午立蛋的都市傳說，笑點在最後一句對「儀式感」的吐槽——適合端午當天立蛋成功後得意地傳到群組，順便嘴一下節日的浮誇
variations: 屈原版：「屈原跳河是為了愛國，我端午立蛋是為了愛現，我們都在用自己的方式被記住。」

en.content:
"They say you can only balance an egg upright at noon on the festival, because gravity is perfectly balanced then."

"Wait, is that true?"

"Nope. With enough practice you can do it any day of the year."

"Then why does everyone only try on festival day?"

"Because it's the one day your whole family applauds you for standing up an egg. Do it any other day and they'll just ask if you're unemployed again."
en.editorNote: Debunks the festival egg-balancing myth, landing on a jab at our need for ritual and applause. Perfect to post in the group chat the moment your egg stays upright on festival day, with a wink at how dramatic the holiday gets
variations: Short version: "Balancing an egg works any day. We just save it for the festival because it's the only day applause is guaranteed."

items.sourceNote: 粽/中 homophone blessing format from 2026 端午祝福語 compilations; rewritten into an original boss-employee dialogue

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，老闆發粽子給大家。

老闆：「祝大家端午包中、業績長紅！」

小陳：「謝謝老闆，那獎金也包中嗎？」

老闆：「獎金的部分，包……在我心裡。」

小陳：「那我把粽子也包在心裡，不吃了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班族端午限定吐槽，老闆「包在心裡」=畫大餅，小陳神回嘴——適合傳到公司群組或同事私訊，年終發粽不發獎金的季節最有共鳴
variations: 單句版：「老闆說端午包中，結果包的是工作量。」——適合配一張加班照

en.content:
The day before the festival, the boss handed out rice dumplings.

Boss: "Here's to a wrapped-up, winning year for all of you!"

Me: "Thanks, boss. Is the bonus getting wrapped up too?"

Boss: "The bonus is... wrapped up safely in my heart."

Me: "Cool. Then I'll keep this dumpling in my heart too and not eat it."
en.editorNote: Office-life festival jab — the boss's 'wrapped up in my heart' is corporate-speak for an empty promise, and the comeback lands clean. Drop it in the work chat during the season when companies hand out dumplings but not bonuses
variations: Short version: "Boss promised a wrapped-up year. Turns out he meant the workload."

items.sourceNote: oar/oarsome dragon boat pun family; rewritten into an original paddler dialogue with a fresh punchline

zh-tw.content:
划龍舟比賽前，隊長精神喊話：「兄弟們，今天我們一定要划出個成績！」

隊員：「隊長，我們上次是最後一名耶。」

隊長：「那是因為我們太謙虛，把舟讓給別人先到。」

隊員：「⋯⋯這次能不能不要這麼有風度？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舟隊的自我安慰式幽默，把吊車尾說成「有風度」——適合運動社團、公司龍舟隊賽前或賽後傳，輸了也能笑著面對
variations: 短版：「我們龍舟隊不是慢，是在欣賞沿途風景。」——適合貼在團隊合照下面

en.content:
Before the dragon boat race, the captain rallied the crew: "Today, boys, we paddle our way to glory!"

Crew: "Captain, we came dead last at the last race."

Captain: "That's because we're polite. We let everyone else finish first."

Crew: "...Can we maybe be a little less oarsome and well-mannered this time?"
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating crew humor that reframes finishing last as good sportsmanship. Great for a sports club or company dragon boat team to share before or after a race — even a loss gets a laugh
variations: Short version: "Our dragon boat team isn't slow. We're just savoring the scenery the whole way down the river."

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone blessing + 減肥 (diet) festival theme common in 端午賀詞 lists; rewritten as an original group-chat scene

zh-tw.content:
閨蜜群組裡。

小美：「端午節到了，這次我絕對只吃一顆粽子。」

三天後。

小美：「我說的『一顆』是指『一次拿一顆』，總共幾次沒講。」

阿芳：「妳這個諧音梗叫『粽（總）共吃很多』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午減肥失敗的閨蜜對話，「一次一顆」的自我欺騙太真實——適合傳到女生群組，端午過後集體罪惡感爆發的時候最有效
variations: 單句版：「端午節我沒有破戒，我只是把戒律包進粽子裡一起吃掉了。」

en.content:
In the group chat.

Mei: "Festival's here. This year I'm only eating ONE rice dumpling. I swear."

Three days later.

Mei: "When I said 'one,' I meant 'one at a time.' I never specified how many times."

Fang: "Girl, that's not a diet. That's a hostage negotiation with carbs."
en.editorNote: The all-too-real festival diet-fail chat. 'One at a time' is peak self-deception — perfect to share in the girls' group when the post-festival guilt kicks in
variations: Short version: "I didn't break my diet this festival. I just wrapped the rules inside a dumpling and ate them too."

items.sourceNote: Qu Yuan / 屈原 historical-irony joke angle from festival origin explainers; rewritten into an original modern punchline

zh-tw.content:
「你知道屈原其實是史上第一個被『已讀不回』氣到的人嗎？」

「怎麼說？」

「他上奏給楚王的建議，全部被忽略，最後只好投江。」

「那我每天傳訊息給暗戀對象也都被忽略，我算什麼？」

「你算端午節的傳人。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原的歷史悲劇連結到現代「已讀不回」的痛，笑點在最後一句的封號——適合傳給每天被已讀不回的朋友，苦中作樂
variations: 短版：「屈原的建議被忽略才投江，我的訊息被忽略只能投手機到沙發。」

en.content:
"Did you know Qu Yuan was basically history's first person driven mad by being left on read?"

"How so?"

"He kept giving the king brilliant advice, the king ignored every word, and he ended up throwing himself into the river."

"So what am I, texting my crush every day and getting ignored?"

"You, my friend, are carrying on the festival's legacy."
en.editorNote: Ties Qu Yuan's tragic history to the modern pain of being left on read, with the title-bestowing punchline doing the work. Perfect for the friend whose crush never texts back — gallows humor for the festival
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan got ignored and threw himself in a river. I get ignored and just throw my phone at the couch."

items.sourceNote: 南北粽 (sweet vs savory dumpling) debate meme theme from 端午梗圖; rewritten as an original couple argument scene

zh-tw.content:
男友：「我跟妳說，南部粽用水煮的才正統。」

女友：「北部粽用炒的才香，你不懂。」

兩人吵了二十分鐘。

阿嬤端出一盤粽子：「來，吃我包的。」

兩人吃完同時說：「⋯⋯這到底是南部還北部？」

阿嬤：「這是『不要在我家吵架』口味。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北粽世紀之爭加上阿嬤一槌定音，「不要吵架口味」收得漂亮——適合傳給每年端午都要為甜鹹、南北吵一架的家庭或情侶
variations: 單句版：「南北粽吵不完，但阿嬤的粽子永遠是冠軍口味。」

en.content:
Boyfriend: "Listen, the southern-style dumpling boiled in water is the only authentic one."

Girlfriend: "No way, the northern stir-fried one is way more fragrant. You don't get it."

Twenty minutes of arguing later.

Grandma sets down a plate: "Here. Eat the ones I made."

They both finish and ask at once: "...Wait, is this northern or southern?"

Grandma: "It's the 'don't-argue-in-my-house' flavor."
en.editorNote: The eternal sweet-vs-savory, north-vs-south dumpling war, settled by Grandma's mic-drop flavor. Perfect for the family or couple who reignites the debate every single festival
variations: Short version: "The north-south dumpling war never ends, but Grandma's dumpling always wins the title."

items.sourceNote: 「手機用粽葉包起來煮，電池續航變兩倍」格式的端午搞笑祝福梗；改寫成原創對話

zh-tw.content:
朋友傳訊：「教你一招，把手機用粽葉包起來，丟進鍋裡煮一小時，電池續航力會變兩倍。」

我：「真的假的？」

朋友：「真的，因為從此你不會再想滑手機了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 假科普真整人的端午冷笑話，前面煞有其事後面突然反轉——適合丟到群組裡看誰真的差點去煮手機，或回給那種整天傳偏方的長輩
variations: 單句版：「端午養生小撇步：把手機包成粽子煮一小時，保證從此戒掉手機癮。」
情境版：傳給總是低頭滑手機的另一半，附註「這招專治你」

en.content:
Friend texts: "Life hack — wrap your phone in bamboo leaves, boil it for an hour, and your battery life doubles."

Me: "Wait, seriously?"

Friend: "Seriously. Because after that you'll never want to look at your phone again."
en.editorNote: A fake-life-hack prank dressed up as festival wisdom, with a deadpan flip at the end. Great for the group chat to see who almost boiled their phone, or as a reply to the relative who forwards every miracle tip
variations: Short version: "Dragon Boat Festival wellness tip: wrap your phone like a dumpling and boil it for an hour. Guaranteed screen-time cure."
Scene version: send to the partner who's always on their phone with the note 'this one's for you'

items.sourceNote: 'drag-gone boat' 龍舟英文諧音梗；中文改以「懶舟」對應，重寫為原創雙語冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
「為什麼這艘龍舟划到一半就不動了？」

「因為它累到變成『懶舟』了。」

隊長扶額：「不是，是因為你們十個人有八個在拍照。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舟諧音冷笑話加上隊長的現實吐槽，「懶舟」對應「龍舟」——適合端午看龍舟賽時傳給朋友，或自嘲團隊划水（雙關）的工作群組
variations: 單句版：「龍舟比賽輸了不是技術問題，是大家忙著拍限動。」
辦公室版：傳到工作群組「我們這組是龍舟還是懶舟？」

en.content:
"Why did the dragon boat stop halfway through the race?"

"Because it turned into a drag-gone boat."

The captain sighs: "No. It's because eight of you ten paddlers are taking selfies."
en.editorNote: A groan-worthy dragon-boat pun followed by the captain's painfully real punchline. Perfect for watching the races with friends, or for the work chat that loves to 'paddle' (slack off)
variations: Short version: "We didn't lose the dragon boat race on skill — we lost it on everyone posting stories mid-paddle."
Office version: drop in the team chat — 'are we a dragon boat or a drag-gone boat today?'

items.sourceNote: 丟粽子餵魚以免魚吃屈原的傳說邏輯漏洞梗；改寫成原創魚群對話

zh-tw.content:
傳說大家丟粽子下河，是怕魚去吃屈原。

河底兩條魚對話。

魚A：「人類丟了好多粽子下來欸。」

魚B：「吃啊，吃飽我們就有力氣⋯⋯去找屈原當飯後甜點了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 戳破端午傳說邏輯漏洞的黑色幽默冷笑話，魚吃飽反而更有力氣——適合傳給喜歡認真考據節日由來的朋友，或單純愛看反轉梗的人
variations: 單句版：「丟粽子餵魚這個計畫，唯一的問題就是魚吃飽會更有精神。」
考據版：傳給每年都要解釋端午由來的那位朋友，附「這個bug你有想過嗎」

en.content:
Legend says people threw dumplings into the river so the fish wouldn't eat Qu Yuan.

Two fish at the bottom, chatting.

Fish A: "The humans dropped a ton of dumplings down here."

Fish B: "Eat up. Once we're full, we'll have the energy to go find Qu Yuan for dessert."
en.editorNote: Dark-humor cold joke poking a hole in the festival's origin logic — the well-fed fish are now more motivated, not less. Great for the friend who loves to fact-check holiday legends
variations: Short version: "The feed-the-fish-dumplings plan had one flaw: full fish are more energetic, not less."
Fact-check version: send to the friend who explains the festival's origin every year, captioned 'ever thought about this bug?'

items.sourceNote: 「粽情」=「縱情」端午諧音祝福語格式；改寫為原創土味情話

zh-tw.content:
「端午節到了，我想對你說一句話。」

「你說。」

「這輩子我只想對你『粽情』到底。」

「⋯⋯你是說縱情，還是說你想用粽子把我綁住？」

「兩個都要。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽情」諧音「縱情」的端午土味情話，最後反問再補一刀超甜——適合傳給另一半，土到對方一邊翻白眼一邊偷笑那種
variations: 單句版：「端午快樂，這輩子粽情於你，一生不換口味。」
已讀版：對方已讀不回時補一句「沒回我？那我把你包成粽子囉」

en.content:
"Dragon Boat Festival's here. I want to tell you something."

"Go on."

"For the rest of my life, I only want to be wrapped up in you."

"...Wrapped up in love, or do you literally want to tie me up like a dumpling?"

"Both."
en.editorNote: A cheesy festival pickup line riffing on dumplings being 'wrapped,' with a flirty double meaning. Perfect for your partner — corny enough that they'll roll their eyes and secretly smile
variations: Short version: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — I'm wrapping up the rest of my life in you, no other flavor."
Left-on-read version: when they don't reply, follow up with 'No answer? Guess I'll just wrap you up like a dumpling.'

items.sourceNote: 端午立蛋習俗梗圖主題；改寫為原創辦公室情境冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，同事們圍在桌邊立蛋。

小李試了半小時，蛋終於立起來了，全場歡呼。

主管走過來：「立得不錯，那這個專案你也立個flag吧。」

小李：「⋯⋯蛋我可以立，那個我不行。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋習俗結合職場「立flag」雙關的冷笑話，最後一句道盡上班族心聲——適合傳到工作群組，端午連假前療癒一下大家
variations: 單句版：「端午節立蛋成功，立flag失敗，這很合理。」
厭世版：傳給同事「蛋立起來了，但我的人生還沒」

en.content:
At noon on the festival, coworkers gather to balance eggs upright.

Li tries for half an hour, the egg finally stands, everyone cheers.

The manager walks over: "Nice balancing. Now go ahead and commit to a deadline on that project too."

Li: "...I can stand an egg up. That, I cannot."
en.editorNote: The festival egg-balancing tradition meets office deadline dread, with a closing line every worker feels in their soul. Great for the team chat right before the holiday weekend
variations: Short version: "Stood the egg up, couldn't commit to the deadline. Sounds about right."
Burnout version: send to a coworker — 'the egg is standing, but my life still isn't.'

items.sourceNote: 划龍舟英文諧音梗（oar-some/row）主題；改寫為原創雙語諧音冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
划龍舟比賽前，隊長替大家打氣。

隊長：「今天我們一定會贏，因為我們的默契——」

隊員：「划得來？」

隊長：「不，是『槳』不離口、心不離手。」

全隊：「⋯⋯隊長你先把口號划順再說。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用「槳」諧音「獎」、「划得來」雙關的划龍舟冷笑話，最後隊員吐槽超有畫面——適合端午前傳到球隊或活動群組暖場
variations: 單句版：「划龍舟最怕什麼？怕『槳』金沒拿到，只拿到一身水。」
辦公室版：傳給同事『今天會議我們要划得來一點，不要各划各的』

en.content:
Before the dragon boat race, the captain fires up the team.

Captain: "We're winning today, because our teamwork is totally—"

Paddler: "Oar-some?"

Captain: "I was going to say 'in sync,' but sure, that too."

Whole team: "...Captain, maybe get the cheer in sync first."
en.editorNote: An 'oar-some' rowing pun with a deadpan team callback. Great for warming up a sports group or event chat in the lead-up to the festival
variations: Short version: "What do dragon boaters fear most? Not winning the prize — just getting soaked instead."
Office version: send to a coworker — 'Let's row in sync in today's meeting, not all paddle our own way.'

items.sourceNote: 粽子吃太多/腸胃負擔的端午梗；改寫為原創家庭情境冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
端午節，阿嬤包了三十顆粽子。

阿嬤：「多吃幾顆，補一下。」

孫子吃到第五顆：「阿嬤，我覺得我快變成粽子了。」

阿嬤：「那正好，明年就不用包你那份。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 阿嬤的端午限定關愛攻擊加上神回的冷笑話，溫馨又好笑——適合端午回家前傳到家庭群組，預告今年也逃不過
variations: 單句版：「端午節的快樂，是用三顆粽子的胃食道逆流換來的。」
減肥版：傳給朋友『今天先不算熱量，這叫文化傳承』

en.content:
On the festival, Grandma wraps thirty rice dumplings.

Grandma: "Eat a few more, build yourself up."

By the fifth one, the grandson groans: "Grandma, I think I'm turning into a dumpling."

Grandma: "Perfect. Then next year I won't have to wrap your share."
en.editorNote: Grandma's festival love-bombing meets a perfect comeback. Warm and funny — send it to the family chat before heading home, fair warning you won't escape this year either
variations: Short version: "Festival joy: paid for in three dumplings' worth of heartburn."
Diet version: text a friend — 'Not counting calories today, this is cultural heritage.'

items.sourceNote: 端午划龍舟包粽子真實年代早於屈原的歷史冷知識；改寫為原創歷史哏冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師：「同學們知道嗎？划龍舟其實比屈原還早出現。」

學生：「那為什麼大家都說是紀念屈原？」

老師：「因為屈原比較會行銷。」

學生：「⋯⋯所以他是第一個把節日做成IP的人？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 結合端午習俗年代早於屈原的冷知識，用現代行銷哏收尾的冷笑話——適合傳給愛考據的朋友，或端午當天當開場白
variations: 單句版：「划龍舟比屈原早，但屈原把版權搶下來了。」
厭世版：傳到工作群組『連端午節都懂的人設經營，我們還在學』

en.content:
History teacher: "Did you know dragon boat racing actually predates the poet it's said to honor?"

Student: "Then why does everyone say it's to remember him?"

Teacher: "Because he was better at marketing."

Student: "...So he was the first guy to turn a holiday into his own brand?"
en.editorNote: A history nugget — the customs are older than the poet — capped with a modern branding joke. Send it to your trivia-loving friend, or use it as an opener on the day
variations: Short version: "The boat races came first, but the poet locked down the copyright."
Burnout version: post in the work chat — 'Even the festival mastered personal branding before we did.'

items.sourceNote: 端午香包/避邪習俗祝福語主題；改寫為原創諧音土味情話

zh-tw.content:
「端午節我送你一個香包。」

「為什麼是香包？」

「因為它能避邪，而你身邊最大的邪——」

「是誰？」

「是別人，因為你只能喜歡我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用端午香包避邪習俗包裝的土味情話，最後霸道收尾，土甜剛好——適合傳給曖昧對象或另一半，端午限定撒糖
variations: 單句版：「香包避邪，我避你旁邊所有想追你的人。」
已讀版：對方裝沒看到時補一句『香包我都準備好了，你要當邪還是當我的人？』

en.content:
"For the festival, I got you a little scented sachet."

"Why a sachet?"

"They're said to ward off bad spirits. And the biggest bad spirit near you—"

"Is who?"

"Is everyone else, because you're only allowed to like me."
en.editorNote: A cheesy pickup line wrapped in the festival's protective-sachet tradition, landing on a possessive-but-sweet punchline. Perfect for a crush or partner — festival-only flirting
variations: Short version: "Sachets ward off bad spirits. I ward off everyone trying to win you over."
Left-on-read version: when they pretend not to see it — 'Sachet's ready. You want to be the bad spirit, or be mine?'

items.sourceNote: 端午立蛋習俗梗圖主題；改寫為原創立蛋對話，加入現代物理吐槽

zh-tw.content:
「媽，為什麼端午節中午要立蛋？」

「因為這天太陽正中，地球引力最平衡，蛋才立得起來。」

小孩試了三十次，蛋一直倒。

「媽，地球引力是不是壞掉了？」

媽媽默默把蛋拿去煮了。

「現在它躺著，比較符合它的人生規劃。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午中午立蛋習俗加上小孩的科學疑問，最後媽媽務實收尾——適合親子端午活動後傳家庭群組，或自嘲立蛋失敗的人
variations: 單句版：「端午立蛋失敗沒關係，至少證明它有自己的想法。」
厭世版：傳到工作群組『我就是那顆立不起來的蛋，被生活拿去煮了』

en.content:
"Mom, why do we stand eggs upright at noon on this festival?"

"Because the sun's directly overhead and gravity's perfectly balanced, so the egg stays up."

The kid tries thirty times. The egg keeps toppling.

"Mom, is gravity broken?"

Mom quietly takes the egg and boils it.

"There. Lying down suits its life plan better."
en.editorNote: The noon egg-standing tradition meets a kid's science questions, with a deadpan practical punchline. Great for the family chat after a festival activity, or for anyone who failed at egg-standing
variations: Short version: "Couldn't stand the egg upright? At least it has a mind of its own."
Burnout version: post in the work chat — 'I'm the egg that won't stand up, so life just boiled me.'

items.sourceNote: 鹹甜粽南北之爭網路梗主題；改寫為原創家庭餐桌對話

zh-tw.content:
南部人問北部人：「你們北部粽是不是把米先炒過再蒸？」

北部人：「對啊，比較香。」

南部人：「那不就是用粽葉包的油飯？」

北部人沉默三秒。

「⋯⋯那你們南部粽水煮的，不就是用粽葉裝的稀飯？」

兩人對視，默默各自吃自己的粽子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北粽永恆之爭，互嗆對方根本不是粽子，最後誰也說服不了誰——適合端午餐桌話題，或傳到南北朋友混雜的群組引戰（友善版）
variations: 單句版：「北部粽是油飯，南部粽是稀飯，和平的關鍵是各吃各的。」
和事佬版：『不管南北粽，沾醬油膏的都是好粽。』

en.content:
Southerner asks the Northerner: "You guys stir-fry the rice before steaming your zongzi, right?"

Northerner: "Yeah, more fragrant that way."

Southerner: "So it's just oily fried rice wrapped in a leaf?"

Three seconds of silence.

"...And your boiled southern ones — isn't that just rice porridge in a leaf?"

They stare at each other, then quietly eat their own.
en.editorNote: The eternal north-vs-south zongzi war, each side insisting the other's isn't a real dumpling, nobody winning. Perfect for the festival dinner table or a friendly stir in a mixed group chat
variations: Short version: "Northern zongzi is fried rice, southern is porridge — peace means everyone eats their own."
Peacemaker version: 'North or south, any zongzi with a good dipping sauce is a good zongzi.'

items.sourceNote: 端午連假主題祝福語；改寫為原創職場放假冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「端午連假你要幹嘛？」

「我要像粽子一樣。」

「什麼意思？」

「被棉繩綁緊緊，在家躺著，誰都別想叫我出門。」

「那連假結束呢？」

「被解開，丟進滾水裡，繼續上班。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把連假比喻成粽子的一生，從被綁著躺平到被丟進滾水（上班）——適合連假前傳到同事群組，引發集體共鳴的厭世幽默
variations: 單句版：「連假就是粽子的賞味期，過了就要回鍋。」
收假版：『假期結束，本粽已下鍋，正在被生活水煮。』

en.content:
Coworker asks: "What are you doing over the festival long weekend?"

"I'm going full zongzi."

"Meaning?"

"Wrapped up tight in string, lying at home, nobody's getting me to go out."

"And after the long weekend?"

"Untied, dropped into boiling water, back to work."
en.editorNote: The long weekend as the life of a zongzi — from bundled-up couch potato to getting boiled (going back to work). Send it to the team chat before the break for some relatable office gloom-humor
variations: Short version: "A long weekend is a zongzi's shelf life — once it's over, back in the pot."
Back-to-work version: 'Holiday's over. This dumpling's in the pot, getting boiled by life.'

items.sourceNote: 英文 dragon boat oar/row 雙關笑話主題；改寫為原創龍舟隊對話並補上中文諧音版

zh-tw.content:
教練：「你們划龍舟最大的問題是什麼，知道嗎？」

隊員：「不知道。」

教練：「你們槳都沒划好。」

隊員：「教練，你是說『獎』還是『槳』？」

教練：「都沒有。你們既沒得獎，也沒划好槳。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舟「槳」與「獎」諧音雙關，教練一句話損隊員兩次——適合運動社團、公司龍舟隊賽後檢討時傳，輸了也能笑著面對
variations: 單句版：「沒得獎沒關係，至少槳要划好——結果兩個都沒有。」
自嘲版：傳到隊伍群組『今年的關鍵字：無槳無獎』

en.content:
Coach: "You know the biggest problem with your dragon boat team?"

Paddler: "No idea."

Coach: "You've got no oar-ganization."

Paddler: "Coach, did you say organization or oar?"

Coach: "Neither. You won no awards, and you can't handle an oar."
en.editorNote: An 'oar/organization' pun where the coach roasts the team twice in one line. Great for a sports club or company dragon boat squad's post-race debrief — you can laugh even after losing
variations: Short version: "No awards is fine, just handle the oar right — turns out, neither happened."
Self-roast version: post in the team chat — 'This year's theme: no oars, no awards.'

items.sourceNote: 南部粽 vs 北部粽 之爭題材；改寫為原創家庭對話與諧音收尾

zh-tw.content:
兒子問：「媽，到底是北部粽好吃還是南部粽好吃？」

媽媽說：「都別吵了，今年我做了一個新口味。」

兒子興奮：「什麼口味？」

媽媽：「中立粽。我兩邊都不得罪。」

兒子：「……所以是沒包料的白米飯對吧。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用「中立」化解南北粽世紀之爭的冷笑話——適合在家族群組裡丟出來，南北口味擁護者吵不完的時候當和事佬
variations: 單句版：「南北粽我都愛，因為我兩個都吃，這叫真正的中立。」
上班版：辦公室訂粽子被問口味，回『我吃中立粽，不參與戰爭』

en.content:
Son: "Mom, which is better — northern-style zongzi or southern-style?"

Mom: "Stop arguing. This year I made a brand-new flavor."

Son, excited: "What flavor?"

Mom: "Neutral. I refuse to take sides."

Son: "...so it's just plain rice with no filling, right?"
en.editorNote: A 'neutral dumpling' joke that defuses the eternal northern-vs-southern zongzi rivalry. Perfect for the family group chat when the regional-taste debate flares up again
variations: Short version: "I love both northern and southern zongzi — I eat both, so I'm officially neutral."
Office version: when asked your zongzi preference for the group order, reply 'I eat the neutral one, I don't join wars.'

items.sourceNote: 屈原投江題材幽默化；改寫為原創對話並避免不敬，聚焦『紀念』與『放假』反差梗

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師問：「同學們，我們為什麼要紀念屈原？」

小明舉手：「因為他讓我們有粽子吃。」

老師：「還有呢？」

小明：「還有……他讓我們放假。」

老師：「所以你最感謝他哪一點？」

小明：「放假那個。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 上班族與學生都懂的端午心聲——紀念屈原是真的，但最感激的果然還是那一天假。適合放假前一天傳給同事自嘲
variations: 單句版：「謝謝屈原，讓我有粽子吃，更謝謝他讓我有假放。」
上班版：請假單備註欄寫『紀念屈原，順便紀念我所剩無幾的特休』

en.content:
History teacher: "Class, why do we honor Qu Yuan?"

Kid: "Because he gave us zongzi to eat."

Teacher: "And?"

Kid: "And... he gave us a day off."

Teacher: "So which do you appreciate most?"

Kid: "The day off one."
en.editorNote: The honest Dragon Boat Festival sentiment every student and office worker shares — we do honor Qu Yuan, but the day off is the real MVP. Great to send a coworker the day before the long weekend
variations: Short version: "Thanks Qu Yuan — for the zongzi, but mostly for the day off."
Office version: on the time-off request, write 'Honoring Qu Yuan, and the last of my paid leave.'

items.sourceNote: 粽子綁繩/解繩減肥題材；改寫為原創減肥自嘲冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
「我決定端午節要減肥。」

「怎麼減？」

「我跟粽子學的——做人要懂得綁住自己。」

「那你綁住了嗎？」

「綁住了，綁住一顆，又一顆，又一顆……」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午節減肥破功的經典自嘲——「綁住自己」表面是自律，結果綁的是粽子。適合放假吃太多後傳到減肥群組求安慰
variations: 單句版：「端午節我學粽子綁住自己，結果一口氣綁住了五顆下肚。」
罪惡版：傳給一起吃粽子的朋友『今天的體重，屈原要負一半責任』

en.content:
"I've decided to diet this Dragon Boat Festival."

"How?"

"I learned from the zongzi — you've gotta tie yourself down."

"So, did you tie yourself down?"

"Sure did. Tied down one... then another... then another..."
en.editorNote: The classic Dragon Boat Festival diet-fail self-roast — 'tying yourself down' sounds like discipline, but it's really just tying up more dumplings to eat. Perfect for the diet group chat after overeating on the holiday
variations: Short version: "I learned self-control from the zongzi this festival — and promptly controlled five of them into my stomach."
Guilt version: text your fellow zongzi-eaters 'Half of today's weight gain is Qu Yuan's fault.'

items.sourceNote: 雄黃酒/許仙白蛇典故幽默化；改寫為原創諧音對話

zh-tw.content:
「端午節要不要喝點雄黃酒？聽說可以避邪。」

「我不用喝。」

「為什麼？」

「因為我遇到你之後，心裡的邪念早就被你收得乾乾淨淨了。」

「……你這句話本身就很邪。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 雄黃酒避邪的節日土味情話——表面說對方淨化了自己，最後一句反轉自爆。適合傳給另一半當應景撩人簡訊
variations: 單句版：「不用雄黃酒，你就是我的避邪符。」——簡單版土味情話，不囉嗦
朋友版：傳給好友『端午記得喝雄黃酒，畢竟你身上的邪我管不了』

en.content:
"Want some realgar wine this Dragon Boat Festival? They say it wards off evil."

"I don't need any."

"Why not?"

"Because ever since I met you, you've swept every wicked thought clean out of my heart."

"...that sentence itself was pretty wicked."
en.editorNote: A festival-themed cheesy pickup line built on the realgar-wine-wards-off-evil tradition — it sounds pure until the self-incriminating last line. Send it to your partner as a seasonal flirt text
variations: Short version: "I don't need realgar wine — you're my lucky charm against evil."
Friend version: text a buddy 'Drink your realgar wine this festival — the evil on you is beyond my powers.'

items.sourceNote: 龍舟團隊合作梗；改寫為原創對話結構

zh-tw.content:
公司端午節辦龍舟賽，主管在岸邊大喊：「划！大家一起划！同舟共濟！」

結果龍舟原地打轉，一動也不動。

主管氣到不行：「你們到底在幹嘛？」

隊員回他：「我們聽你的啊，每個人都在『各划各的』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「同舟共濟」變成「各划各的」——職場版龍舟笑話，神還原團隊合作的真相。適合傳到公司群組，尤其是剛開完一場毫無共識的會議之後
variations: 單句版：「端午划龍舟才知道，原來我們團隊唯一的共識就是沒有共識。」
自嘲版：傳給同事『同舟共濟我不會，同舟翻船我很可以』

en.content:
The company held a dragon boat race for the festival. The manager bellowed from the shore: "Row! Everybody row together! We're all in the same boat!"

The boat spun in a circle and went nowhere.

The manager lost it: "What on earth are you doing?"

A crew member shouted back: "We're following you — everyone's rowing their own way."
en.editorNote: 'In the same boat' turns into 'everyone for themselves' — the office dragon-boat joke that nails what teamwork really looks like. Send it to the work group chat right after a meeting where nobody agreed on anything
variations: Short version: "Dragon boat day taught me our team's only consensus is having no consensus."
Self-roast: text a coworker 'I'm bad at us all rowing together, but capsizing together? Nailed it.'

items.sourceNote: 端午立蛋習俗幽默化；改寫為原創對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，小美蹲在桌前立蛋立了半小時，一顆都沒成功。

阿宏走過來，把蛋輕輕一放，蛋就穩穩立住了。

小美瞪大眼：「你怎麼辦到的？」

阿宏：「因為我心裡有你啊，只要想到你，我整個人就特別穩。」

小美：「……蛋倒了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋的土味情話，最後一句神反轉自打臉——情話說太滿，蛋直接倒給你看。適合端午中午傳給曖昧對象當話題開場
variations: 單句版：「端午立蛋我立不起來，但對你的心意，怎麼放都很穩。」
損友版：傳給朋友『你連蛋都立不穩，難怪感情也立不穩』

en.content:
At noon on the festival, Mei crouched over the table trying to balance an egg upright for half an hour — not one would stand.

Hong walked over, set an egg down gently, and it balanced perfectly.

Mei's eyes went wide: "How'd you do that?"

Hong: "Because I've got you on my mind. Whenever I think of you, my whole world feels steady."

Mei: "...the egg just fell over."
en.editorNote: A cheesy pickup line built on the Dragon Boat noon egg-balancing tradition, with a self-sabotaging punchline — pour the flirt on too thick and the egg topples on cue. Great as a noontime opener for someone you're into
variations: Short version: "I can't get the egg to balance this festival, but my feelings for you stand steady any way you set them."
Roast version: text a friend 'You can't even balance an egg — no wonder your love life won't stand up either.'

items.sourceNote: 香包避邪習俗幽默化；改寫為原創諧音對話

zh-tw.content:
「端午節我送你一個香包，戴著可以避邪喔。」

「謝謝！那它能避開什麼？」

「蚊子、壞運、還有……」

「還有什麼？」

「還有想跟你搶最後一顆粽子的人，包括我。」

「所以這香包是用來防你自己的？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午香包避邪的冷笑話，最後反轉成「防自己偷粽子」——溫馨中帶點賊。適合送香包或聊端午習俗時當伴手梗
variations: 單句版：「送你香包避邪，順便避開我這個會偷吃你粽子的人。」
貪吃版：傳給家人『香包我戴了，但對粽子的邪念它擋不住』

en.content:
"I got you a fragrant sachet for the festival — wear it and it wards off evil."

"Thanks! What does it ward off, exactly?"

"Mosquitoes, bad luck, and..."

"And what?"

"And anyone trying to steal the last zongzi from you. Myself included."

"So the sachet is to protect me from you?"
en.editorNote: A cold-joke built on the Dragon Boat fragrant-sachet tradition, flipping into 'protection from my own dumpling-thieving.' Sweet with a sneaky edge — perfect when gifting a sachet or chatting about festival customs
variations: Short version: "Here's a sachet to ward off evil — and to ward off me, the guy who'll steal your zongzi."
Greedy version: text family 'I'm wearing the sachet, but it can't block my evil cravings for your dumplings.'

items.sourceNote: 剝粽子過程幽默化；改寫為原創冷笑話對話

zh-tw.content:
「我跟你說，剝粽子真的是一門學問。」

「怎麼說？」

「你看，這條線到底要從哪裡開始拆？拆錯一步，整顆就散給你看。」

「那你拆對了嗎？」

「沒有，但我已經把人生的死結都練得很熟了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 剝粽子綁線的日常崩潰，升華成人生哲學自嘲——粽子拆不開，但死結倒是越打越會。適合端午吃粽子前傳到群組討拍
variations: 單句版：「我這輩子拆過最難的結，不是感情，是端午節這顆粽子的線。」
崩潰版：傳給朋友『粽子還沒吃到，我已經先輸給那條棉線了』

en.content:
"Let me tell you, unwrapping a zongzi is a real science."

"How so?"

"Look — where does this string even start? One wrong pull and the whole thing falls apart on you."

"So did you get it right?"

"Nope. But I've gotten really good at tying life's dead knots."
en.editorNote: The everyday meltdown of unwrapping a tied-up dumpling, elevated into life-philosophy self-roast — can't undo the zongzi, but great at making knots. Send it to the group chat before the festival meal for some sympathy
variations: Short version: "The hardest knot I've ever faced isn't a relationship — it's the string on this festival zongzi."
Meltdown version: text a friend 'Haven't even tasted the dumpling and I've already lost to a piece of cotton string.'

items.sourceNote: 屈原詩人身份幽默化；改寫為原創冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
老師問學生：「端午節是為了紀念誰？」

小宏：「屈原。」

老師：「很好，那你知道他是做什麼的嗎？」

小宏：「詩人啊，而且還是史上最早的『投稿』高手。」

老師：「投稿？」

小宏：「對啊，他直接把自己投進江裡，連退稿都不用等。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 屈原投江的諧音冷笑話，「投稿」一語雙關——黑色幽默但不失應景，適合在端午聊歷史典故時丟出來冷場救援
variations: 單句版：「屈原是史上最敬業的詩人，連投稿都用全身的力氣。」
文青版：傳給寫作的朋友『你退稿很難過？人家屈原投一次就上頭版兩千年』

en.content:
The teacher asked: "Who does the Dragon Boat Festival commemorate?"

"Qu Yuan," said Ming.

"Good. And do you know what he did?"

"He was a poet — and history's earliest master of the 'submission.'"

"Submission?"

"Yeah. He submitted himself straight into the river. Didn't even have to wait for a rejection letter."
en.editorNote: A dark-but-seasonal pun on Qu Yuan drowning in the river, playing on 'submission' (as in submitting a manuscript). Drop it when the festival history talk gets too solemn
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan was history's most dedicated poet — he put his whole body into every submission."
Writer version: text a writer friend 'Sad about a rejection? Qu Yuan submitted once and made the front page for two thousand years.'

items.sourceNote: 粽葉包手機延長電池的網路笑話格式；改寫為原創對話與結構

zh-tw.content:
「跟你說一個端午節省電秘訣。」
「快講。」
「把手機用粽葉包起來，丟進鍋裡煮一小時，電池續航直接翻倍。」
「真的假的？」
「真的，因為你的手機從此再也開不了機，當然超省電。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典「假教學真整人」格式，前面講得煞有其事，最後一句把人推下坑——適合在家族群組或同事群裡丟出來，看誰真的差點照做
variations: 懶人版：「端午省電法：手機包粽葉煮一小時，從此再也不用充電，因為它永遠關機了。」
接龍版：「那充電線呢？」「充電線拿去綁龍舟，划得比較快。」

en.content:
"Want a Dragon Boat Festival battery-saving hack?"
"Go on."
"Wrap your phone in bamboo leaves and boil it for an hour. Doubles your battery life."
"Seriously?"
"Seriously. Your phone will never turn on again — so the battery lasts forever."
en.editorNote: Classic fake-tutorial bait. Sounds legit until the last line drops the floor out. Drop it in the family group chat and watch who almost tries it
variations: Short version: "Festival battery hack: boil your phone in bamboo leaves for an hour. Never needs charging again — it never turns on."
Follow-up: "And the charging cable?" "Use it to tie the dragon boat. Makes it row faster."

items.sourceNote: 粽子諧音祝福語格式（粽=總）；改寫為原創土味情話

zh-tw.content:
「端午節到了，我準備了一個粽子要送你。」
「為什麼是粽子？」
「因為我想跟你說——我會粽生粽世對你好。」
「噁。那餡料是什麼？」
「我的真心，還有一點點鹹蛋黃，怕你嫌我太甜。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽生粽世」=「終生終世」的諧音土味情話，最後用鹹蛋黃自嘲化解尷尬——適合傳給另一半，土到對方腳趾摳出三房兩廳剛剛好
variations: 極簡版：「我會粽生粽世對你好，不加香菜那種。」
回擊版：對方說噁，你回「不然我換豆沙的，甜到你不敢嫌。」

en.content:
"It's the Dragon Boat Festival, so I made you a zongzi."
"Why a zongzi?"
"Because I wanted to say — I'm wrapped up in you, now and forever."
"Ugh. What's the filling?"
"My whole heart. Plus a little salted egg yolk, in case I'm too sweet for you."
en.editorNote: Cheesy festival pickup playing on a zongzi being 'wrapped up,' then defusing with the salted-egg self-burn. Text it to your partner — cheesy enough to make them curl their toes
variations: Short version: "I'm wrapped up in you now and forever — no cilantro included."
Comeback: if they say 'ugh,' reply 'Fine, I'll switch to red bean paste — too sweet to complain about.'

items.sourceNote: 「屈原不姓屈」端午冷知識；改寫為原創冷笑話對話

zh-tw.content:
「你知道嗎？屈原其實不姓屈。」
「蛤？那他姓什麼？」
「姓羋，叫羋平，屈是他的氏。」
「等等，那我們紀念他兩千年，名字都叫錯？」
「對啊，所以他在江底最大的遺憾不是沒被救上來，是沒人念對他名字。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冷知識變冷笑話，最後一句把考據轉成黑色幽默——適合在端午聚會當開場白，既能炫學問又能逗笑，順便看誰會唸「羋」
variations: 單句版：「屈原最大的遺憾不是投江，是被叫錯名字兩千年。」
教學版：傳給朋友『考你：屈原姓什麼？答屈的請去江邊罰站。』

en.content:
"Did you know Qu Yuan's surname wasn't actually Qu?"
"Huh? Then what was it?"
"It was Mi. His name was Mi Ping — Qu was just his clan name."
"Wait, so we've been calling him the wrong name for two thousand years?"
"Yep. So his real regret at the bottom of the river isn't that nobody saved him — it's that nobody got his name right."
en.editorNote: A festival fun-fact flipped into deadpan dark humor on the last line. Great opener at a Dragon Boat gathering — show off the trivia and get a laugh in one shot
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan's biggest regret isn't drowning — it's two thousand years of people mispronouncing his name."
Quiz version: text a friend 'Pop quiz: what was Qu Yuan's surname? If you said Qu, go stand by the river.'

items.sourceNote: dragon boat / rowing pun format (oar/canoe wordplay); rewritten with original setup and bilingual adaptation

zh-tw.content:
划龍舟比賽前，隊長對大家精神喊話：
「今天我們一定要划得快，划得齊，划得有默契！」
隊員舉手：「隊長，那要是輸了怎麼辦？」
隊長：「輸了也沒關係，至少我們『同舟共濟』過，輸也要輸得『槳』心比心。」
全隊翻白眼，但還是默默上了船。
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舟主題諧音梗，「槳」=「將」心比心——適合在公司運動會或團隊活動前丟出來，土歸土，但確實能炒熱划船的氣氛
variations: 簡短版：「划龍舟最重要的不是贏，是『槳』心比心，順便練手臂。」
職場版：傳給同事『這季專案我們同舟共濟，輸了大不了一起被罵，至少有伴。』

en.content:
Before the dragon boat race, the captain gave a pep talk:
"Today we row fast, we row together, we row as one!"
A rower raised a hand: "Captain, what if we lose?"
The captain said: "If we lose, that's fine — at least we'll all be in the same boat. We've just got to keep our oar-ll for one, one for oar-ll spirit."
The whole team groaned and climbed aboard anyway.
en.editorNote: A dragon-boat pun riffing on 'all for one' as 'oar for one.' Cheesy enough to groan at, but it warms up the crowd before a company race or team event
variations: Short version: "In a dragon boat, winning isn't the point — it's the oar-ll for one spirit. And the arm workout."
Office version: text a coworker 'This quarter we're all in the same boat — if we sink, at least we sink together.'

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone greeting format from 端午賀詞 compilations; rewritten as original減肥 dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「我端午節決定要減肥。」
「你不是每年都這樣說？」
「對啊，但今年不一樣。」
「哪裡不一樣？」
「今年我『粽』於下定決心了。」
「那你手上那第三顆粽子是怎麼回事？」
「……這顆是要紀念我下定決心的那一刻。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽於」=「終於」，笑點在最後自打嘴巴——適合端午連假傳到減肥失敗的好友群組，每個人都對號入座
variations: 簡短版：「端午節立志減肥的人，最後都『粽』身難忘自己吃了幾顆。」
自嘲版：發在限動配粽子照『今年「粽」算管住嘴了，從第四顆開始。』

en.content:
"I'm going on a diet this Dragon Boat Festival."
"Don't you say that every year?"
"Yeah, but this year's different."
"How so?"
"This year I've zong-clusively made up my mind."
"Then what's that third rice dumpling in your hand?"
"...This one's to commemorate the moment I decided."
en.editorNote: 'Zong-clusively' plays on 'conclusively' (zongzi/粽). The punchline is the self-sabotage on the last line — perfect for a group chat of friends whose diets never survive the festival
variations: Short version: "Everyone who vows to diet on Dragon Boat Festival ends up zong-counting exactly how many they ate."
Self-roast version: caption a dumpling photo 'This year I finally controlled my appetite — starting from dumpling number four.'

items.sourceNote: drag-gone / dragon boat pun seed; rewritten into original setup-punchline joke

zh-tw.content:
「為什麼龍舟比賽結束後，那條龍都不見了？」
「為什麼？」
「因為大家都划完就『龍』走高飛了。」
「……」
「比賽輸的那隊更慘，連龍都嫌他們慢，先跑了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「龍走高飛」=「一走了之」的諧音玩法，笑點在補刀輸家——適合龍舟賽後傳到隊伍群組，輸的人會邊罵邊笑
variations: 簡短版：「龍舟比賽最快的不是船，是輸了之後想離開的心。」
聚會版：賽後傳給隊友『今天那條龍跑得比我們快，牠是不是覺得跟錯隊了？』

en.content:
"Why does the dragon always disappear after the boat race?"
"Why?"
"Because once it's over, it's drag-gone."
"..."
"The losing team has it worse — even the dragon thought they were too slow and bailed first."
en.editorNote: Classic 'drag-gone' pun with an extra jab at the losing crew. Great to drop in a team chat right after a race — the people who lost will groan and laugh at the same time
variations: Short version: "The fastest thing at a dragon boat race isn't the boat — it's the dragon leaving after."
Gathering version: text your crew 'That dragon left faster than we rowed. Think it picked the wrong team?'

items.sourceNote: 立蛋 (egg-balancing) noon custom humor; original dialogue written around the tradition

zh-tw.content:
中午十二點，全家圍著桌子立蛋。

表弟立了二十分鐘，蛋一直倒。

他崩潰大喊：「為什麼別人三秒就立起來，我這顆就是不行？」

阿嬤淡淡地說：「你這顆是滾蛋。」

表弟：「……阿嬤你是說蛋還是說我？」

阿嬤：「你說呢。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋習俗梗，「滾蛋」一語雙關，阿嬤神回——適合中午立蛋失敗時傳到家族群組，配一張歪倒的蛋照效果加倍
variations: 簡短版：「端午立蛋立不起來的，通常是蛋有問題，偶爾是人有問題。」
自嘲版：限動配立蛋失敗照『練了一小時，我跟這顆蛋一樣，站不起來。』

en.content:
At noon, the whole family gathered to balance eggs on the table.

My cousin tried for twenty minutes. The egg kept toppling.

He lost it: "Why does everyone else do it in three seconds and mine just won't stand?"

Grandma said flatly: "That's a rotten egg."

Cousin: "...Grandma, do you mean the egg or me?"

Grandma: "You tell me."
en.editorNote: Built around the Dragon Boat noon egg-balancing custom. Grandma's deadpan 'rotten egg' works both ways — drop it in the family chat after your own egg flops, bonus points with a photo of the tipped-over egg
variations: Short version: "If your egg won't stand at noon, it's usually the egg's fault. Occasionally it's yours."
Self-roast version: caption a failed egg photo 'One hour of practice and, like this egg, I still can't stand up.'

items.sourceNote: sweet-vs-savory zongzi rivalry humor (甜鹹之爭); rewritten as original group-chat scenario

zh-tw.content:
家族群組，端午節當天。

北部親戚：「鹹粽才是正統！」
南部親戚：「甜粽才是經典！」

吵了三十則訊息後，阿公出來說話了：

「你們吵這麼兇，重點是——粽子又不會因為你們吵架就變好吃。」

全場安靜。

阿公接著說：「而且我都吃，因為兩種我都不用包。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 甜鹹粽之爭是端午經典話題，阿公一句話收尾兼凡爾賽——適合在群組戰火最旺時丟出來滅火，順便偷渡長輩智慧
variations: 簡短版：「端午節最強的不是甜粽也不是鹹粽，是那個兩種都吃還不用包的人。」
和事佬版：傳到吵架群組『甜的鹹的都別吵了，會包的最大，會吃的次之，只會吵的回家面壁。』

en.content:
Family group chat, Dragon Boat Festival day.

Northern relative: "Savory rice dumplings are the only authentic ones!"
Southern relative: "Sweet ones are the real classic!"

Thirty messages of arguing later, Grandpa finally chimes in:

"You're all fired up — but here's the thing, the dumplings don't get tastier because you two fight."

Dead silence.

Grandpa adds: "Besides, I eat both. Because I don't have to wrap either one."
en.editorNote: The sweet-vs-savory dumpling war is a classic festival debate. Grandpa's closer both ends the fight and humble-brags — drop it when the group chat is at peak war to put out the fire and sneak in some elder wisdom
variations: Short version: "The real winner of Dragon Boat Festival isn't sweet or savory dumplings — it's the guy who eats both and wraps neither."
Peacemaker version: text the warring chat 'Sweet, savory, whatever — the one who wraps them rules, the one who eats them is next, and the one who just argues goes face the wall.'

items.sourceNote: 'drag-gone boat' English pun seed; rewritten into original bilingual dialogue with new setup

zh-tw.content:
「為什麼那艘龍舟比賽當天都沒出現？」
「不知道，為什麼？」
「因為它是一艘『落跑龍舟』，划到一半就龍逃了。」

旁邊的隊友默默把槳放下：「難怪我們隊永遠最後一名，原來問題在你嘴上。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「龍逃」=「落跑」的諧音改編，適合丟到划龍舟的社團群組或公司端午活動群——隊友嫌你冷的時候，正好證明你贏了
variations: 簡短版：「划龍舟最怕的不是落水，是隊上有個只會講冷笑話的。」
自嘲版：報名龍舟賽後傳給朋友『我這組叫落跑龍舟，因為大家報名完就龍逃了，只剩我。』

en.content:
"Why didn't that dragon boat ever show up on race day?"
"No idea, why?"
"Because it was a drag-gone boat — it bailed halfway and dragon-ran off."

A teammate quietly set down his paddle: "No wonder we always finish last. Turns out the problem is your mouth."
en.editorNote: Plays on 'drag-gone' sounding like 'dragon.' Perfect for the rowing club chat or a company Dragon Boat event group — when teammates groan, that's how you know you won
variations: Short version: "The scariest thing in a dragon boat isn't falling in — it's having one guy who only tells bad puns."
Self-roast version: text a friend after signing up 'My team's called the Drag-Gone Boat, because everyone dragon-ran off after registering and it's just me now.'

items.sourceNote: viral 'Qu Yuan gave us three days off' line; rewritten as original gratitude-monologue scenario

zh-tw.content:
端午連假第一天，小明對著天空誠心誠意地說：

「屈原大大，謝謝你。
別人讀書讀到你的詩會頭痛，
但你最偉大的作品，
是這三天連假。」

他媽從廚房探出頭：「你那麼感謝他，怎麼不去把粽子拿來孝敬一下？」

小明：「我怕拿去江邊餵魚了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 改自「屈原給我們放了三天假」的網路名言，笑點在小明那句餵魚的回馬槍——適合連假當天發限動，配一張癱在沙發上的廢人照效果最好
variations: 簡短版：「屈原最偉大的作品不是離騷，是這三天連假。」
上班族版：收假前一天傳到同事群組『感謝屈原，但也想問問下一個願意跳江放假的詩人在哪。』

en.content:
First day of the Dragon Boat long weekend, Ming looks up at the sky and says, very sincerely:

"Qu Yuan, thank you.
Everyone else gets a headache reading your poems,
but your greatest work
is these three days off."

His mom pokes her head out of the kitchen: "If you're so grateful, go fetch some dumplings to honor him."

Ming: "I'm scared they'll end up feeding the fish in the river."
en.editorNote: Riffs on the viral line 'Qu Yuan gave us three days off.' The punchline lands on the fish callback (legend says people threw dumplings in the river to keep fish from eating Qu Yuan's body). Great for a holiday-morning story post with a couch-potato photo
variations: Short version: "Qu Yuan's greatest work wasn't his poetry — it was this three-day weekend."
Office version: text the work chat the night before returning 'Grateful to Qu Yuan. Also, where's the next poet willing to jump in a river for our day off?'

items.sourceNote: 'boats go with the row' English wordplay; rewritten with original team-dynamics framing

zh-tw.content:
教練問划龍舟的隊員：「為什麼我們這隊永遠這麼順？」

隊長驕傲地說：「因為我們很有默契，划得很整齊。」

教練搖頭：「不是。是因為你們從來都『隨波逐流』——我喊左你們也左，我喊右你們也右，根本不會自己想。」

隊長：「……所以這是稱讚還是罵人？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「隨波逐流」這個成語從貶義玩成龍舟梗，笑點在隊長搞不清楚是褒是貶——適合運動社團群組，自嘲團隊「很聽話」的時候用
variations: 簡短版：「龍舟隊最大的優點就是隨波逐流，缺點也是。」
職場版：開會被主管說很配合時，心裡默念『我們整組就是一艘龍舟，隨波逐流，但至少不會翻。』

en.content:
The coach asks his dragon boat crew: "Why does this team always go so smoothly?"

The captain says proudly: "Because we're in sync — we row in perfect unison."

The coach shakes his head: "Nope. It's because you always go with the row. I call left, you go left. I call right, you go right. None of you ever thinks for himself."

Captain: "...Is that a compliment or an insult?"
en.editorNote: Spins 'go with the flow' into 'go with the row,' with a captain who can't tell praise from a roast. Good for a sports club chat when you want to gently rib a team that's a little too agreeable
variations: Short version: "A dragon boat crew's best quality is going with the row. Also its worst."
Workplace version: when your boss says you're 'a great team player,' quietly think 'We're one big dragon boat — we go with the row, but at least we don't capsize.'

items.sourceNote: zongzi-wrapping struggle humor; original mother-child dialogue composed around the custom

zh-tw.content:
媽媽教我包粽子，包了一個小時。

我的粽子全部都散開，米掉滿桌。

我崩潰：「為什麼妳包得那麼快又那麼漂亮，我包的全都解體？」

媽媽淡定地說：「因為我包的是粽子，你包的是『心事』——綁不住的那種。」

我：「……媽，妳是不是平常文章看太多了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「綁不住」從包粽延伸到心事，媽媽突然文藝起來的反差是笑點——適合包粽失敗當天發到家族群組，配一張散掉的粽子照自嘲
variations: 簡短版：「包粽子最難的不是綁繩子，是綁住自己想放棄的那顆心。」
廢柴版：傳給朋友『今年我包的粽子叫開放式粽子，因為它從頭到尾都拒絕閉合。』

en.content:
Mom spent an hour teaching me to wrap zongzi.

Every single one of mine fell apart, rice all over the table.

I lost it: "Why do yours come out fast and beautiful, while mine completely disintegrate?"

Mom said calmly: "Because I'm wrapping dumplings. You're wrapping your feelings — the kind you can't tie down."

Me: "...Mom, have you been reading too much poetry?"
en.editorNote: Turns 'can't tie it down' from a wrapping fail into something unexpectedly poetic, with the punch in mom suddenly going lyrical. Perfect for the family chat after a wrapping disaster, paired with a photo of your collapsed dumpling
variations: Short version: "The hardest part of wrapping zongzi isn't the string — it's tying down the part of you that wants to quit."
Hopeless version: text a friend 'This year's batch is open-concept zongzi, because they refused to close from start to finish.'

items.sourceNote: 端午祝福語 諧音 pun format (粽/中); rewritten into an original greeting-card gag

zh-tw.content:
朋友傳來一張端午賀卡，上面寫著：

「祝你端午節，事事粽如意，好運粽是來，包你粽中獎。」

我回他：「謝謝，但你這張卡片用了三個粽，是想暗示我該減肥了嗎？」

他：「不是，我是想暗示你欠我三顆粽子。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽如意／粽是來／粽中獎」一次塞三個諧音梗，反轉在朋友討債的回覆——適合端午當天回傳給傳罐頭祝福的朋友，幽默回敬不失禮
variations: 簡短版：「端午節快樂，祝你粽是被好運包圍，但別被粽子包圍。」
討債版：傳給欠你東西的朋友『端午快樂，祝你粽如意，順便還我上次那頓飯。』

en.content:
A friend sends me a Dragon Boat greeting card that reads:

"Wishing you a festival where everything goes your way, good luck zong-stantly rolls in, and you're zong to win big."

I text back: "Thanks, but you crammed three 'zong' puns in here — are you hinting I should go on a diet?"

Him: "No, I'm hinting you owe me three rice dumplings."
en.editorNote: Stacks three 'zong'/'always' puns into one greeting, with the twist landing on the friend collecting a debt ('zong' sounds like 總/'always'). A fun comeback to send the friend who blasted you a copy-paste holiday greeting
variations: Short version: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may you be zong-stantly surrounded by good luck, just not by dumplings."
Debt-collector version: text a friend who owes you 'Happy festival, may everything go your way — and also pay me back for that meal.'

items.sourceNote: drag-gone boat / oar pun format from English dragon-boat joke lists; rewritten into an original dialogue gag

zh-tw.content:
比賽前，隊長替大家打氣：「今天我們一定要划得比所有人都快！」

隊員小陳舉手：「隊長，那如果我們划輸了怎麼辦？」

隊長：「那這艘就不叫龍舟了。」

小陳：「那叫什麼？」

隊長：「龍丟臉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「龍丟臉」諧音「龍舟」，自嘲式團隊冷笑話——適合公司龍舟隊或社團出賽前傳到群組，輸贏都能笑，氣氛瞬間放鬆
variations: 簡短版：「我們這隊不是來划龍舟的，是來划龍丟臉的。」
認輸版：傳到群組『沒關係，輸了我們就改名龍舟隊為龍走隊，下次用走的。』

en.content:
Before the race, the captain rallies the team: "Today we paddle faster than everyone out there!"

A rower raises his hand: "Captain, what if we lose?"

Captain: "Then this thing isn't a dragon boat anymore."

Rower: "Then what is it?"

Captain: "A drag-gone boat."
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating team gag built on the 'drag-gone' pun. Drop it in the group chat before a company or club dragon-boat race — works whether you win or lose, and instantly loosens the nerves
variations: Short version: "We're not here to row a dragon boat — we're here to row a drag-gone boat."
Sore-loser version: text the team 'No worries, if we lose we'll just rename it the drag-gone boat and walk next time.'

items.sourceNote: 小明 zongzi-shape classroom joke format from 端午笑話 compilations; rewritten with an original punchline

zh-tw.content:
老師問：「端午節為什麼要包粽子？」

小明：「因為要丟到河裡給魚吃，魚才不會去吃屈原。」

老師：「很好，那你知道為什麼粽子是三角形嗎？」

小明：「因為人生已經夠方了，粽子就讓它有點稜角吧。」

老師：「你坐下，你想太多了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把小學生神回覆跟人生哲理結合，老師最後一句神吐槽——適合分享到有當老師或家長的群組，孩子的腦洞永遠比課本精彩
variations: 厭世版：「粽子是三角形，因為它知道圓融在這個社會活不下去。」
簡短版：「為什麼粽子是三角形？因為人生已經夠方了。」

en.content:
Teacher: "Why do we wrap zongzi for the Dragon Boat Festival?"

Kid: "So we can throw them in the river for the fish to eat, so the fish leave Qu Yuan alone."

Teacher: "Good. And do you know why zongzi are triangular?"

Kid: "Because life already has enough sharp corners — might as well let the dumpling have a few too."

Teacher: "Sit down, you're overthinking it."
en.editorNote: A kid's deadpan answer collides with accidental life philosophy, capped by the teacher's flat retort. Great for the group chat with teacher or parent friends — kids' logic always beats the textbook
variations: Jaded version: "Zongzi are triangular because they know being well-rounded gets you nowhere in this economy."
Short version: "Why are zongzi triangular? Because life already has enough sharp corners."

items.sourceNote: Qu Yuan loyalty/poet legend; rewritten into an original modern-day workplace gag

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「端午節到底在紀念什麼？」

我說：「紀念一個很會寫文章、很忠心、但提的建議都不被採用的人。」

同事愣了一下：「這聽起來怎麼有點像在說我？」

我：「對啊，所以這個節日放假，是要讓你好好休息，別跳河。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把屈原的處境投射到現代上班族身上，溫柔又厭世的安慰——適合端午連假前傳給提案總是被打槍的同事，笑中帶一點同病相憐的溫度
variations: 簡短版：「端午節放假，是國家叫你別跳河，好好休息。」
鼓勵版：傳給加班的朋友『屈原寫得再好建議也沒人聽，但你今天還是要記得下班。』

en.content:
A coworker asks me: "What are we actually commemorating on the Dragon Boat Festival?"

I say: "A guy who wrote beautifully, stayed loyal, and watched every suggestion he made get ignored."

They pause: "Why does that sound a lot like me?"

Me: "Exactly. So the holiday off is the country telling you to rest — and please don't jump in the river."
en.editorNote: Projects Qu Yuan's plight onto the modern office worker — a gentle, world-weary consolation. Perfect to send a coworker whose proposals keep getting shot down, right before the long holiday weekend
variations: Short version: "The Dragon Boat holiday is the country telling you to rest and not jump in any rivers."
Pep-talk version: text an overworked friend 'Qu Yuan wrote brilliantly and no one listened either, but you still need to clock out today.'

items.sourceNote: 粽 homophone love-confession format from 端午祝福語 compilations; rewritten into an original cheesy exchange

zh-tw.content:
「我覺得你跟粽子很像。」
「為什麼？是因為很甜嗎？」
「不是。」
「那是因為很有料？」
「也不是。是因為我想把你綁緊緊，一輩子都不放開。」

對方：「你這樣講很噁，但我居然有點心動。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用粽子「綁緊緊」的特性比喻想長相廝守，土到極致反而甜——適合傳給穩定交往的對象，端午當天加一句『記得吃粽子，但別吃太多』更貼心
variations: 簡短版：「你是我的粽子，我想把你綁在身邊一輩子。」
反擊版：對方說很噁時回『噁的是粽葉，甜的是裡面的你。』

en.content:
"You know, you remind me of a zongzi."
"Why, because I'm sweet?"
"Nope."
"Because I'm full of good stuff?"
"Also no. It's because I want to wrap you up tight and never let go."

Them: "That was disgusting, and somehow I'm into it."
en.editorNote: Uses the way a zongzi is tied up tight as a metaphor for wanting to stay together forever — so cheesy it loops back around to sweet. Best for a steady partner; tack on 'eat a dumpling, but not too many' for bonus points
variations: Short version: "You're my zongzi — I want to keep you tied close for life."
Comeback version: when they say it's gross, reply 'The leaf is gross, the sweet part is you inside.'

items.sourceNote: 粽子幾何形狀（正四面體/四個三角面）數學趣味梗；改寫為原創課堂師生對話

zh-tw.content:
數學老師：「誰能說出一個生活中的正四面體？」

全班安靜。

小傑舉手：「粽子！」

老師眼睛一亮：「沒錯！肉粽就是正四面體，四個面都是三角形，古人數學超強。」

小傑：「老師，那為什麼我阿嬤包的每顆形狀都不一樣？」

老師：「……那是行為藝術。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「肉粽是正四面體」這個冷知識變成師生吐槽——適合傳到家族群組或當作端午冷知識開場，阿嬤聽到「行為藝術」通常會笑著回你一句
variations: 短句版：「端午冷知識：標準肉粽是正四面體，四個面都是三角形。我阿嬤包的則是抽象派。」
教師版：傳給數學老師朋友『端午節唯一一次學生能秒答正四面體的機會，請好好把握。』

en.content:
Math teacher: "Can anyone name a tetrahedron you'd find in real life?"

Dead silence.

Little Jay raises his hand: "A zongzi!"

Teacher beams: "Exactly! A rice dumpling is a perfect tetrahedron — four triangular faces. The ancients were great at geometry."

Little Jay: "Then why is every one my grandma wraps a different shape?"

Teacher: "...That's performance art."
en.editorNote: Turns the fun fact that a zongzi is a tetrahedron into a classroom roast. Great as a Dragon Boat Festival 'did you know' opener, or to send a teacher friend
variations: Short version: "Festival fact: a proper rice dumpling is a tetrahedron with four triangular faces. Grandma's, however, are abstract art."
Teacher edition: 'The one day a year a student can instantly name a tetrahedron — cherish it.'

items.sourceNote: 端午安康 vs 端午快樂祝福爭議網路梗；改寫為原創群組對話

zh-tw.content:
家族群組裡，表哥傳：「祝大家端午節快樂！」

馬上有長輩跳出來：「端午節要說『安康』，不能說『快樂』，這是祭祀的日子。」

表哥：「喔好，那祝大家端午節安康。」

長輩：「這就對了。」

三秒後，表哥又傳：「那請問連假玩三天，要說『安康』還是『快樂』？」

群組安靜了。

阿嬤：「玩的時候快樂，記得回來吃粽就好。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把每年都會吵的「端午安康 vs 快樂」做成群組情境，最後讓阿嬤一句話收尾——適合端午前丟到家族群組，化解長輩糾正氣氛，誰都不得罪
variations: 短句版：「端午到底要說安康還是快樂？阿嬤說：玩得快樂，平安回家就好。」
職場版：傳到公司群組『祝各位端午安康，連假快樂，收假冷靜。』

en.content:
In the family group chat, Cousin posts: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival, everyone!"

An elder jumps in immediately: "You're supposed to say 'peace and health,' not 'happy' — it's a day of remembrance."

Cousin: "Oh, okay. Wishing everyone peace and health."

Elder: "That's better."

Three seconds later, Cousin: "So for the three-day break, do I say 'peace' or 'happy'?"

The chat goes quiet.

Grandma: "Be happy while you're out, just come home for the dumplings."
en.editorNote: Bottles the annual 'peace and health vs. happy' etiquette debate into a chat scene, with Grandma landing the wholesome closer. Drop it in the family chat to defuse the corrections without offending anyone
variations: Short version: "Peace or happy this festival? Grandma says: be happy out there, just get home safe for the dumplings."
Office edition: 'Wishing you peace and health, a happy long weekend, and a calm return to work.'

items.sourceNote: 粽子綁繩「解套」諧音 + 股市梗；改寫為原創餐桌對話

zh-tw.content:
爸爸一邊解開粽子的繩子一邊嘆氣：「唉，要是我的股票也能這樣解套就好了。」

兒子：「爸，這叫『解粽』，不是『解套』。」

爸爸：「對我來說都一樣，反正都是被綁住的東西。」

兒子：「那你套牢多久了？」

爸爸：「比這顆粽子放在冷凍庫的時間還久。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「解粽」諧音「解套」，把股市套牢的無奈包進端午餐桌——適合傳給有在玩股票的朋友或家人，苦中作樂，懂的人會苦笑
variations: 短句版：「端午心願：粽子解粽，股票解套。」
安慰版：傳給被套牢的朋友『至少粽子吃完就沒了，你的股票還有機會回來。』

en.content:
Dad sighs as he unties the string on his zongzi: "If only I could unwrap my stocks this easily."

Son: "Dad, this is called 'unwrapping a dumpling,' not 'unwinding a position.'"

Dad: "Same thing to me — both are tied up tight."

Son: "How long have you been stuck holding them?"

Dad: "Longer than this dumpling sat in the freezer."
en.editorNote: Plays on how a zongzi is bound tight, the way an underwater stock position keeps you 'tied up.' Send it to friends who dabble in stocks — gallows humor they'll wince-laugh at
variations: Short version: "Festival wish: may the dumplings come unwrapped and the stocks come unstuck."
Consolation edition: 'At least the dumpling's gone once you eat it — your stocks still have a shot at coming back.'

items.sourceNote: 端午立蛋失敗 + 自我安慰幽默；改寫為原創親子對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，小芸蹲在地上立蛋，立了半小時都倒。

媽媽：「聽說正午陽氣最強，比較好立。」

小芸：「媽，現在就是正午，可是它一直倒。」

媽媽：「那可能是這顆蛋陽氣不夠。」

小芸：「……或者是我手抖。」

媽媽：「不，是蛋的問題。我們家不背鍋。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋失敗的日常，媽媽硬要甩鍋給蛋——適合端午當天傳到群組配上自己立蛋失敗的照片，自嘲又應景
variations: 短句版：「端午立蛋第三十次失敗，結論：不是我的問題，是蛋陽氣不足。」
勝利版：立成功時傳『端午正午立蛋成功，今年運勢穩了，蛋說的。』

en.content:
Noon on the festival, and Yun has been crouched on the floor trying to stand an egg upright for half an hour. It keeps tipping over.

Mom: "They say the energy peaks at noon, so it's easier then."

Yun: "Mom, it IS noon, and it keeps falling."

Mom: "Then maybe this egg just has weak energy."

Yun: "...Or my hand is shaky."

Mom: "No, it's the egg's fault. This family doesn't take the blame."
en.editorNote: The very relatable festival egg-balancing fail, with Mom firmly pinning it on the egg. Post it with a photo of your own toppled egg for a self-deprecating, on-theme laugh
variations: Short version: "Thirty-first failed attempt to balance the egg. Conclusion: not my fault, the egg just has weak energy."
Victory edition: when it finally stands, post 'Balanced the egg at high noon — good luck locked in for the year. The egg said so.'

items.sourceNote: 龍舟划槳 oar-some 諧音梗；改寫為原創隊友對話

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽結束，隊長癱在船上：「我們今天划得怎麼樣？」

隊友：「划得很『槳』勵人心。」

隊長：「……我們是最後一名。」

隊友：「對，但我們是最後一名裡面最快樂的。」

隊長：「你這句話我可以印在獎牌上嗎？我們又沒得獎牌。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「槳」諧音「獎」，把龍舟划輸的尷尬轉成阿Q精神——適合運動會、龍舟隊或任何輸得很開心的場合，自嘲用最對味
variations: 短句版：「划龍舟最重要的不是名次，是有沒有人幫你『槳』勵。」
勝利版：贏的時候傳『今天這槳，名副其實，有划有獎。』

en.content:
The dragon boat race is over, and the captain collapses on the deck: "So how'd we paddle out there today?"

Teammate: "Honestly? Oar-inspiring."

Captain: "...We came in last."

Teammate: "Right, but we were the happiest team in last place."

Captain: "Can I get that engraved on a medal? Oh wait, we didn't win one."
en.editorNote: Leans on the classic 'oar-some / oar-inspiring' boat pun to spin a last-place finish into pure good vibes. Perfect for any team that loses cheerfully — race day, sports day, you name it
variations: Short version: "In dragon boat racing, it's not the ranking that counts — it's whether someone's there to oar you on."
Victory edition: when you actually win, post 'Every paddle paid off — oar-some effort, real reward.'

items.sourceNote: 粽如泰山/粽口同聲 成語諧音祝福格式；改寫為原創群組吐槽對話

zh-tw.content:
公司群組裡，主管傳了一長串：「祝大家端午粽如泰山、粽口同聲、粽橫四海、粽生有幸！」

小李：「主管，最後一句是不是怪怪的？」

主管：「哪裡怪？」

小李：「『粽生有幸』聽起來像是我這輩子只剩吃粽子這件值得慶幸的事。」

主管：「……那就改成『粽身難忘』。」

小李：「這個更慘。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把端午常見的「粽」字成語祝福玩到崩壞，最後越改越慘——適合在收到長輩或主管轟炸式祝福時，回傳這則自嘲一下，氣氛瞬間輕鬆
variations: 短句版：「端午祝你粽如泰山，但別粽生只剩吃粽。」
認真版：想正常祝福就傳『端午安康，粽口同聲祝你順心。』

en.content:
In the company chat, the boss drops a long string: "Wishing everyone a festival as steady as a mountain, in perfect harmony, sweeping across the seas, and dumpling-blessed for life!"

Lee: "Boss, isn't that last one a little... off?"

Boss: "What's off about it?"

Lee: "'Dumpling-blessed for life' makes it sound like eating zongzi is the only thing I've got going for me."

Boss: "...Fine, let's change it to 'unforgettable dumpling.'"

Lee: "That's even worse."
en.editorNote: Pokes fun at those copy-paste 'zong'-themed festival blessings (in Chinese, 粽/zong gets swapped into idioms) by letting the rewrites spiral downhill. Great comeback when an elder or boss carpet-bombs the chat with greetings
variations: Short version: "May your festival be steady as a mountain — but may dumplings not be the only blessing you've got."
Sincere version: if you just want a normal greeting, send 'Peace and health this festival, and smooth sailing ahead.'

items.sourceNote: 連假後吃太多粽子 + 體重幽默；改寫為原創收假對話

zh-tw.content:
端午連假結束，同事們收假第一天。

小美：「你連假過得怎麼樣？」

阿傑：「我跟粽子培養了很深的感情。」

小美：「多深？」

阿傑：「深到它們現在都住在我肚子上，而且看起來不打算搬走。」

小美：「那叫脂肪，不叫感情。」

阿傑：「在我心裡，它們是同一件事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 收假日專用，把連假吃太多粽子的後果包成「感情」——適合端午連假後傳到公司群組，大家一起認領自己長出來的那幾公斤
variations: 短句版：「端午連假結束，我跟粽子的感情，全寫在體重計上。」
決心版：傳給健身的朋友『粽子住進來了，這個月開始勸它們搬家。』

en.content:
The long weekend is over, and it's everyone's first day back.

Mei: "So how was the break?"

Jay: "I formed a deep emotional bond with zongzi."

Mei: "How deep?"

Jay: "Deep enough that they now live on my stomach and show no intention of leaving."

Mei: "That's not a bond, that's fat."

Jay: "In my heart, they're the same thing."
en.editorNote: Built for the back-to-work day, reframing a weekend of over-eating dumplings as a 'deep emotional bond.' Drop it in the office chat after the long weekend and let everyone claim the kilos they grew
variations: Short version: "The long weekend's over, and my bond with the dumplings is written all over the scale."
Resolution version: for the gym friends — 'The dumplings have moved in. This month I start gently asking them to leave.'

items.sourceNote: 龍吃粽子 'dragon' dumpling 英文諧音梗；改寫為原創親子問答

zh-tw.content:
小朋友問：「媽媽，端午節的龍會吃什麼？」

媽媽：「當然是吃粽子啊。」

小朋友：「為什麼龍要吃粽子？」

媽媽：「因為它划龍舟划了一整天，肚子很餓。」

小朋友：「那它怎麼解開粽子的繩子？它沒有手。」

媽媽：「……所以你看，當人類比較方便，要好好讀書。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 親子版冷笑話，從天真童言問到媽媽硬轉成教育——適合帶小孩的家長傳到媽媽群組，最後那句神轉折最戳中爸媽的職業病
variations: 短句版：「龍舟划一整天，連龍都餓到想吃粽，何況是你。」
純童趣版：「端午節的龍最愛吃粽子，因為那是『龍』舟節限定大餐。」

en.content:
Kid: "Mommy, what does the dragon eat on Dragon Boat Festival?"

Mom: "Zongzi, of course."

Kid: "Why does the dragon eat zongzi?"

Mom: "Because it rowed the dragon boat all day and it's starving."

Kid: "But how does it untie the string? It doesn't have hands."

Mom: "...See? This is why it's handy to be human. Now go study hard."
en.editorNote: A kid-friendly groaner that drifts from innocent question into a sneaky parenting moment. Made for parents to share in the mom chat — that last pivot nails the occupational reflex every mom and dad has
variations: Short version: "Row a dragon boat all day and even a dragon gets hungry enough for dumplings — so do you."
Pure-fun version: "The festival dragon loves zongzi — it's the 'dragon' boat feast, after all."

items.sourceNote: 'oar-some' / 'too row-mantic' 划船英文諧音梗；改寫為原創龍舟隊對話

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽前，隊長對全隊精神喊話：「我們這隊最大的優點是什麼？」

隊員：「團結？」

隊長：「不對。」

隊員：「速度？」

隊長：「也不對。是我們每個人都很『槳』。」

隊員：「……所以我們是要划船還是要講冷笑話？」

隊長：「成熟的隊伍，兩個都要。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「很槳」諧音「很棒」，把龍舟的槳玩成稱讚——適合公司端午龍舟隊出賽前傳到群組暖場，把緊張氣氛先用冷笑話劃開
variations: 短句版：「我們這隊不一定划得最快，但絕對是最『槳』的。」
回嗆版：傳給隊友『再講這種冷笑話，我下槳就划錯邊。』

en.content:
Before the race, the captain rallies the team: "What's our biggest strength?"

Teammate: "Teamwork?"

Captain: "Nope."

Teammate: "Speed?"

Captain: "Also no. It's that every one of us is absolutely oar-some."

Teammate: "...Are we racing or doing stand-up?"

Captain: "A mature team does both."
en.editorNote: Built on the classic 'oar-some' (awesome) paddle pun — perfect for a corporate dragon boat team to drop in the group chat before the race, slicing through the pre-race nerves with a groaner
variations: Short version: "We might not be the fastest crew, but we're definitely the oar-some-est."
Comeback version: for a teammate — 'One more pun like that and I'm paddling on the wrong side.'

items.sourceNote: 端午節祝福語諧音哏（粽/中）格式；改寫為原創職場對話

zh-tw.content:
端午節前，老闆發了一封全公司信：「祝大家端午佳節，粽得人心、好事粽來。」

小李回信：「謝謝老闆，那獎金也會粽來嗎？」

老闆回：「會，粽有一天。」

小李：「……是哪一天？」

老闆：「粽之就不是今天。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「粽得人心」=「眾得人心」、「粽來」=「總來」、「粽之」=「總之」——一連串諧音梗，最後老闆用「粽之不是今天」躲掉獎金，職場日常神還原
variations: 短句版：「老闆祝我們好事粽來，我只想問獎金粽來沒。」
樂觀版：傳給同事『獎金粽有一天會來，我們先把粽子吃完等它。』

en.content:
Before the festival, the boss emails the whole company: "Happy Dragon Boat Festival — may good fortune zong-tinue to roll your way!"

Li replies: "Thanks, boss. Will the bonus zong-tinue to roll our way too?"

Boss: "It will. Zong day."

Li: "...Which day exactly?"

Boss: "Zong-clusively, not today."
en.editorNote: A chain of zong-puns ('zong-tinue/continue', 'zong day/some day', 'zong-clusively/conclusively') where the boss dodges the bonus question with the last one — peak relatable office energy
variations: Short version: "Boss wished us good fortune zong-tinuing our way. I just want to know about the bonus."
Optimistic version: for a coworker — 'The bonus will roll in zong day. Let's finish the dumplings while we wait.'

items.sourceNote: 'to get to the other tide' 過河英文諧音梗；改寫為原創自嘲笑話

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我：「龍舟為什麼要過河？」

我說：「為了到對岸啊。」

朋友：「錯，是為了追上『時』間的潮流。」

我：「這梗也太爛了吧。」

朋友：「爛歸爛，但你還是笑了，這就是我划龍舟的速度——慢，但你躲不掉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 改編英文「to get to the other tide」諧音梗，中文版用「潮流」承接水的意象，重點在朋友那句「慢但你躲不掉」的自嘲收尾——適合傳給愛講冷笑話又不要臉的那種朋友
variations: 短句版：「龍舟過河不為對岸，是為了追上潮流——順便淹死你的耐心。」
互嗆版：『你的笑話跟龍舟一樣，看起來會動，其實原地打轉。』

en.content:
A friend asks me: "Why did the dragon boat cross the river?"

Me: "To get to the other side?"

Friend: "Wrong. To get to the other tide."

Me: "That is genuinely terrible."

Friend: "Terrible, sure. But you still laughed. That's my paddling speed — slow, but you can't outrun it."
en.editorNote: A riff on the 'other tide' (other side) water pun, landing on the friend's self-deprecating 'slow but you can't outrun it.' Made for that one friend who tells awful puns and refuses to be ashamed
variations: Short version: "The dragon boat didn't cross for the other side — it crossed for the other tide, and to drown your patience."
Roast version: 'Your jokes are like a dragon boat — they look like they're moving, but really they're just spinning in place.'

items.sourceNote: 端午安康 vs 端午快樂 爭議哏；改寫為原創家族群組對話

zh-tw.content:
家族群組裡，二叔貼文：「端午節要說『安康』不能說『快樂』，這是禮數！」

小姪女問：「為什麼？」

二叔：「因為端午是紀念屈原投江，是肅穆的日子。」

小姪女：「那我們吃粽子吃到肚子很快樂，這樣可以嗎？」

二叔沉默了五秒。

二叔：「……肚子可以快樂，嘴巴要說安康。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「端午安康 vs 快樂」的網路爭議，用小姪女天真一問化解——二叔最後那句「肚子可以快樂，嘴巴要說安康」神級折衷，適合傳到有長輩堅持禮數的家族群組
variations: 短句版：「端午嘴巴說安康，肚子偷偷快樂，兩全其美。」
和稀泥版：傳給長輩『安康也好快樂也好，您開心最重要。』

en.content:
In the family chat, Uncle posts: "On Dragon Boat Festival you say 'stay well,' NOT 'happy holiday.' It's proper etiquette!"

Little niece: "Why?"

Uncle: "Because it commemorates Qu Yuan throwing himself into the river. It's a solemn day."

Niece: "So if I eat so much zongzi my tummy is happy, is that allowed?"

Five seconds of silence.

Uncle: "...Your tummy may be happy. Your mouth says 'stay well.'"
en.editorNote: Defuses the perennial 'stay well vs. happy festival' etiquette debate with a kid's innocent question — Uncle's 'tummy happy, mouth proper' compromise is gold. Perfect for the family chat with an etiquette-stickler elder
variations: Short version: "On the festival, the mouth says 'stay well' while the tummy stays quietly happy. Best of both."
Peacemaker version: for an elder — 'Stay well or happy, whichever — the important thing is that you're glad.'

items.sourceNote: 'oar-some' oar pun format from English dragon boat joke lists; rewritten as original couple dialogue with matching Chinese pun

zh-tw.content:
「你划龍舟最想要什麼隊友？」

「當然是你啊。」

「為什麼？」

「因為有你在船上，這趟就『槳』勵到我了。」

「……你這梗跟我們的船一樣，原地打轉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「槳」諧「獎」，把划龍舟的槳玩成「獎勵」——適合傳給會一起報名活動的另一半或死黨，尷尬中帶點甜，對方吐槽你才是真的有在聽
variations: 單句版：「跟你同一條船，再爛的槳我都划得動。」
自嘲版：『我划龍舟不快，但我保證每一槳都在想你——所以才這麼慢。』

en.content:
