"If you were on a dragon boat, who would you want paddling next to you?"

"You, obviously."

"Why?"

"Because with you here, every stroke feels oar-some."

"...That pun is like our boat — going in circles."
en.editorNote: Classic 'oar-some' (awesome) paddle pun landing on a self-aware roast. Great for a partner or friend you'd actually sign up for a race with — cringe with a side of sweet
variations: Short version: "Same boat as you? I'd row through anything — oar-some or not."
Self-roast version: 'I'm not a fast paddler, but every stroke is me thinking of you — which is exactly why I'm so slow.'

items.sourceNote: North vs. south dragon boat 'rescue mission' humor circulating online; rewritten as original observational bit

zh-tw.content:
有人研究划龍舟的南北差異，得出結論：

南部隊伍——動作整齊、速度飛快，全程像一場精密的救援任務。

北部隊伍——划到一半有人掉水裡，全隊停下來撈人。

結論：南部是真的想救屈原，北部是划一划就下去陪屈原了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟競賽包裝成「救援屈原」的老梗玩到底，南北對比的荒謬感很好笑——適合節日發到地方梗很多的群組，記得別真的戰南北，笑笑就好
variations: 短句版：「南部隊划龍舟是去救屈原，北部隊是划到一半自己也想跳下去。」
自黑版：傳給自己那隊『我們這隊划龍舟，屈原看了都想再投一次江。』

en.content:
Someone studied the difference between northern and southern dragon boat crews and concluded:

Southern crews — perfectly synced, blazing fast, the whole thing looks like a precision rescue mission.

Northern crews — someone falls in halfway, and the whole boat stops to fish them out.

Verdict: the south is genuinely trying to rescue Qu Yuan. The north just keeps ending up in the river to keep him company.
en.editorNote: Runs the old 'we're rescuing Qu Yuan' gag through a regional-crew comparison — the absurd contrast is the payoff. Fun for a group chat that loves local ribbing; keep it light, no real rivalry
variations: Short version: "The southern crew paddles to rescue Qu Yuan; the northern crew paddles until they want to jump in too."
Self-roast version: for your own team — 'The way our crew paddles, Qu Yuan is thinking about jumping in a second time.'

items.sourceNote: 'troubles peeled away like bamboo leaves, happiness sticks like glutinous rice' well-wish imagery; rewritten as original blessing with a twist ending

zh-tw.content:
端午祝福升級版：

「願你的煩惱像粽葉一樣，一層一層被剝掉；願你的幸福像糯米一樣，黏得牢牢的。」

朋友回：「那我的體重呢？」

「……體重也像糯米，黏得牢牢的，跟幸福同進退。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「煩惱像粽葉剝掉、幸福像糯米黏牢」的正經祝福，用一句「那我體重呢」瞬間拉回現實——適合傳給端午一定會吃胖的朋友，祝福加吐槽剛剛好
variations: 純祝福版：「願煩惱層層剝落，幸福顆顆黏牢，端午安康。」
互嗆版：『幸福黏得牢，體重也黏得牢——恭喜你端午收穫滿滿。』

en.content:
Upgraded festival wish:

"May your worries peel away layer by layer like bamboo leaves, and may happiness stick to you like glutinous rice."

Friend replies: "And my body weight?"

"...Also like glutinous rice. Sticks around. Comes and goes with the happiness."
en.editorNote: Takes the earnest 'worries peel away, happiness sticks' blessing and yanks it back to reality with 'and my weight?' — perfect for the friend who always gains a few pounds over the festival. Warm wish plus a gentle jab
variations: Pure-wish version: "May worries peel away layer by layer and happiness stick to you tight — stay well this festival."
Ribbing version: 'Happiness sticks, and so does the weight — congrats on a very full festival.'

items.sourceNote: viral ultrasound-zongzi meme concept; rewritten as original hospital dialogue, no image reference

zh-tw.content:
孕婦去照超音波，護士盯著螢幕看了很久。

護士：「恭喜，是個……健康的粽子。」

孕婦：「什麼？！」

護士：「開玩笑的，是端午節前後懷的吧？形狀有點應景，包得很緊實。」

孕婦：「……我要換一家醫院。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 改編網路瘋傳的「超音波照出粽子」惡搞哏，用護士一本正經的冷笑話收尾——適合傳給準爸媽或愛吃粽的朋友，無厘頭到讓人白眼但還是笑出來
variations: 短句版：「端午前後懷的寶寶，超音波看起來特別像顆包得很緊的粽子。」
接龍版：『那出生要不要沾醬？』——傳給敢接爛梗的朋友

en.content:
A pregnant woman goes in for an ultrasound. The nurse stares at the screen for a long time.

Nurse: "Congratulations. It's a... healthy zongzi."

Woman: "WHAT?!"

Nurse: "Kidding. Conceived around Dragon Boat Festival, right? The shape's just very seasonal. Wrapped nice and tight."

Woman: "...I'm switching hospitals."
en.editorNote: Riffs on the viral 'ultrasound reveals a zongzi' meme, delivered deadpan by the nurse. Good for expecting parents or a friend who lives for zongzi — absurd enough to earn an eye-roll and a laugh
variations: Short version: "Conceived around the festival — no wonder the ultrasound looks like a tightly wrapped zongzi."
Follow-up version: 'So at birth, dipping sauce or no dipping sauce?' — for a friend who can take a bad pun and raise you one

items.sourceNote: 南部粽 vs 北部粽 甜鹹之爭 common festival banter theme; rewritten as original office dialogue

zh-tw.content:
辦公室的南北粽大戰又開打了。

北部同事：「北部粽用炒的，粒粒分明，這才叫講究。」
南部同事：「南部粽用水煮的，軟糯入味，這才叫古早味。」

兩個人吵到臉紅脖子粗，最後轉頭問旁邊默默吃便當的小陳：「你支持哪一派？」

小陳：「我支持……老闆請客那一派。」

全辦公室瞬間安靜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 南北粽之爭是每年端午必吵的經典話題，用小陳「老闆請客那派」一句把政治正確發揮到極致——適合傳到公司群組，尤其在有人想揪團訂粽子的時候丟出來
variations: 簡短版：「南部粽北部粽我都吃，只要是別人出錢的都好吃。」
職場版：『粽子哪一派我沒意見，加班費那一派我很堅定。』——傳給同溫層同事保證秒懂

en.content:
The office north-vs-south zongzi war has flared up again.

Northern coworker: "Northern zongzi is stir-fried — every grain separate. That's craftsmanship."
Southern coworker: "Southern zongzi is boiled — soft, sticky, flavor all the way through. That's tradition."

They argue until they're red in the face, then both turn to Chen, who's quietly eating his lunchbox: "So which side are you on?"

Chen: "I'm on... the side that gets treated by the boss."

The whole office goes silent.
en.editorNote: The north-vs-south zongzi feud is a yearly Dragon Boat Festival ritual in Taiwan. Chen's 'whichever side the boss pays for' answer is peak diplomacy — great for the work group chat, especially when someone's organizing a group order
variations: Short version: "North or south, I'll eat any zongzi — as long as someone else is paying, it tastes great."
Office version: 'No opinion on which zongzi wins, but I'm very firm on the overtime-pay side.'

items.sourceNote: 立蛋 (standing an egg upright at noon) festival custom; rewritten as original comedic scene

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，全家在客廳圍著一顆蛋，準備見證立蛋奇蹟。

爸爸試了半小時，蛋一直倒。
媽媽試了半小時，蛋還是倒。

最後五歲的兒子走過來，把蛋往桌上輕輕一放——立起來了。

全家歡呼：「你怎麼辦到的？！」

兒子：「我剛剛把它煮熟了。」

爸爸：「……那是要立生蛋。」
兒子：「你們又沒說。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 端午立蛋是台灣經典習俗，用小孩把蛋煮熟的無厘頭邏輯破解「奇蹟」——適合傳給有小孩的家庭群組，或每年立蛋都失敗的朋友
variations: 短句版：「立蛋失敗沒關係，煮熟了它自己會站。」
厭世版：『我連蛋都立不起來，還立什麼志。』——傳給端午還在加班的朋友

en.content:
Noon on Dragon Boat Festival. The whole family gathers around a single egg in the living room, ready to witness the standing-egg miracle.

Dad tries for half an hour — the egg keeps toppling.
Mom tries for half an hour — still toppling.

Then the five-year-old walks over, sets the egg gently on the table, and — it stands.

Everyone cheers: "How did you do that?!"

Kid: "I hard-boiled it first."

Dad: "...You're supposed to stand a RAW egg."
Kid: "Nobody told me."
en.editorNote: Standing an egg upright at noon is a classic Taiwanese festival custom. The kid's cheat — boiling it first — is flawless toddler logic. Good for family group chats or the friend whose egg never stands
variations: Short version: "Egg won't stand? Just boil it — problem solved."
Deadpan version: 'I can't even make an egg stand, so let's not talk about my life goals.'

items.sourceNote: 龍舟隊合作 teamwork gag; rewritten as original dragon boat race dialogue

zh-tw.content:
龍舟比賽前，隊長對全隊精神喊話：

「我們這隊最大的優勢是什麼？」

隊員甲：「團結！」
隊員乙：「默契！」
隊員丙：「體力！」

隊長：「都不對。我們最大的優勢是——對面那隊昨天全體吃了太多粽子。」

結果比賽開始，我方划到一半也集體肚子痛。

隊長：「……原來我們昨天也有吃。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟比賽的熱血喊話用「大家都吃太多粽子」拉回現實，笑點在隊長最後才想起自己人也吃了——適合傳給端午連假吃到扶牆的朋友，或公司龍舟隊群組
variations: 短句版：「龍舟比賽輸了不是技術問題，是粽子問題。」
自嘲版：『我划龍舟的速度，取決於我昨天吃了幾顆粽子。』

en.content:
Before the dragon boat race, the captain gives a pep talk:

"What's our team's biggest advantage?"

Member A: "Teamwork!"
Member B: "Chemistry!"
Member C: "Stamina!"

Captain: "All wrong. Our biggest advantage is — the other team ate way too many zongzi yesterday."

The race starts. Halfway through, our whole boat gets a stomachache too.

Captain: "...Turns out we ate them yesterday as well."
en.editorNote: Takes the fired-up dragon boat pep talk and grounds it with 'everyone ate too many zongzi.' The punchline: the captain forgetting his own team feasted too. Perfect for a friend who ate their way through the festival, or a company dragon boat crew chat
variations: Short version: "We didn't lose the boat race on skill — we lost it on zongzi."
Self-deprecating version: 'My paddling speed is directly determined by how many zongzi I ate the night before.'

items.sourceNote: 屈原投江 historical reference reframed as modern absurd joke; original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
歷史老師問：「同學們知道端午節為什麼要吃粽子嗎？」

小明舉手：「因為要丟進江裡餵魚，不要讓魚去吃屈原。」

老師：「很好，你有讀書。」

小明：「所以我覺得屈原很偉大——他讓後代每年多放一天假。」

老師：「……這不是重點。」

小明：「對現代人來說，這才是重點。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用小學生的視角把屈原投江的歷史悲劇轉成「多放一天假」的現實觀點，笑中帶點荒謬——適合傳給只在乎連假的朋友，屈原本人聽到大概會再投一次
variations: 短句版：「謝謝屈原，讓我每年端午都有假可以放。」
厭世版：『屈原投江是為了理想，我只求連假能放好放滿。』

en.content:
History teacher asks: "Class, does anyone know why we eat zongzi on Dragon Boat Festival?"

Little Ming raises his hand: "Because you throw them in the river to feed the fish, so the fish won't eat Qu Yuan."

Teacher: "Very good, you did your reading."

Little Ming: "So I think Qu Yuan was a great man — he gave every future generation an extra day off."

Teacher: "...That's not the point."

Little Ming: "For modern people, that IS the point."
en.editorNote: Reframes the tragic tale of Qu Yuan drowning himself through a schoolkid's lens: he's great because he earned everyone a day off. Absurd but affectionate — for the friend who only cares about long weekends. Qu Yuan would probably jump again
variations: Short version: "Thank you, Qu Yuan, for the day off every Dragon Boat Festival."
Deadpan version: 'Qu Yuan jumped in the river for his ideals; I just want my long weekend fully intact.'

items.sourceNote: 端午立蛋習俗 reframed as modern relationship joke; original dialogue structure

zh-tw.content:
端午節中午，她蹲在桌前立蛋，立了半小時都立不起來。

男友走過來：「妳知道為什麼立不起來嗎？」

她：「為什麼？」

男友：「因為妳的心早就被我立走了，剩下的都站不穩。」

她把蛋放下：「你再講一句，等一下站不穩的是你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把端午節正午立蛋的習俗改寫成放閃對話，土味情話收在女友的冷回應——適合傳給另一半，或當作立蛋失敗時的自嘲文案
variations: 短句版：「端午立蛋我立不起來，因為心已經傾向你那邊了。」——適合配立蛋照片發限動
厭世版：『蛋立不起來沒關係，反正我這輩子也沒站穩過。』

en.content:
It's noon on Dragon Boat Festival, and she's been trying to stand an egg on its end for half an hour. No luck.

Her boyfriend walks over: "Know why it won't stand up?"

"Why?"

"Because you already stood my heart up straight, and nothing else can balance after that."

She sets the egg down: "Say one more line like that and the thing that won't stay standing is you."
en.editorNote: Turns the noon egg-balancing tradition into a cheesy-couple exchange, capped by her deadpan comeback. Great as a caption when your egg-standing attempt fails spectacularly
variations: Short version: "My egg won't stand up on Dragon Boat noon — my heart's already tilted toward you."
Deadpan version: 'The egg won't balance, but honestly neither have I my whole life.'

items.sourceNote: canoe-dle / paddle water pun family; rewritten into original bilingual dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「這艘龍舟這麼窄，我們要怎麼一起划？」

「很簡單啊。」

「怎麼說？」

「妳負責划，我負責在旁邊跟妳膩在一起——反正龍舟這麼窄，剛好可以名正言順地擠在一起。」

「所以你其實根本沒打算出力？」

「我出的是感情，不是力氣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把龍舟的窄船身當成放閃藉口，男方擺爛得理直氣壯——適合傳給喜歡在活動裡偷懶的另一半，或情侶報名龍舟賽前互相調侃
variations: 短句版：「龍舟這麼窄，剛好可以名正言順地跟你擠在一起。」
反擊版：『你出感情不出力，那等一下翻船你自己游回來。』

en.content:
"This dragon boat is so narrow — how are we supposed to paddle together?"

"Easy."

"How?"

"You paddle, and I'll canoodle right next to you. The boat's this tight anyway, so we've got a perfectly legit excuse to squeeze in close."

"So you were never planning to actually row?"

"I'm putting in feelings, not effort."
en.editorNote: Leans on the paddle/canoodle pun and the boat's narrow seat as a flirting excuse. Good for the partner who dodges effort at any activity, or as pre-race banter
variations: Short version: "This dragon boat's so tight, I've got a legit reason to squeeze in next to you."
Comeback version: 'You bring feelings, not effort? Then swim yourself back when we capsize.'

items.sourceNote: 端午吃粽熱量 diet-anxiety angle; original punchline written fresh

zh-tw.content:
醫生：「你端午節吃了幾顆粽子？」

阿明：「才三顆而已。」

醫生：「一顆粽子的熱量大概等於一碗半的白飯。」

阿明想了三秒：「所以我等於吃了四碗半的飯？」

醫生：「加上花生跟蛋黃，差不多五碗。」

阿明：「那我下次直接吃五碗飯，至少不用剝葉子。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用粽子熱量嚇人的事實收在阿明的擺爛結論——適合傳給端午節嗑粽子毫無罪惡感的朋友，或減肥失敗互相取暖的群組
variations: 短句版：「一顆粽子等於一碗半白飯，所以我今天吃的是一鍋飯。」
自嘲版：『粽子熱量高沒關係，反正我的體重從來沒立起來過。』

en.content:
Doctor: "How many zongzi did you eat over the festival?"

Ming: "Only three."

Doctor: "One zongzi is about a bowl and a half of rice."

Ming thinks for three seconds: "So I ate four and a half bowls of rice?"

Doctor: "With the peanuts and egg yolk, closer to five."

Ming: "Next time I'll just eat five bowls of rice — at least I won't have to unwrap the leaves."
en.editorNote: Uses the scary-real calorie math of zongzi, capped by Ming's give-up logic. Perfect for the friend who eats dumplings guilt-free, or the group chat where everyone's diet already failed
variations: Short version: "One zongzi is a bowl and a half of rice, so today I basically ate a whole pot."
Self-deprecating version: 'High-calorie zongzi, whatever — my weight has never stood up straight anyway.'

items.sourceNote: 艾草掛門避邪 custom reframed as workplace joke; original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
端午節前一天，同事在辦公室門口掛了一把艾草。

我：「你掛這個幹嘛？公司又不是家。」

同事：「艾草可以避邪、驅趕不好的東西。」

我：「那怎麼沒把主管趕走？」

同事看了一眼主管的辦公室：「這束的等級不夠，那個要用整片艾草田才壓得住。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把端午掛艾草避邪的習俗轉成吐槽主管的職場笑話——適合傳到公司群組（確認主管不在的時候），或端午連假前的辦公室閒聊
variations: 短句版：「艾草能避邪，但顯然壓不住我主管，那個要動用一整片艾草田。」
厭世版：『掛了艾草還是沒放假，看來邪的是行事曆。』

en.content:
The day before the festival, a coworker hangs a bundle of mugwort by the office door.

Me: "Why here? This isn't your house."

Coworker: "Mugwort wards off evil and drives away bad things."

Me: "Then why hasn't it driven off our manager?"

Coworker glances at the manager's office: "This bundle isn't strong enough. That one needs a whole field of mugwort to hold it down."
en.editorNote: Turns the door-hung mugwort tradition into a manager-roast office joke. Best shared in the work group chat (once you've confirmed the boss is offline) or as pre-holiday small talk
variations: Short version: "Mugwort wards off evil, but clearly not my manager — that one takes a whole field."
Deadpan version: 'Hung the mugwort and still no day off — turns out the cursed thing is the calendar.'

items.sourceNote: 甜粽 vs 鹹粽 north-south debate reframed as couple argument; rewritten fresh

zh-tw.content:
「我跟你講，粽子一定要吃鹹的，甜粽根本邪教。」

「不對，甜粽沾糖才是正統，鹹粽太油。」

兩個人吵到臉紅脖子粗。

最後她說：「那我們到底還要不要在一起？」

他想了想：「要，但我們家的粽子分兩鍋煮。」

她：「這是我聽過最浪漫的讓步。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把南北粽甜鹹之爭寫成情侶吵架，收在「分兩鍋煮」的成熟妥協——適合傳給口味不合但硬要在一起的另一半，或調侃愛吵吃什麼的情侶
variations: 短句版：「甜粽鹹粽我們永遠吵不完，但我願意為你多煮一鍋。」
反擊版：『分兩鍋煮可以，但洗鍋的是提議的人。』

en.content:
"Listen, zongzi has to be savory. Sweet ones are basically a cult."

"Wrong. Sweet zongzi dipped in sugar is the true form. Savory ones are too greasy."

They argue until they're both red in the face.

Finally she says: "So are we even staying together or not?"

He thinks it over: "Yes. But in our house we'll cook two separate pots."

She: "That's the most romantic compromise I've ever heard."
en.editorNote: Turns the sweet-vs-savory zongzi debate into a couple's fight, resolved by the grown-up 'two pots' compromise. Great for the partner whose taste never matches yours but you stay together anyway
variations: Short version: "We'll never settle sweet vs savory zongzi, but I'll cook a second pot just for you."
Comeback version: 'Two pots is fine, but whoever proposed it washes both.'

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 聖誕老公公為什麼從來不迷路？因為他有一整年的時間先做好路線規劃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 聖誕聚會暖場、交換禮物時的輕鬆笑話
variations: 聖誕老公公不迷路，是因為他早就把路線背熟了。
他有一整年可以規劃路線，當然不會迷路啦。

en.content: Why does Santa never get lost? He's had all year to plan the route.
en.editorNote: Icebreaker for holiday parties or gift exchanges
variations: Santa never gets lost because he mapped it out months ago.
With a whole year to prep, of course Santa knows the way.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 感恩節的火雞為什麼不吃甜點？因為牠已經塞飽飽了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 感恩節餐桌上聊天用，一語雙關「塞餡料」
variations: 火雞不吃甜點，因為肚子裡早就塞滿料了。
感恩節的火雞說：我已經被塞爆了，甜點免了。

en.content: Why didn't the Thanksgiving turkey want dessert? It was already stuffed.
en.editorNote: Great for the dinner table; plays on 'stuffed'
variations: The turkey skipped dessert because it was completely stuffed.
No pie for the turkey, it was stuffed already.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 為什麼日曆跨年時最緊張？因為牠的日子都被數光了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 跨年倒數時的冷笑話,適合朋友聚會
variations: 日曆跨年最緊張,因為牠的日子屈指可數。
跨年那天日曆最慌,牠知道自己快被換掉了。

en.content: Why is the calendar so nervous on New Year's Eve? Its days are numbered.
en.editorNote: A groaner for the countdown; works at any NYE gathering
variations: The calendar panics on NYE because its days are literally numbered.
New Year's Eve stresses the calendar out, its days are running out.

items.sourceNote: Beach/ocean pun family (waves, micro-waves); reworked into original dialogue and Chinese wordplay

zh-tw.content:
問：大海最有禮貌，牠見到沙灘都會做什麼？
答：跟牠揮揮手（浪）啊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天海邊限定的冷笑話,「浪」諧音揮手的動作——適合海邊出遊拍照配文,或群組裡丟一個讓大家哀嚎
variations: 大海一整天都在跟沙灘打招呼,難怪一波接一波。
沙灘問大海:你煩不煩?大海說:我這叫熱情,一浪一浪的。

en.content:
What did the ocean say to the beach when it showed up?
Nothing at all. It just waved.
en.editorNote: A gentle groaner for the beach — great as a caption for shoreline photos or to lob into the group chat and watch everyone sigh
variations: Why is the ocean so friendly? It waves at everyone.
The ocean never says hi to the beach out loud. It's too busy waving.

items.sourceNote: Lazy-crustacean-on-beach 'slobster' pun; rewritten with fresh setup

zh-tw.content:
有一隻龍蝦,整個夏天都躺在沙灘上曬太陽,什麼都不做。
大家都叫牠——懶蝦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日耍廢主題的冷笑話,「懶蝦」既是懶惰又像在罵人——適合形容放假只想躺平的自己或朋友
variations: 問朋友:你這個夏天的計畫是什麼?回:當一隻在沙灘上曬太陽的懶蝦。
海邊最常見的生物不是螃蟹,是攤在躺椅上的懶蝦。

en.content:
What do you call a crustacean that lies on the beach doing absolutely nothing all summer?
A slob-ster.
en.editorNote: Peak summer-laziness humor — perfect for captioning that photo of you melted into a beach chair
variations: My summer spirit animal is the slobster: all beach, no hustle.
The beach isn't full of lobsters. It's full of slobsters.

items.sourceNote: 'Where do eggs vacation? New Yolk City' pun; adapted with new framing

zh-tw.content:
問:蛋放暑假最想去哪裡玩?
答:紐約(蛋)啊,牠說那裡最有蛋味。
zh-tw.editorNote: 食物諧音梗,英文原梗是 egg/yolk,中文改成蛋想去紐約——適合暑假出遊時開個無傷大雅的玩笑
variations: 蛋出國玩只挑一個地方:紐約,因為聽起來最像牠自己。
問蛋暑假去哪?牠說滾去紐約,順便滾一下。

en.content:
Where do eggs go on summer vacation?
New Yolk City.
en.editorNote: A quick food pun for road-trip and travel banter — harmless enough to land with kids and coworkers alike
variations: Eggs never argue about the destination. They always crack and go to New Yolk.
Why did the egg book a summer trip? It needed to get out of its shell.

items.sourceNote: Statue of Liberty 'can't sit down' joke; reworded with original setup

zh-tw.content:
問:為什麼自由女神像永遠站在紐約港,從來不坐下?
答:因為她要是坐下來,大家就找不到她了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 美國國慶(七月四日)應景冷笑話,英文原意是「她根本坐不下來」——適合暑假去美國旅遊、或聊到獨立紀念日時來一個
variations: 自由女神舉火把舉了一百多年,問她累不累,她說:累啊,但我更怕坐下。
為什麼自由女神不休息?因為她的工作就是站著讓人拍照。

en.content:
Why does the Statue of Liberty stand in New York Harbor and never sit down?
Because if she sat, nobody could find her.
en.editorNote: A clean Fourth of July groaner — good for Independence Day cookouts or captioning that harbor cruise photo
variations: The Statue of Liberty never takes a break. Standing is basically her whole job.
Why won't Lady Liberty sit? She's been holding that torch way too long to give up now.

items.sourceNote: 'What do you eat on July 5th? Independence-Day-old pizza' joke; rewritten

zh-tw.content:
問:美國人七月五號早上都吃什麼?
答:昨天國慶烤肉剩下的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 接在七月四日之後的節日餘韻笑話——適合連假隔天、大家都在清冰箱剩菜時發到群組裡引起共鳴
variations: 國慶隔天的餐桌主題只有一個:昨天沒吃完的。
問七月五號的行程?回:把昨天的烤肉再熱一次。

en.content:
What do Americans eat on the morning of July 5th?
Independence-Day-old leftovers.
en.editorNote: The perfect post-holiday joke — drop it in the group chat the day after when everyone's fridge is packed with cookout leftovers
variations: July 5th menu: whatever survived the July 4th barbecue.
The most patriotic thing you can do on July 5th is finish the leftovers.

items.sourceNote: National Ice Cream Day (third Sunday of July) 'committed relationship with ice cream' one-liner; rewritten as a dialogue scene

zh-tw.content:
「你最近是不是有對象了?每天都偷偷傳訊息。」

阿明:「有,關係穩定,每天見面。」

「叫什麼名字?」

阿明:「哈根達斯。」

「⋯⋯那是冰淇淋。」

阿明:「所以我說關係很『冰』定啊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冰淇淋節(七月第三個星期日)應景笑話,「冰定」諧音「穩定」——適合傳給那個夏天每天都在吃冰、體重直線上升卻毫無悔意的朋友
variations: 短版:「我沒有戀愛,我只是跟冰淇淋維持著一段很認真的關係。」
「快樂買不到,但冰淇淋買得到,而且基本上是同一件事。」

en.content:
"You've got someone new, right? You're texting all secretly."

Ming: "Yeah. Very committed. We see each other every day."

"What's their name?"

Ming: "Ben. And Jerry."

"...That's ice cream."

Ming: "Exactly. Rock-solid relationship."
en.editorNote: A National Ice Cream Day (third Sunday of July) groaner. Perfect for the friend who's eating a pint a night this summer and refuses to feel bad about it
variations: Short version: "I'm not single. I'm in a committed relationship with ice cream."
You can't buy happiness, but you can buy ice cream, and honestly that's close enough.

items.sourceNote: World Emoji Day (July 17) emoji-based joke concept; original scene about over-texting with emojis

zh-tw.content:
小美傳訊息給男友:「在幹嘛😀😀😀」

男友回:「⋯⋯你到底在笑什麼?」

小美:「今天世界表情符號日,我在慶祝。」

男友:「所以你沒事。」

小美:「我從來沒說我有事,是你自己腦補的。」

男友:「那三個笑臉是什麼意思?」

小美:「催你回訊息的意思。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 世界表情符號日(七月十七日)應景笑話——精準吐槽「用一排笑臉施壓對方回訊息」的現代溝通日常,適合傳給那個訊息永遠已讀不回的人
variations: 短版:「我傳一排😀不是在笑,是在提醒你該回訊息了。」
「現代人的表情符號:表面在笑,實際在催。」

en.content:
Mei texts her boyfriend: "whatcha doing😀😀😀"

Him: "...What are you laughing about?"

Mei: "It's World Emoji Day. I'm celebrating."

Him: "So nothing's wrong."

Mei: "I never said anything was wrong. You assumed that yourself."

Him: "Then what are the three smileys for?"

Mei: "They mean answer me faster."
en.editorNote: A World Emoji Day (July 17) joke that nails modern texting — the smiley wall isn't cheerful, it's pressure. Send it to the friend who leaves you on read
variations: Short version: "A row of 😀 isn't me laughing. It's me reminding you to reply."
Modern emoji translation: smiling on the outside, nagging on the inside.

items.sourceNote: 'How do celebrities stay cool? They have many fans' summer heatwave joke; rewritten with original setup

zh-tw.content:
問:大明星夏天那麼熱,是怎麼保持涼快的?
答:因為他們有很多『粉』絲,搧一搧就涼了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏日熱浪應景冷笑話,英文原意是「fans」一詞雙關『粉絲』與『電風扇』,中文用「搧」呼應——適合七月飆高溫、大家在群組裡哀嚎「熱死了」的時候丟一個
variations: 問:為什麼香蕉夏天要擦防曬?答:因為它會『脫皮』(曬傷剝皮)。
夏天最忠心的家電只有電風扇——別人吹冷氣它還在硬撐。

en.content:
How do celebrities stay cool during a July heat wave?
They have way too many fans.
en.editorNote: A clean summer groaner that plays on 'fans' meaning both admirers and the thing on your ceiling. Drop it in the group chat when everyone's complaining it's too hot to function
variations: Why do bananas wear sunscreen in July? Because they peel.
In a heat wave, your most loyal appliance is the fan — the AC gets all the credit, but the fan never quit.

items.sourceNote: 'Where do sheep go on vacation? The Baa-hamas' beach vacation pun; rewritten as a family travel dialogue

zh-tw.content:
小孩:「爸,今年暑假我們去哪裡玩?」

爸爸:「去海邊。」

小孩:「哪個海邊?」

爸爸:「你阿公家門口那條大排水溝。」

小孩:「⋯⋯那不是海。」

爸爸:「有水、有夕陽、有蚊子,氣氛到位就好。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假旅遊應景笑話——精準戳中「說要出去玩、結果就近打發」的爸爸經濟學,適合傳到家族群組,尤其是那種每年都說要去墾丁最後去河濱公園的家庭
variations: 短版:「我們家的海邊,叫做家裡的浴缸,加一包海鹽。」
問:綿羊放暑假都去哪?答:巴『咩』馬(Bahamas)。

en.content:
Kid: "Dad, where are we going this summer?"

Dad: "The beach."

Kid: "Which beach?"

Dad: "The big drainage ditch behind Grandpa's house."

Kid: "...That's not a beach."

Dad: "It's got water, a sunset, and mosquitoes. Close enough."
en.editorNote: A summer-vacation joke that skewers budget dad-logic. Great for the family that promises a tropical trip every July and ends up at the local river park
variations: Short version: "Our beach vacation is the bathtub with a packet of sea salt added."
Where do sheep go on summer vacation? The Baa-hamas.

items.sourceNote: Bastille Day (July 14) French-themed pun concept; original dialogue scene built around the holiday

zh-tw.content:
「今天是法國國慶欸,我們來點法式的。」

阿芳:「好啊,法式浪漫?」

「不,法式吐司。」

阿芳:「⋯⋯這也太務實了。」

「浪漫會餓,吐司不會。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 法國國慶(七月十四日)應景冷笑話,把「法式浪漫」硬轉成「法式吐司」——適合傳給那種嘴上說要儀式感、實際只想吃東西的朋友,務實得很好笑
variations: 短版:「今天法國國慶,我決定用法式吐司致敬全法國。」
問:法國人早餐吃什麼慶祝國慶?答:自由、平等、還有可頌。

en.content:
"It's Bastille Day. Let's do something French."

Fang: "Ooh, French romance?"

"No. French toast."

Fang: "...That's incredibly practical."

"Romance leaves you hungry. Toast doesn't."
en.editorNote: A Bastille Day (July 14) groaner that swaps 'French romance' for 'French toast.' Send it to the friend who talks a big game about ambiance but really just wants breakfast
variations: Short version: "Happy Bastille Day. I'm honoring all of France with a plate of French toast."
What do the French have for a Bastille Day breakfast? Liberté, égalité, and a croissant.

items.sourceNote: National Ice Cream Day (third Sunday of July) meltdown pun concept; original dialogue scene written from scratch

zh-tw.content:
「今天是冰淇淋節欸,你怎麼看起來這麼消沉?」

阿宏:「因為我剛剛情緒崩潰了。」

「怎麼了?」

阿宏:「我的冰淇淋掉在地上,融化成一灘。它先崩潰的,我只是陪它。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際冰淇淋節(七月第三個週日)應景笑話,「崩潰」和冰淇淋「融化」雙關——適合大熱天傳給那種冰買了沒三秒就滴滿手的朋友,或當作曬冰照片的配文
variations: 短版:「冰淇淋節唯一的悲劇,是它融化的速度永遠比我吃的速度快。」
問:哪一種冰淇淋最愛哭?答:雙淇淋(雙倍哭),因為它有兩球可以崩潰。

en.content:
"It's National Ice Cream Day. Why do you look so down?"

Hong: "Because I just had a total meltdown."

"What happened?"

Hong: "My ice cream hit the pavement and melted into a puddle. It melted down first. I was just keeping it company."
en.editorNote: A National Ice Cream Day (third Sunday of July) joke built on 'meltdown' doubling for a melting cone. Perfect for a scorching day, or as a caption for that photo of ice cream dripping down your wrist
variations: Short version: "The only tragedy of Ice Cream Day: it melts faster than I can eat it."
What kind of ice cream cries the most? A double scoop — twice the chance for a meltdown.

items.sourceNote: Summer-heat exaggeration joke format; original punchline and characters

zh-tw.content:
「今天到底幾度啊?」

阿玲:「我出門的時候是三十六度。」

「那現在呢?」

阿玲:「現在我是三十六度,體溫。剩下的溫度全部進到我身體裡了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 七月高溫應景冷笑話,把外面的熱轉成「都進到我身體裡」的荒謬結論——適合傳給那種一到夏天就融化在冷氣房不肯出門的朋友,共鳴度百分之百
variations: 短版:「夏天不是我在流汗,是我在原地融化,只是速度比較慢。」
問:七月的太陽最討厭聽到什麼?答:「你可以小力一點嗎?」

en.content:
"How hot is it out there?"

Ling: "It was ninety-six degrees when I left the house."

"And now?"

Ling: "Now I'm ninety-six degrees. Body temperature. All that heat had to go somewhere."
en.editorNote: A July heatwave groaner that lands on the absurd idea of absorbing the outdoor temperature. Send it to the friend who becomes one with the AC unit every summer
variations: Short version: "In summer I'm not sweating — I'm melting in place, just slowly."
What does the July sun hate hearing most? 'Could you maybe tone it down a bit?'

items.sourceNote: Kids-on-summer-break parent humor; original scene and dialogue

zh-tw.content:
小孩放暑假第一天,興奮地問:「媽,我今天可以做什麼?」

媽媽:「你想做什麼都可以。」

小孩:「真的?任何事?」

媽媽:「對,只要是『寫評量、整理房間、幫忙洗碗』這三件事裡面的任何一件。」

小孩瞬間懷念學校。
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假應景親子笑話,精準捕捉「自由的暑假其實是家事集中營」的真相——適合傳到有小孩的家族群組,尤其是那種開學前一週小孩開始想念老師的家庭
variations: 短版:「暑假的自由,是媽媽發的,也是媽媽收回去的。」
問:為什麼小孩放暑假第三天就想開學?答:因為在家的作業叫做家事,而且沒有寒暑假。

en.content:
First day of summer break, the kid asks excitedly: "Mom, what can I do today?"

Mom: "Anything you want."

Kid: "Really? Anything?"

Mom: "Yep — as long as it's one of these three: worksheets, clean your room, or help with the dishes."

The kid suddenly missed school.
en.editorNote: A summer-break parenting joke that nails the truth that 'free' vacation is really a chore boot camp. Great for the family group chat, especially the households where kids start missing their teacher by week two
variations: Short version: "Summer freedom is issued by Mom, and can be revoked by Mom."
Why does a kid want school back by day three of break? Because homework at home is called 'chores,' and it has no vacation.

items.sourceNote: Summer beach/vacation pun format; original wordplay and characters

zh-tw.content:
「這個暑假你最想去哪裡?」

阿雅:「海邊。」

「為什麼?」

阿雅:「因為我想聽海浪的聲音。」

「那你怎麼不看我?」

阿雅:「因為看你,我的心也會有一種『浪』過來的感覺。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假海邊主題情話,把海「浪」轉成心動的「浪」——土到不行但很甜,適合傳給另一半當作邀約去海邊的開場白,對方翻白眼但嘴角會上揚
variations: 短版:「這個夏天我不想去海邊了,因為看你,浪已經來了。」
問:為什麼海邊最適合告白?答:因為就算被拒絕,也可以說是浪太大聽不清楚。

en.content:
"Where do you most want to go this summer?"

Ya: "The beach."

"Why?"

Ya: "I want to hear the waves."

"Then why are you looking at me?"

Ya: "Because looking at you gives me the same feeling — like a wave crashing in."
en.editorNote: A summer beach pickup line where ocean 'waves' double for the flutter of a crush. Cheesy but sweet — use it to invite your special someone to the shore. They'll roll their eyes, then smile
variations: Short version: "I don't need the beach this summer — looking at you, the waves already showed up."
Why is the beach the best place to confess your feelings? Even if you're rejected, you can claim the waves were too loud to hear.

items.sourceNote: International Joke Day (July 1) meta-humor concept; original self-referential dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是國際笑話日欸,講個笑話來聽聽。」

阿凱:「好,從前從前,有一個人很會講笑話。」

「然後呢?」

阿凱:「然後就沒有然後了,因為那個人不是我。」

「⋯⋯這個笑話本身就是笑話。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際笑話日(七月一日)應景後設冷笑話,笑點在「講笑話的人自嘲不會講笑話」——適合傳給那種每次講笑話都冷場、但自己講得很開心的朋友,一種以爛制爛的高級幽默
variations: 短版:「國際笑話日快樂,今天我就是那個笑話。」
問:為什麼冷笑話日這麼冷?答:因為笑話全都不好笑,現場自然降溫。

en.content:
"It's International Joke Day. Tell me a joke."

Kai: "Okay. Once upon a time, there was a person who was really good at telling jokes."

"And then?"

Kai: "And then nothing, because that person wasn't me."

"...The joke IS the joke."
en.editorNote: An International Joke Day (July 1) bit of meta-humor: the punchline is the teller admitting they're bad at jokes. Perfect for the friend whose jokes always flop but who tells them with total joy anyway
variations: Short version: "Happy International Joke Day. Today, I'm the joke."
Why is a bad-joke day so chilly? Because none of the jokes land, so the room cools down on its own.

items.sourceNote: National Hot Dog Day (third Wednesday of July) 'relish' pun family; original heat/dog wordplay written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「今天怎麼這麼熱？」

阿明：「因為今天是國際熱狗日啊。」

「熱狗日跟天氣有什麼關係？」

阿明：「你想想，熱成這樣，連我家那隻狗都快變成熱狗了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際熱狗日（七月第三個星期三）冷笑話，把「熱狗」硬拆成「熱」＋「狗」——適合大熱天傳到群組，一種天氣越熱、笑話越冷的微妙平衡
variations: 短版：「熱狗日快樂，今天出門記得別真的變成一條熱狗。」
問：熱狗最怕遇到什麼人？答：遇到問牠『要不要加芥末』的人，因為牠只想安靜地當一隻狗。

en.content:
"Why is it so hot today?"

Ming: "Because it's National Hot Dog Day."

"What does that have to do with the weather?"

Ming: "Think about it — it's so hot my dog's basically turning into a hot dog."
en.editorNote: A National Hot Dog Day (third Wednesday of July) groaner that splits 'hot dog' back into 'hot' plus 'dog.' Perfect for the group chat on a scorching day — the hotter it gets, the colder the joke
variations: Short version: "Happy Hot Dog Day. Try not to actually become one out there."
The best way to enjoy a hot dog is to relish it. The second best way is to not ask what's in it.

items.sourceNote: Moon Landing Day (July 20) 'no atmosphere' classic; rewritten as a fresh dialogue scene

zh-tw.content:
「聽說月球上開了一家餐廳。」

阿凱：「生意好嗎？」

「食物超好吃，可惜完全沒氣氛。」

阿凱：「太可惜了，為什麼沒氣氛？」

「因為月球上根本沒有大氣層啊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 登月紀念日（七月二十日，一九六九年阿波羅十一號登月）應景冷笑話，笑點在「氣氛」與「大氣層」的雙關（英文都是 atmosphere）——適合傳給喜歡天文或愛講冷笑話的朋友
variations: 短版：「月球餐廳評價——食物滿分，氣氛零分，因為那裡真的一點大氣層都沒有。」
問：太空人為什麼跟另一半分手？答：因為他說他需要一點空間。

en.content:
"Heard they opened a restaurant on the moon."

Kai: "Any good?"

"Amazing food. Shame there's no atmosphere."

Kai: "That's a shame — why no atmosphere?"

"Because it's the moon. There's literally no atmosphere up there."
en.editorNote: A Moon Landing Day (July 20, Apollo 11 in 1969) classic riding on 'atmosphere' meaning both the vibe and the layer of air. Made for the astronomy nerd or the dad-joke friend in your life
variations: Short version: "Moon restaurant review: five stars for food, zero for atmosphere — there isn't any."
Why did the astronaut break up with his partner? He said he needed some space.

items.sourceNote: National Junk Food Day (July 21) diet-vs-snack humor; original couple dialogue written from scratch

zh-tw.content:
「今天是垃圾食物日，我要毫無罪惡感地嗑完一整包洋芋片。」

阿雅：「你昨天不是才說要減肥？」

「減肥是明天的我要煩惱的事，今天的我只想跟這包洋芋片天長地久。」

阿雅：「你對零食都比對我專情。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際垃圾食物日（七月二十一日）情侶對話，笑點在「對零食比對人專情」的自嘲——適合傳給那種嘴上喊減肥、手上拿雞排的朋友，或當作放縱一天的正當理由
variations: 短版：「垃圾食物日快樂，今天卡路里不算數，這是官方規定。」
問：減肥的人最怕哪一天？答：垃圾食物日，因為連藉口都幫你準備好了。

en.content:
"It's National Junk Food Day, so I'm demolishing a whole bag of chips guilt-free."

Ya: "Didn't you just say yesterday you were on a diet?"

"The diet is tomorrow-me's problem. Today-me just wants to grow old with this bag of chips."

Ya: "You're more committed to snacks than you are to me."
en.editorNote: A National Junk Food Day (July 21) couple bit where someone's more loyal to chips than to their partner. Built for the friend who orders a salad and then eats all your fries — or as an airtight excuse to splurge for one day
variations: Short version: "Happy Junk Food Day. Calories don't count today. Those are the rules."
What day do dieters dread most? Junk Food Day — it even hands you the excuse.

items.sourceNote: Cousins Day (July 24) family-gathering humor; original reverse-interrogation dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是表親日，要不要揪表哥表姊出來聚一聚？」

阿明：「好啊，不過先說好，不要又在飯桌上被問薪水跟什麼時候結婚。」

「放心，今天換我們問他們。」

阿明：「那這頓飯終於有意義了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 表親日（七月二十四日）家庭聚會哏，笑點在「反過來拷問親戚」的復仇快感——適合傳到表親群組揪聚會，或當作被長輩逼問後的心理補償
variations: 短版：「表親日快樂，今年換我們問你什麼時候結婚。」
問：為什麼表親聚會這麼歡樂？答：因為終於有人陪你一起吐槽長輩，而且不會回去告狀。

en.content:
"It's Cousins Day. Want to round up the cousins and hang out?"

Ming: "Sure, but let's agree — no more dinner-table questions about my salary or when I'm getting married."

"Relax. This year, WE get to ask THEM."

Ming: "Finally, a family dinner with a purpose."
en.editorNote: A Cousins Day (July 24) family bit about finally turning the interrogation around on your relatives. Send it to the cousin group chat to plan a reunion — or as payback therapy after the last holiday grilling
variations: Short version: "Happy Cousins Day. This year, WE ask when YOU'RE settling down."
Why are cousin hangouts the best? You finally have someone to roast the elders with — and no one snitches.

items.sourceNote: International Day of Friendship (July 30) friendship-test humor; original 'unpaid loan' dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是國際友誼日，我想認真跟你說，你是我最好的朋友。」

阿凱：「哇，好感動，怎麼突然這麼正經？」

「因為只有最好的朋友，才會借我五百塊，然後還一直裝忘記。」

阿凱：「⋯⋯所以你是來討債的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際友誼日（七月三十日）損友哏，笑點在「真正的友情測試就是借錢」的殘酷真實——適合傳給那種可以互相吐槽又不會翻臉的死黨，溫馨中帶著討債的殺氣
variations: 短版：「友誼日快樂，順便溫馨提醒，你還欠我五百塊。」
問：怎麼知道對方是不是真朋友？答：借他一筆錢，然後看你們的友情能撐多久。

en.content:
"It's International Friendship Day, and I just want to say — you're my best friend."

Kai: "Aw, that's so sweet. Why so serious all of a sudden?"

"Because only a best friend would lend me twenty bucks and then keep pretending to forget it."

Kai: "...So you came to collect a debt."
en.editorNote: An International Day of Friendship (July 30) bit where the real friendship test is an unpaid loan. Perfect for the ride-or-die friend you can roast without consequences — warm, with a faint whiff of debt collection
variations: Short version: "Happy Friendship Day. Also, gentle reminder: you still owe me twenty bucks."
How do you know if someone's a true friend? Lend them money and see how long the friendship lasts.

items.sourceNote: National Watermelon Day (Aug 3); original 92%-water pun dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是西瓜日，我想跟你告白。」

阿芳：「哇，這麼突然，你想說什麼？」

「西瓜有百分之九十二是水，而我有百分之百是被你迷得暈頭轉向。」

阿芳：「所以你剩下那百分之八是什麼？」

「是準備要被你打槍的心理準備。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 西瓜日（八月三日）告白哏，笑點在「百分比自嘲」加上早就預期被拒絕的自覺——適合傳給正在曖昧、可以互相虧的對象，夏天配西瓜傳效果最好
variations: 短版：「西瓜日快樂，你就像西瓜，看起來冷冰冰，其實裡面很甜。」
問：西瓜為什麼那麼受歡迎？答：因為它百分之九十二是水，卻能讓你百分之百清涼。

en.content:
"It's National Watermelon Day, so I've got a confession."

Fang: "Whoa, out of nowhere. What is it?"

"A watermelon is 92% water, and I'm 100% dizzy over you."

Fang: "So what's the other 8% of you?"

"Emotional prep for getting turned down."
en.editorNote: A National Watermelon Day (Aug 3) confession riffing on the 92%-water fact, with a self-aware braced-for-rejection punchline. Best sent to a crush you can already tease — bonus points if there's actual watermelon involved
variations: Short version: "Happy Watermelon Day. You're like a watermelon — cool on the outside, secretly sweet inside."
Why is watermelon so popular? It's 92% water but makes you 100% refreshed.

items.sourceNote: National Ice Cream Sandwich Day (Aug 2); original melting-metaphor dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是冰淇淋三明治日，我覺得我們兩個超像冰淇淋三明治。」

阿德：「怎麼說？」

「你是外面那兩片餅乾，我是中間那塊冰淇淋。」

阿德：「聽起來很甜欸。」

「重點是，只要天氣一熱，我就會先融化，然後把場面搞得一團亂。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冰淇淋三明治日（八月二日）自嘲哏，笑點在「本來要放閃結果變成自我吐槽」——適合傳給那種相處起來很輕鬆、可以互相亂虧的朋友或情侶，配一張冰淇淋照更應景
variations: 短版：「冰淇淋三明治日快樂，記得吃快一點，不然融化的速度跟我的意志力一樣快。」
問：夏天吃冰淇淋三明治最大的挑戰是什麼？答：跟時間賽跑，輸的人手上會黏黏的。

en.content:
"It's National Ice Cream Sandwich Day, and honestly, we're basically an ice cream sandwich."

Dee: "How so?"

"You're the two cookies on the outside, I'm the ice cream in the middle."

Dee: "Aw, that's kind of sweet."

"The catch is, the second it gets hot, I melt first and make a total mess."
en.editorNote: A National Ice Cream Sandwich Day (Aug 2) bit that starts as a couple metaphor and collapses into self-roast. Made for the easygoing friend or partner you can trade jabs with — pair it with an ice cream photo
variations: Short version: "Happy Ice Cream Sandwich Day. Eat fast — it melts about as quick as my willpower."
What's the hardest part of a summer ice cream sandwich? Racing the clock. The loser ends up with sticky hands.

items.sourceNote: International Cat Day (Aug 8); original cat-attitude dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是國際貓咪日，我幫主子準備了新玩具、新罐罐，還買了一個超貴的貓跳台。」

阿貞：「你家貓一定很開心吧？」

「牠看都不看，直接躺進裝貓跳台的紙箱裡。」

阿貞：「⋯⋯所以你花那筆錢是為了買一個紙箱。」

「貓奴的日常，你不懂。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際貓咪日（八月八日）養貓哏，笑點在「貓只愛紙箱」的萬年真理——適合傳到貓奴群組引起共鳴，或傳給那個花大錢寵貓卻被無視的朋友
variations: 短版：「國際貓咪日快樂，記得今天貓是主子，你是奴才，跟平常一樣。」
問：怎麼讓貓開心？答：花大錢買玩具，然後把玩具的箱子留給牠。

en.content:
"It's International Cat Day, so I got my little overlord new toys, premium treats, and a ridiculously expensive cat tower."

Jen: "Your cat must be thrilled!"

"It didn't even glance at any of it. Just climbed straight into the box the tower came in."

Jen: "...So you spent all that money to buy a cardboard box."

"Cat-parent life. You wouldn't understand."
en.editorNote: An International Cat Day (Aug 8) bit built on the eternal truth that cats only love the box. Drop it in the cat-owner group chat for instant relatability, or send it to the friend who spoils their cat and gets ignored anyway
variations: Short version: "Happy International Cat Day. Reminder: today the cat is the boss and you're the staff. Same as always."
How do you make a cat happy? Buy an expensive toy, then give it the box the toy came in.

items.sourceNote: Left-Handers Day (Aug 13); original right-handed-world dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是左撇子日，身為左撇子，我想爭取一點尊重。」

阿豪：「說吧，你想要什麼？」

「一把不會把我手弄髒的原子筆，跟一把能用的剪刀，還有寫字不會被本子鐵環卡到的人生。」

阿豪：「這是節日願望還是陳情書？」

「對左撇子來說，這兩個是同一件事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 左撇子日（八月十三日）自嘲哏，笑點在「右撇子世界對左撇子的日常小折磨」——適合傳給身邊的左撇子朋友表示理解，右撇子看了也會突然意識到自己活得多輕鬆
variations: 短版：「左撇子日快樂，今天請右撇子朋友體驗一下用左手剪東西，你就懂了。」
問：左撇子最大的成就是什麼？答：在一個為右手設計的世界裡，還能好好活到現在。

en.content:
"It's Left-Handers Day, and as a lefty, I'd like to demand a little respect."

Hao: "Go on, what do you want?"

"A pen that doesn't smear all over my hand, scissors that actually work, and a life where spiral notebooks don't fight me."

Hao: "Is that a holiday wish or a formal complaint?"

"For a lefty, those are the same thing."
en.editorNote: A Left-Handers Day (Aug 13) self-roast about the tiny daily indignities of a right-handed world. Send it to the lefty in your life to show solidarity — righties reading it suddenly realize how easy they've had it
variations: Short version: "Happy Left-Handers Day. Righties, try cutting paper with your left hand today. Then you'll get it."
What's a left-hander's greatest achievement? Surviving this long in a world built entirely for right hands.

items.sourceNote: World Emoji Day (Jul 17); original texting-generation-gap dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是世界表情符號日欸，我傳一個笑臉給我媽。」

過了一分鐘，媽媽回：一個骷髏頭。

「媽你為什麼回我骷髏頭？」

「骷髏頭不是代表笑到骨頭都散了嗎？我看你們年輕人都這樣用。」

「⋯⋯媽你這樣用，我朋友會以為你在詛咒我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 世界表情符號日（七月十七日）親子代溝哏，笑點在「長輩自創表情符號解讀」——每個家庭群組都有一個把 💀 當笑臉、把 🙏 當「給你讚」的長輩，傳給家人保證會心一笑
variations: 短版：「世界表情符號日快樂，今天請不要傳一整排你根本不知道意思的表情給爸媽，他們會很努力研究一整天。」
問：長輩最愛用的表情符號是什麼？答：一個玫瑰花，不管在講什麼都可以插一朵。

en.content:
"It's World Emoji Day, so I texted my mom a smiley face."

A minute later she replies: a skull.

"Mom, why'd you send me a skull?"

"Doesn't the skull mean laughing so hard your bones fall apart? I've seen you kids use it."

"...Mom, when YOU use it, my friends think you're putting a curse on me."
en.editorNote: A World Emoji Day (Jul 17) generation-gap bit. Every family group chat has one elder who thinks 💀 is a smile and 🙏 is a thumbs up. Send it to the family thread for guaranteed knowing groans
variations: Short version: "Happy World Emoji Day. Please don't send your parents a row of emojis you don't understand — they will research it all day."
What's a parent's favorite emoji? A single rose, added to every message no matter the topic.

items.sourceNote: National Ice Cream Day (Jul 19, third Sunday); original diet-excuse dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是冰淇淋日，可是我在減肥。」

「那你就不要吃啊。」

「你不懂，節日是要尊重的，尊重傳統是一種美德。」

「所以你買了幾球？」

「三球，我很尊重。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冰淇淋日（七月第三個週日）減肥破功哏，笑點在「用節日當藉口的完美邏輯」——每個嘴上減肥的人都懂這種自我說服，傳給那個永遠明天再開始的朋友剛剛好
variations: 短版：「冰淇淋日快樂，今天融化的不只是冰淇淋，還有我的減肥計畫。」
問：減肥的人為什麼特別喜歡冰淇淋日？答：因為這是唯一一天，破功可以說成過節。

en.content:
"It's National Ice Cream Day, but I'm on a diet."

"Then don't eat any."

"You don't get it. Holidays deserve respect. Honoring tradition is a virtue."

"So how many scoops did you buy?"

"Three. I'm very respectful."
en.editorNote: A National Ice Cream Day (third Sunday of July) diet-fail bit. Anyone who's ever 'started their diet tomorrow' knows this flawless logic. Send it to the friend who treats every holiday as a cheat day
variations: Short version: "Happy National Ice Cream Day. Today the only thing melting faster than my cone is my diet."
Why do dieters love Ice Cream Day? It's the one day you can call falling off the wagon 'celebrating.'

items.sourceNote: National Hot Dog Day (Jul 16); original literal-vs-food misunderstanding

zh-tw.content:
「今天是熱狗日，我家狗狗可以過節嗎？」

「熱狗日是慶祝那個食物，不是慶祝熱的狗。」

「可是我家狗今天真的很熱，牠也算熱狗吧？」

「⋯⋯你這樣講，牠聽了會很沒有安全感。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 熱狗日（七月十六日）諧音誤會哏，笑點在「把節日名稱照字面亂解讀」——養狗的人看到會邊翻白眼邊傳給另一半，冷得剛剛好又有畫面感
variations: 短版：「熱狗日快樂，提醒各位飼主：今天慶祝的是食物，你家的狗不需要被夾進麵包裡。」
問：狗狗在熱狗日最怕聽到什麼？答：「主人，我們今天來玩一個角色扮演。」

en.content:
"It's National Hot Dog Day. Can my dog celebrate?"

"Hot Dog Day is about the food, not about a dog that's hot."

"But my dog really is hot today. Doesn't that make him a hot dog?"

"...Say that any louder and he's going to have some serious trust issues."
en.editorNote: A National Hot Dog Day (Jul 16) literal-misreading bit. Every dog owner will roll their eyes and forward it to their partner — dry, silly, and weirdly vivid
variations: Short version: "Happy National Hot Dog Day. Reminder to pet owners: today celebrates the food. Your dog does not need to go in a bun."
What's a dog's worst fear on Hot Dog Day? Hearing 'Hey buddy, let's try a little role-play today.'

items.sourceNote: Bastille Day (Jul 14); original French-cliché self-aware dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是法國國慶日，我決定入境隨俗過一下。」

「你要做什麼？」

「早餐吃可頌，午餐配紅酒，然後對所有事情聳肩說『這就是人生』。」

「那你跟平常有什麼不一樣？」

「⋯⋯今天我聳肩的時候，心裡是有文化底蘊的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 法國國慶日（七月十四日）自嘲哏，笑點在「用刻板印象過節，結果發現自己本來就這樣」——適合傳給那種凡事都很佛系、遇事就攤手的朋友，笑點溫和不冒犯
variations: 短版：「法國國慶日快樂，今天請容我優雅地聳肩，並宣稱這是一種生活態度。」
問：怎麼看出一個人在慶祝法國國慶日？答：他手上有可頌，眼神很憂鬱，然後說這叫浪漫。

en.content:
"It's Bastille Day, so I'm going full French today."

"What does that involve?"

"Croissant for breakfast, wine with lunch, and shrugging at everything while saying 'that's life.'"

"How is that different from your normal day?"

"...Today, when I shrug, there's cultural depth behind it."
en.editorNote: A Bastille Day (Jul 14) self-roast about leaning into clichés, only to realize you already live that way. Great for the friend who shrugs at everything — gentle, not mean
variations: Short version: "Happy Bastille Day. Please allow me to shrug elegantly today and call it a philosophy."
How do you spot someone celebrating Bastille Day? Croissant in hand, melancholy in the eyes, and they call it romance.

items.sourceNote: Qixi / Chinese Valentine's (upcoming Aug); original single-person self-roast dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「七夕快到了，你有什麼計畫？」

「有啊，我要陪牛郎織女一起隔著銀河遙望。」

「你也有暗戀的人喔？」

「沒有，我是遙望我的存款，看它跟我一年也只能見一次面。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 七夕（農曆七月七日，約落在陽曆八月）單身自嘲哏，笑點在「把牛郎織女的距離套用到自己跟錢的關係」——適合單身的人在情人節檔期發限動，比放閃更有共鳴，笑中帶一點心酸
variations: 短版：「七夕快樂，今晚我跟織女一樣，都在等一個不會來的人。」
問：七夕最適合單身的人做什麼？答：抬頭看星星，順便看看有沒有一顆願意脫單。

en.content:
"Qixi's coming up. Any plans?"

"Yeah, I'm going to gaze across the Milky Way with the Cowherd and Weaver Girl."

"Oh, you've got a secret crush too?"

"No, I'm gazing at my savings account. It also only gets to see me once a year."
en.editorNote: A Qixi (Chinese Valentine's, 7th day of the 7th lunar month, usually August) single-life self-roast. It maps the star-crossed lovers' once-a-year reunion onto your relationship with money. More relatable than a couple selfie — funny with a pinch of ache
variations: Short version: "Happy Qixi. Tonight, like the Weaver Girl, I'm waiting for someone who isn't coming."
What's the best Qixi activity for single people? Look up at the stars — and check if any of them want to settle down.

items.sourceNote: National Tequila Day (Jul 24); original bar-order dialogue about the salt-and-lime ritual

zh-tw.content:
「今天是龍舌蘭日，來一杯嗎？」

「好啊，可是為什麼喝之前要先舔鹽、喝完再咬檸檬？」

「這叫儀式感，先苦後酸，中間那口才有意義。」

「⋯⋯你這根本是在形容我上禮拜的感情生活。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 龍舌蘭日（七月二十四日）喝酒哏，笑點在「舔鹽、喝酒、咬檸檬」的順序被硬套成人生比喻——適合傳到那種週五晚上會約喝一杯的群組，先笑再乾杯
variations: 短版：「龍舌蘭日快樂，人生就像這杯：先苦、後酸，中間還會讓你有點暈。」
問：龍舌蘭日隔天最需要什麼？答：一個不會問你昨晚傳了什麼訊息的朋友。

en.content:
"It's National Tequila Day. Want a shot?"

"Sure, but why do we lick salt first, then bite a lime after?"

"It's the ritual. Bitter first, sour last, and the good part's squeezed in the middle."

"...You just described my love life from last week."
en.editorNote: A National Tequila Day (Jul 24) drinking bit that turns the salt-shot-lime ritual into an accidental life metaphor. Perfect for the group chat that plans Friday drinks — laugh first, then clink
variations: Short version: "Happy National Tequila Day. Life's like this shot: bitter, then sour, and somewhere in there it makes you dizzy."
What do you need most the day after Tequila Day? A friend who won't ask what you texted last night.

items.sourceNote: National Cheesecake Day (Jul 30); original diet-excuse dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「你不是說要減肥？怎麼在吃起司蛋糕？」

「今天是起司蛋糕日，不吃是對節日的不尊重。」

「那昨天的珍奶呢？」

「昨天是⋯⋯珍奶日。」

「⋯⋯我怎麼覺得你每天都有一個很剛好的節日。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 起司蛋糕日（七月三十日）減肥破功哏，笑點在「用節日當藉口，而且每天都有一個」——適合傳給那種一邊喊減肥一邊點甜點的朋友，一秒被說中
variations: 短版：「起司蛋糕日快樂，今天的熱量不算數，這是節日規定。」
問：減肥的人遇到起司蛋糕日該怎麼辦？答：先拍照，再吃掉證據。

en.content:
"Weren't you on a diet? Why are you eating cheesecake?"

"It's National Cheesecake Day. Skipping it would be disrespectful to the holiday."

"And the bubble tea yesterday?"

"Yesterday was... Bubble Tea Day."

"...Somehow you have a conveniently perfect holiday every single day."
en.editorNote: A National Cheesecake Day (Jul 30) diet-excuse bit — there's always a food holiday to justify the craving. Send it to the friend who orders dessert while announcing they're cutting back
variations: Short version: "Happy National Cheesecake Day. Today's calories don't count — it's a holiday rule."
What should a dieter do on Cheesecake Day? Take the photo first, then eat the evidence.

items.sourceNote: Take Your Pants For A Walk Day (Jul 27); original literal-interpretation dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是『帶你的褲子去散步日』，我要出門了。」

「⋯⋯那不就是叫你穿褲子出門？」

「不一樣，這是我今天出門唯一的理由，很有意義。」

「所以你平常出門是不穿褲子的？」

「⋯⋯我是說有沒有節日，我的褲子的心情是不一樣的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 遛褲子日（七月二十七日，Take Your Pants For A Walk Day）照字面亂解讀哏，笑點在「把一個廢到笑的節日當成出門的正當理由」——適合傳給那種週末不想出門、需要一個荒謬藉口的宅友
variations: 短版：「遛褲子日快樂，今天請帶你的褲子出去走走，順便把你自己也帶上。」
問：怎麼慶祝遛褲子日？答：穿上褲子，走出門，然後跟褲子說今天是牠的日子。

en.content:
"It's Take Your Pants For A Walk Day, so I'm heading out."

"...Isn't that just telling you to wear pants outside?"

"It's different. Today it's my one meaningful reason to leave the house."

"So normally you go out without pants?"

"...I mean my pants just feel differently when there's a holiday."
en.editorNote: A Take Your Pants For A Walk Day (Jul 27) literal-reading bit — a gloriously pointless holiday repurposed as an excuse to finally go outside. Send it to the homebody friend who needs an absurd reason to leave the couch
variations: Short version: "Happy Take Your Pants For A Walk Day. Please take your pants outside today, and bring yourself along too."
How do you celebrate Take Your Pants For A Walk Day? Put on pants, step outside, and tell them it's their special day.

items.sourceNote: National Tug-Of-War Tournament Day (Jul 21); original relationship-metaphor dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是拔河日，你想到什麼？」

「想到我跟我媽在爭遙控器。」

「那算哪門子拔河？」

「一條繩子兩邊拉，僵持不下、誰都不肯放手，最後累的人先鬆手——這不就是我家客廳每晚的日常？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 拔河日（七月二十一日，National Tug-Of-War Tournament Day）家庭日常哏，笑點在「把搶遙控器比喻成拔河，還講得頭頭是道」——適合傳到家族群組，尤其是家裡有人愛搶頻道的
variations: 短版：「拔河日快樂，人生最難的那場拔河，對手往往住在你家。」
問：拔河日最強的對手是誰？答：不肯放手又體力驚人的另一半。

en.content:
"It's National Tug-Of-War Day. What comes to mind?"

"Me and my mom fighting over the TV remote."

"How is that tug-of-war?"

"One rope, two sides pulling, nobody letting go, and whoever gets tired first loses — isn't that just my living room every night?"
en.editorNote: A National Tug-Of-War Tournament Day (Jul 21) domestic bit that reframes the nightly remote-control standoff as a sporting event. Send it to the family thread, especially if someone at home is a channel hog
variations: Short version: "Happy Tug-Of-War Day. The hardest match of your life is usually against someone who lives in your house."
Who's the toughest opponent on Tug-Of-War Day? A partner who won't let go and has surprising stamina.

items.sourceNote: National Watermelon Day (Aug 3); original how-far-do-you-eat dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是西瓜日，你吃西瓜都吃到哪裡？」

「吃到看見白色為止。」

「那我媽是吃到看見綠色為止。」

「⋯⋯所以你們家一顆西瓜可以養活兩代人。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 西瓜日（八月三日，National Watermelon Day）夏日日常哏，笑點在「媽媽把西瓜啃到剩綠皮，一顆能養兩代人」——適合傳到家族群組，尤其家裡有那種捨不得浪費的長輩
variations: 短版：「西瓜日快樂，人生就像西瓜，最甜的都在中間，邊邊留給有耐心的人。」
問：西瓜日怎麼過最快樂？答：抱一整顆，配一支湯匙，不用切、不用分。

en.content:
"It's National Watermelon Day. How far into a watermelon do you eat?"

"Until I see white."

"My mom eats until she sees green."

"...So one watermelon feeds two generations in your house."
en.editorNote: A National Watermelon Day (Aug 3) summer bit — mom eats all the way down to the green rind, so one melon feeds the whole family tree. Send it to the family thread, especially if someone at home hates wasting food
variations: Short version: "Happy National Watermelon Day. Like a watermelon, the sweetest part's always in the middle — the edges are for the patient."
How do you best celebrate Watermelon Day? Hug a whole one, grab a spoon, no cutting, no sharing.

items.sourceNote: National Ice Cream Sandwich Day (Aug 2); original melting-race dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是冰淇淋三明治日，我買了一個。」

「那你怎麼一手黏答答的？」

「因為它融化的速度比我吃的速度快。」

「那不就輸了？」

「不會，我把手也舔乾淨了，一口都沒浪費。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冰淇淋三明治日（八月二日，National Ice Cream Sandwich Day）夏天融化哏，笑點在「跟融化速度賽跑，連沾到手的也不放過」——適合傳給那種夏天吃冰吃得手忙腳亂的朋友，一秒被說中
variations: 短版：「冰淇淋三明治日快樂，夏天最公平的比賽：你和融化速度的賽跑。」
問：冰淇淋三明治日的隱藏規則是什麼？答：手上沾到的也算你的，不准浪費。

en.content:
"It's National Ice Cream Sandwich Day, so I bought one."

"Then why is one hand all sticky?"

"Because it melts faster than I can eat it."

"So it beat you?"

"Nope. I licked my hand clean too — didn't waste a single bite."
en.editorNote: A National Ice Cream Sandwich Day (Aug 2) summer bit about racing the melt and refusing to waste the drips. Send it to the friend who turns every ice cream into a two-handed emergency
variations: Short version: "Happy Ice Cream Sandwich Day. Summer's fairest race: you versus the melting."
What's the hidden rule of Ice Cream Sandwich Day? Whatever drips on your hand still counts — no wasting.

items.sourceNote: National Lazy Day (Aug 10); original slacking-vs-celebrating dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是懶人日，你打算做什麼？」

「什麼都不做。」

「那你昨天做了什麼？」

「也是什麼都不做。」

「那有什麼差別？」

「昨天是偷懶，今天是慶祝，心境完全不一樣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 懶人日（八月十日，National Lazy Day）躺平哏，笑點在「同樣什麼都不做，昨天叫偷懶今天叫過節」——適合傳給那種週末只想躺著的朋友，給他一個名正言順的理由
variations: 短版：「懶人日快樂，今天躺著不是墮落，是在遵守節日精神。」
問：懶人日最忌諱什麼？答：問一個懶人『你今天做了什麼』。

en.content:
"It's National Lazy Day. What are your plans?"

"Nothing at all."

"What did you do yesterday?"

"Also nothing at all."

"So what's the difference?"

"Yesterday I was slacking. Today I'm celebrating. Totally different mindset."
en.editorNote: A National Lazy Day (Aug 10) do-nothing bit — the same empty afternoon counts as slacking one day and honoring the holiday the next. Send it to the friend who just wants to lie down all weekend, and hand them a legit excuse
variations: Short version: "Happy National Lazy Day. Lying around today isn't laziness — it's observing the holiday."
What's the biggest taboo on Lazy Day? Asking a lazy person what they got done today.

items.sourceNote: International Left-Handers Day (Aug 13); original lefty-complaints dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是左撇子日，身為左撇子你最想抱怨什麼？」

「剪刀、開罐器、還有全世界的桌子。」

「桌子怎麼了？」

「大學那種右邊有小桌板的椅子，我坐了四年都是懸空寫字。」

「所以你的字醜是有原因的。」

「⋯⋯謝謝你終於懂我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 左撇子日（八月十三日，International Left-Handers Day）左撇子日常哏，笑點在「右手世界的各種不友善，連字醜都情有可原」——適合傳給身邊的左撇子朋友，一秒被理解的感動
variations: 短版：「左撇子日快樂，這世界百分之九十是為右手設計的，剩下的十趴才是我們的主場。」
問：左撇子日最想收到什麼禮物？答：一把真的剪得斷東西的左手剪刀。

en.content:
"It's Left-Handers Day. As a lefty, what's your biggest complaint?"

"Scissors, can openers, and every desk on earth."

"What's wrong with desks?"

"Those lecture-hall chairs with the little tray on the right — I wrote in mid-air for four years of college."

"So there's a reason your handwriting is bad."

"...Thank you for finally understanding me."
en.editorNote: An International Left-Handers Day (Aug 13) bit about a right-handed world — scissors, can openers, and desks that turn every lefty into a mid-air writer. Send it to your left-handed friend for that instant feeling of being understood
variations: Short version: "Happy Left-Handers Day. Ninety percent of the world is built for right hands — the other ten percent is our home turf."
What does a lefty want most on Left-Handers Day? A pair of left-handed scissors that actually cut.

items.sourceNote: International Juggling Day (Jul 16); original life-juggling metaphor dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是國際雜耍日，你會雜耍嗎？」

「會啊，工作、房貸、還有我媽的期望，我每天同時丟三顆球。」

「那你都沒掉過？」

「掉過，只是我假裝那顆球本來就不重要。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際雜耍日（七月十六日，International Juggling Day）人生哏，笑點在「同時應付三件事還要假裝掉的那顆不重要」——適合傳給那種被生活追著跑的大人朋友，苦中作樂剛剛好
variations: 短版：「雜耍日快樂，我一手工作一手生活，只是兩隻手都在發抖。」
問：現代人最厲害的雜耍是什麼？答：一邊回訊息說『在忙』，一邊繼續滑手機。

en.content:
"It's International Juggling Day. Can you juggle?"

"Sure — work, my mortgage, and my mom's expectations. I keep three balls in the air every single day."

"And you never drop one?"

"Oh, I drop them. I just pretend that one was never important anyway."
en.editorNote: An International Juggling Day (Jul 16) bit about juggling life instead of props — the punchline is pretending the ball you dropped never mattered. Send it to the friend who's always running on empty; it's gallows humor that lands
variations: Short version: "Happy Juggling Day. I've got work in one hand and life in the other — both hands are shaking."
What's the hardest juggling act of modern life? Texting 'so busy right now' while you keep scrolling.

items.sourceNote: National Caviar Day (Jul 18); original luxury-food dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「魚子醬日快樂，來，嘗一口頂級享受。」

「⋯⋯這不就是鹹的、貴的、小小的黑色顆粒？」

「這一小匙要三百塊。」

「那我吃三顆就好。」

「怎麼？怕胖？」

「不，怕破產。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 魚子醬日（七月十八日，National Caviar Day）平民哏，笑點在「頂級美食在錢包面前一秒破功」——適合傳給那種餐廳只看右邊價錢欄的省錢朋友，會心一笑
variations: 短版：「魚子醬日快樂，人家吃的是奢華，我吃的是心痛。」
問：魚子醬和我的存款有什麼共通點？答：都是一小口就沒了。

en.content:
"Happy Caviar Day. Here, taste a little luxury."

"...Aren't these just salty, expensive, tiny black beads?"

"That spoonful costs thirty bucks."

"Then I'll have three beads."

"What, watching your weight?"

"No. Watching my bank account."
en.editorNote: A National Caviar Day (Jul 18) bit where fancy food loses to the wallet in one second. Send it to the friend who only reads the right-hand price column on a menu — instant recognition
variations: Short version: "Happy Caviar Day. Some people taste luxury; I taste heartburn from the bill."
What do caviar and my savings have in common? Both disappear in one small bite.

items.sourceNote: Flitch Day / Dunmow Flitch tradition (mid-July); original marriage-comedy dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是培根日，聽說結婚滿一年又一天、完全不後悔的夫妻，可以贏一整塊培根。」

「所以獎品是⋯⋯培根？」

「對。」

「那我們穩拿。」

「你這麼有把握不後悔？」

「我後悔的不是婚姻，是當年沒先問清楚有沒有培根獎品。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 培根日源自英國古老的 Dunmow Flitch 傳統：能證明結婚一年零一天都不後悔的夫妻可贏得一塊培根。笑點在「用培根當結婚不後悔的證據」——適合傳給結婚多年的朋友或另一半，土味中帶點甜
variations: 短版：「培根日快樂，我們的婚姻值一塊培根，這是官方認證的幸福。」
問：怎麼判斷一段婚姻夠幸福？答：看他們願不願意為一塊培根宣誓不後悔。

en.content:
"It's Flitch Day. Apparently couples married a year and a day with zero regrets can win a whole side of bacon."

"So the prize is... bacon?"

"Yep."

"Then we've got this in the bag."

"You're that sure you don't regret it?"

"I don't regret the marriage. I regret not asking about the bacon prize sooner."
en.editorNote: Flitch Day comes from the old English Dunmow Flitch tradition — couples who prove a year and a day of no marital regret win a flitch of bacon. The joke is using bacon as proof of a happy marriage. Cute-but-cheesy send for your spouse or long-married friends
variations: Short version: "Happy Flitch Day. Our marriage is officially worth a side of bacon — certified bliss."
How do you know a marriage is happy enough? See if they'll swear off regret for a slab of bacon.

items.sourceNote: Ugly Truck Contest Day (Jul 20); original beat-up-truck pride dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是醜卡車比賽日，你要報名嗎？」

「我這台開了二十年，保險桿用膠帶黏的，兩邊後照鏡還不同顏色。」

「聽起來很有機會欸。」

「什麼叫有機會。」

「不然叫什麼？」

「我是衛冕冠軍。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 醜卡車比賽日（七月二十日，Ugly Truck Contest Day）反向炫耀哏，笑點在「把車醜當成一種驕傲的成就」——適合傳給那種對自己老車、舊機車有莫名感情的朋友，越醜越有故事
variations: 短版：「醜卡車日快樂，我的車不是舊，是有歲月的質感。」
問：怎麼分辨一台車是醜還是有個性？答：看車主講起它的時候眼睛有沒有在發光。

en.content:
"It's Ugly Truck Contest Day. You entering?"

"My truck's twenty years old, the bumper's held on with tape, and the two mirrors don't even match colors."

"Sounds like you've got a real shot."

"A shot?"

"What would you call it?"

"I'm the defending champion."
en.editorNote: An Ugly Truck Contest Day (Jul 20) reverse-flex bit where a wrecked truck becomes a badge of pride. Send it to the friend who's weirdly attached to their beat-up car or old scooter — the uglier it is, the better the story
variations: Short version: "Happy Ugly Truck Day. My truck isn't old — it's got the texture of experience."
How do you tell if a car is ugly or just full of character? Watch if the owner's eyes light up describing it.

items.sourceNote: National Relaxation Day (Aug 15); original anxious-relaxation dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「今天是放鬆日，你放鬆了嗎？」

「我試著放鬆，結果一直想著等等還有多少事沒做，越想越焦慮。」

「那不就完全沒放鬆到？」

「有啦。」

「有？」

「我焦慮得很放鬆。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 放鬆日（八月十五日，National Relaxation Day）現代人哏，笑點在「連放鬆都要焦慮，最後只好焦慮得很放鬆」——適合傳給那種一閒下來就渾身不對勁的工作狂朋友，看了苦笑
variations: 短版：「放鬆日快樂，我今天躺了一整天，腦袋卻加班加到半夜。」
問：現代人怎麼放鬆？答：滑手機到凌晨，然後怪自己為什麼那麼累。

en.content:
"It's National Relaxation Day. Are you relaxed?"

"I tried, but I kept thinking about everything I still have to do, and got more anxious the more I thought."

"So you didn't relax at all?"

"I did, though."

"You did?"

"I'm anxious in a very relaxed way."
en.editorNote: A National Relaxation Day (Aug 15) bit for modern life — even relaxing turns into anxiety, until you're just anxious in a relaxed way. Send it to the workaholic friend who feels wrong the moment they slow down; it earns a rueful laugh
variations: Short version: "Happy Relaxation Day. I lay down all day while my brain worked overtime till midnight."
How do modern people relax? Scroll until 2am, then blame themselves for being so tired.

items.sourceNote: National Book Lovers Day (Aug 9); original TBR-pile dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「書蟲日快樂，你最近在看什麼書？」

「我買了七本。」

「哇，看完了？」

「我看完了博客來的結帳頁面。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 書蟲日（八月九日，National Book Lovers Day）現代讀者哏，笑點在「買書如山倒，看書如抽絲」——適合傳給那種書架滿了卻一本沒翻、還一直下單的囤書型朋友
variations: 短版：「我不是不看書，我是在培養它們的感情，讓它們在書架上先熟一下。」
問：書蟲最強的技能是什麼？答：把『之後再看』延到天荒地老。

en.content:
"Happy Book Lovers Day! What are you reading these days?"

"I bought seven books."

"Wow, finished them all?"

"I finished the checkout page."
en.editorNote: A Book Lovers Day (Aug 9) bit for the modern reader — buying books and reading books are two entirely separate hobbies. Send it to the friend whose shelf is full of untouched spines who keeps clicking 'add to cart'
variations: Short version: "I'm not ignoring my books. I'm letting them ripen on the shelf first."
What's a book lover's greatest skill? Postponing 'I'll read it later' until the heat death of the universe.

items.sourceNote: National Tell A Joke Day (Aug 16); original bad-joke-timing dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「今天是說笑話日，快講一個。」

「好，敲敲門。」

「誰在外面？」

「……我忘記後面了。」

「這就是最好笑的部分。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 說笑話日（八月十六日，National Tell A Joke Day）自嘲哏，笑點在「講到一半忘詞，結果那個空白反而最好笑」——適合傳給那種永遠記不住笑話結尾、卻很愛講的朋友，或群組裡專門冷場的那位
variations: 短版：「我今天要講一個超好笑的笑話，但我怕你們笑到報警。所以算了。」
問：說笑話日怎麼確保全場大笑？答：講完後自己先笑，笑到別人尷尬也得笑。

en.content:
"It's Tell A Joke Day. Give me one."

"Okay. Knock knock."

"Who's there?"

"...I forgot the rest."

"That's the funniest part."
en.editorNote: A Tell A Joke Day (Aug 16) bit — the setup lands, the punchline evaporates, and the blank stare becomes the joke. Send it to the friend who loves telling jokes but can never remember how they end, or the one who kills every group chat
variations: Short version: "I've got a hilarious joke for Tell A Joke Day, but I'm scared you'll laugh hard enough to call 911. So never mind."
How do you guarantee laughs on Tell A Joke Day? Laugh at your own joke first, until everyone else has to join out of secondhand embarrassment.

items.sourceNote: National Waffle Day (Aug 24); original waffle-vs-indecision pun dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「鬆餅日快樂，要吃鬆餅還是格子鬆餅？」

「嗯……我不確定。」

「快選。」

「你別逼我，我在這種事上就是很格子。」

「格子？」

「格子代表我心裡有很多格，每格都想要。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 鬆餅日（八月二十四日，National Waffle Day）諧音哏，英文 waffle 有「猶豫不決」的意思，中文用「格子」硬凹選擇困難——適合傳給那種點餐永遠選不出來、菜單看半小時的朋友
variations: 短版：「鬆餅日我糾結了一小時，最後兩種都點，這才是正確答案。」
問：鬆餅日最難的是什麼？答：不是煎鬆餅，是決定要不要加第二球冰淇淋。

en.content:
"Happy Waffle Day! Pancakes or waffles?"

"Hmm... I'm not sure."

"Just pick."

"Don't rush me. On decisions like this I really waffle."

"You waffle?"

"Yeah, and today of all days, I'm proud of it."
en.editorNote: A Waffle Day (Aug 24) pun — to 'waffle' means to hesitate, so it's the one holiday where indecision is on theme. Send it to the friend who stares at the menu for thirty minutes and still can't choose
variations: Short version: "Spent an hour waffling on Waffle Day, then ordered both. That's the correct answer."
What's the hardest part of Waffle Day? Not making the waffle — deciding whether to add a second scoop of ice cream.

items.sourceNote: National Dog Day (Aug 26); original dog-loyalty-vs-honesty dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「狗狗日快樂，你家狗最愛你什麼？」

「牠說牠最愛我的忠誠。」

「狗會說話？」

「不會，但每次我開冰箱牠都超忠誠地跟過來。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 狗狗日（八月二十六日，National Dog Day）溫馨吐槽哏，笑點在「狗的忠誠其實是對食物的忠誠」——適合傳給養狗的朋友，或家裡那隻只有吃飯時間才認得你的毛小孩主人
variations: 短版：「我家狗把我當全世界，前提是我手上有零食。」
問：狗狗日怎麼證明牠愛你？答：牠願意在你吃東西時，用最真誠的眼神盯著你。

en.content:
"Happy Dog Day! What does your dog love most about you?"

"He says he loves my loyalty."

"Your dog talks?"

"No, but every time I open the fridge he loyally follows me over."
en.editorNote: A National Dog Day (Aug 26) bit — a dog's devotion is real, it's just aimed squarely at the fridge. Send it to the dog owner whose pup only remembers their name at mealtimes
variations: Short version: "My dog thinks I'm his whole world — as long as there's a treat in my hand."
How does a dog prove its love on Dog Day? By staring at you with total sincerity the entire time you're eating.

items.sourceNote: Moon Day / Space Exploration Day (July 20); original distance-joke dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「七月二十號是月球日，你知道人類為什麼要登陸月球嗎？」

「為了探索宇宙？」

「不是，是為了證明——再遠的距離，只要肯花錢，都到得了。」

「那你為什麼還沒來我家？」

「……你家比月球難停車。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 月球日（七月二十日，紀念一九六九年阿波羅登月）冷吐槽哏，笑點在「登月容易停車難」——適合傳給住在巷子超窄或永遠找不到車位那區的朋友
variations: 短版：「人類登得上月球，卻停不進我家巷口——這就是科技的極限。」
問：月球日最偉大的發現是什麼？答：原來最遠的不是月球，是週末想約你出門的那段路。

en.content:
"July 20th is Moon Day. Know why humans landed on the moon?"

"To explore the universe?"

"No — to prove that any distance is reachable if you throw enough money at it."

"Then why haven't you come over to my place yet?"

"...Your neighborhood is harder to park in than the moon."
en.editorNote: A Moon Day bit (July 20, marking the 1969 Apollo landing) — we can reach the moon but not a parking spot. Send it to the friend who lives on the block where a space has never once been free
variations: Short version: "We landed on the moon but still can't park near my house. That's the real final frontier."
What was Moon Day's greatest discovery? That the longest distance in the universe is the trip to actually get you out of the house on a weekend.

items.sourceNote: International Day of Friendship (July 30); friend-ship pun rebuilt as original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「友誼日快樂！我覺得我們的友情像一艘船。」

「這麼堅固？」

「不是，是因為——常常說要出航，結果每次都停在原地。」

「你是說我們約十次有九次都取消？」

「對，但至少我們約得很有誠意。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 友誼日（七月三十日，聯合國國際友誼日）自嘲哏，笑點在「友情堅固到只剩下約好卻不出門」——適合傳給那個每次都說「下次一定」的多年好友，彼此心知肚明
variations: 短版：「我們的友情像存款——很久沒動，但一直都在。」
問：真正的好朋友多久見一次？答：不重要，反正每次見面都像沒斷過。

en.content:
"Happy Friendship Day! Our friendship is like a ship."

"That solid, huh?"

"No — we keep saying we'll set sail and then never leave the dock."

"You mean we cancel nine out of ten plans?"

"Right, but we cancel with real sincerity."
en.editorNote: An International Day of Friendship bit (July 30, the UN observance) — the sturdiest friend-ship is the one permanently parked at the harbor. Send it to the old friend whose every 'next time for sure' you both know is a lie
variations: Short version: "Our friendship is like a savings account — untouched for ages, but always there."
How often do real friends see each other? Doesn't matter — every time feels like no time passed at all.

items.sourceNote: National Avocado Day (July 31); original avocado-price joke written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「酪梨日快樂，你知道酪梨為什麼被叫做超級食物嗎？」

「因為營養很高？」

「不是，是因為——它的價錢也是超級高。」

「所以吃一顆等於……」

「等於你今天的伙食費，恭喜你，很健康地破產了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 酪梨日（七月三十一日，National Avocado Day）錢包吐槽哏，笑點在「健康食物最傷的是荷包」——適合傳給熱愛酪梨吐司又常喊窮的朋友，或每次結帳都倒抽一口氣的自己
variations: 短版：「酪梨是唯一一種，還沒吃就先讓你心痛的水果。」
問：怎麼判斷酪梨熟了沒？答：等你決定要買的那一刻，它剛好熟過頭、又剛好漲價。

en.content:
"Happy Avocado Day! Know why avocados are called a superfood?"

"Because they're super nutritious?"

"No — because the price is super high too."

"So eating one is basically..."

"Basically your whole food budget for the day. Congrats, you went broke very healthily."
en.editorNote: A National Avocado Day bit (July 31) — the healthiest thing about avocados is what they do to your wallet. Send it to the avo-toast devotee who's always broke, or to yourself at the checkout
variations: Short version: "Avocados: the only fruit that breaks your heart before you even eat it."
How do you know when an avocado is ripe? The exact second you decide to buy it, it's overripe AND the price just went up.

items.sourceNote: International Cat Day (Aug 8); cat-indifference joke rewritten as original dialogue

zh-tw.content:
「國際貓咪日快樂，你家貓最愛你哪一點？」

「牠說牠最欣賞我的獨立自主。」

「貓會說話？」

「不會，牠是用一整天不理我來表達的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 國際貓咪日（八月八日，International Cat Day）溫馨吐槽哏，笑點在「貓的愛就是把你當空氣」——適合傳給貓奴朋友，尤其是那種被貓已讀不回還很開心的類型
variations: 短版：「我家貓把我當家人——那種可有可無、但負責開罐頭的家人。」
問：貓咪日怎麼證明牠愛你？答：牠願意在你身上睡覺，前提是你當天沒有要移動的打算。

en.content:
"Happy International Cat Day! What does your cat love most about you?"

"She says she really admires my independence."

"Your cat talks?"

"No, she expresses it by ignoring me all day."
en.editorNote: An International Cat Day bit (Aug 8) — a cat's deepest love language is treating you like furniture. Send it to the cat person who's genuinely thrilled to be left on read by a small animal
variations: Short version: "My cat treats me like family — the optional kind that happens to open the cans."
How does a cat prove its love on Cat Day? By sleeping on you — provided you have absolutely no plans to move that day.

items.sourceNote: Left Hander's Day (Aug 13); original left-handed-world joke written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「左撇子日快樂！你知道左撇子最大的天賦是什麼嗎？」

「頭腦特別靈活？」

「不是，是每天都在跟一個為右手設計的世界搏鬥。」

「例如？」

「例如剪刀、開罐器、還有那個永遠在錯邊的收銀機刷卡機。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 左撇子日（八月十三日，Left Hander's Day）共鳴吐槽哏，笑點在「左撇子的超能力其實是忍耐」——適合傳給身邊的左撇子朋友，他們會邊笑邊點頭邊翻出被剪刀弄痛的手
variations: 短版：「左撇子不是特別，只是每天都在破解一個右手設計的關卡。」
問：怎麼快速認出左撇子？答：看誰用那把難用到爆的剪刀，還能剪出直線。

en.content:
"Happy Left Hander's Day! Know what a lefty's greatest talent is?"

"A really flexible brain?"

"No — battling a world designed entirely for right hands, every single day."

"Like what?"

"Like scissors, can openers, and that card reader that's always on the wrong side."
en.editorNote: A Left Hander's Day bit (Aug 13) — a lefty's real superpower is patience. Send it to your left-handed friends, who'll nod and laugh while flexing the hand those righty scissors just pinched
variations: Short version: "Lefties aren't special — we just beat a right-handed obstacle course every day."
How do you spot a lefty fast? Find the one cutting a straight line with those impossible right-handed scissors.

items.sourceNote: Taiwan Father's Day (Aug 8, 八八節); original 爸氣 wordplay dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「爸，父親節快樂，你想要什麼禮物？」

「爸爸什麼都不缺。」

「真的？」

「真的，除了你上次借走還沒還的那三千塊。」

「……我懂了，我這就去買禮物。」

「不用買，直接還就好，這樣我們都省事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 父親節（八月八日）家庭吐槽哏，笑點在「爸爸嘴上說不缺，帳記得比誰都清楚」——適合父親節前傳到家庭群組，或發給那個每次都說「不用啦」的爸爸本人
variations: 短版：「爸爸說不用送禮，意思是——你人到就好，錢也到更好。」
問：爸爸最想要的父親節禮物是什麼？答：你把冷氣關掉、燈關掉，然後早點睡。

en.content:
"Happy Father's Day, Dad. What do you want as a gift?"

"Your father needs nothing."

"Really?"

"Really. Except the sixty bucks you borrowed and never paid back."

"...Got it. I'll go buy a present."

"Don't buy anything. Just pay me back. Saves us both the trip."
en.editorNote: A Father's Day bit (Aug 8 in Taiwan) — Dad claims he needs nothing while keeping flawless accounting records. Drop it in the family group chat, or send it straight to the dad who always says 'don't get me anything'
variations: Short version: "When Dad says no gifts, he means: just show up. Showing up with cash is even better."
What does Dad actually want for Father's Day? For you to turn off the AC, turn off the lights, and go to bed early.

items.sourceNote: Qixi Festival 2026 (Aug 19); original long-distance-relationship angle on the Cowherd and Weaver Girl legend

zh-tw.content:
「七夕快樂，牛郎織女一年只能見一次面，好慘。」

「慘嗎？我覺得他們感情才好。」

「為什麼？」

「因為一年見一次，永遠來不及吵架。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 七夕（二〇二六年八月十九日）感情吐槽哏，笑點在「距離不是問題，見太多面才是」——適合傳給遠距離情侶互相安慰，或傳給老是拌嘴的另一半，殺傷力剛剛好
variations: 短版：「牛郎織女能撐幾千年，靠的不是愛，是低頻率。」
問：七夕最浪漫的是什麼？答：一年只見一次，所以每次都是最好的樣子——連脾氣都來不及拿出來。

en.content:
"Happy Qixi. The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl only get to meet once a year. Brutal."

"Brutal? I'd say that's why they're still together."

"How so?"

"Once a year means they never have time to fight."
en.editorNote: A Qixi Festival bit (Aug 19, 2026) — the secret to a millennia-long romance is a really low meeting frequency. Send it to your long-distance friends as consolation, or to a partner you bicker with, gently
variations: Short version: "That legendary couple lasted thousands of years on love? No — on scheduling."
What's the most romantic part of Qixi? Meeting once a year means you always show your best self — no time to unpack the bad mood.

items.sourceNote: Ghost Month 2026 begins Aug 13; original taboo-list humor written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「鬼月開始了，媽說不能去海邊、不能半夜曬衣服、不能搬家、不能買車。」

「那可以做什麼？」

「上班。」

「所以鬼月唯一不忌諱的事情就是上班？」

「對，連好兄弟都覺得那太苦了，不會跟你搶。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 鬼月（二〇二六年八月十三日起）社畜黑色幽默，笑點在「什麼都禁忌，只有加班百無禁忌」——適合鬼月第一天傳到辦公室群組，苦中作樂效果最好
variations: 短版：「鬼月禁忌一百條，沒有一條寫著『不能上班』，很有事。」
問：鬼月最恐怖的是什麼？答：不是好兄弟，是月底的信用卡帳單。

en.content:
"Ghost Month started. Mom says no beach, no hanging laundry at night, no moving house, no buying a car."

"So what can you do?"

"Go to work."

"Work is the only thing that isn't taboo during Ghost Month?"

"Right. Even the spirits think it looks miserable — they won't compete for it."
en.editorNote: A Ghost Month bit (starting Aug 13, 2026) — the folklore bans everything except overtime. Post it in the office chat on day one of the month; gallows humor lands best with coworkers
variations: Short version: "A hundred Ghost Month taboos and not one of them says 'don't go to work.' Suspicious."
What's the scariest part of Ghost Month? Not the spirits — the credit card bill at the end of it.

items.sourceNote: Zhongyuan Festival 2026 (Aug 27); original 普渡供品 self-interest joke written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「中元普渡的供品是誰決定的？」

「我啊，我最有誠意。」

「所以買了什麼？」

「洋芋片、可樂、還有那個很貴的巧克力。」

「好兄弟愛吃這些？」

「不知道，但我很愛，拜完剛好都是我的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 中元節（二〇二六年八月二十七日）生活觀察哏，笑點在「普渡供品其實是買給自己的零食清單」——適合中元前後傳給同事，因為公司普渡的餅乾大家心裡都有一本帳
variations: 短版：「普渡供品的第一原則：拜的是好兄弟，吃的是自己。」
問：怎麼看出誰負責採購普渡供品？答：看誰買的東西整箱都是他最愛的口味。

en.content:
"Who picks the offerings for the Ghost Festival table?"

"Me. I'm the most sincere one here."

"So what did you buy?"

"Chips, cola, and that expensive chocolate."

"The spirits like all that?"

"No idea. But I do — and after the ceremony it's all mine."
en.editorNote: A Zhongyuan Festival bit (Aug 27, 2026) — the offering table is really just a snack wishlist with extra steps. Good for the office chat, since everyone quietly tracks who eats the ceremonial cookies
variations: Short version: "Rule one of festival offerings: honor the spirits, feed yourself."
How do you spot who did the offering shopping? Every single box is their personal favorite flavor.

items.sourceNote: End-of-summer-break countdown (late Aug); original parent-and-kid homework dialogue written fresh

zh-tw.content:
「暑假剩兩個禮拜，作業寫多少了？」

「進度非常穩定。」

「多少？」

「零。從第一天到今天，一頁都沒動，超級穩定。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 暑假倒數（八月下旬）親子哏，笑點在「把擺爛講成穩定發揮」——適合開學前傳到家長群組，或傳給那個報告永遠最後一天才動工的自己
variations: 短版：「暑假作業的正確寫法：前五十天累積靈感，最後兩天累積字數。」
問：暑假為什麼過那麼快？答：因為前面在放鬆，後面在贖罪，中間根本沒有中間。

en.content:
"Two weeks of summer break left. How's the homework going?"

"My progress has been extremely consistent."

"Meaning?"

"Zero. Same page since day one. Rock solid consistency."
en.editorNote: An end-of-summer-break bit (late August) — reframing total procrastination as admirable consistency. Send it to the parent group chat before school starts, or to yourself, the person who starts every report the night before
variations: Short version: "How to do summer homework: fifty days gathering inspiration, two days gathering word count."
Why does summer break go so fast? Because it's relaxing at the front and atonement at the back — there is no middle.
